PR #38 — docs/followup-34-chat-rendering → main (MERGED)
  Title: docs(operational-notes): CC chat rendering and password
         fidelity findings (Followup #34)
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/38
  Merged 2026-05-01T16:10:38Z, merge commit 503dd93.

  Closes Followup #34. New file
  docs/operational-notes/cc-chat-rendering.md (129 lines)
  documenting a three-point byte-diff diagnostic of CC's chat-
  rendering pipeline. Repro script lives in operator workspace
  (Path B, never committed); only the findings note enters the
  repo.

  Diagnostic methodology:
    - Point A: operator's local terminal direct stderr, byte-faithful
      ground truth (`node <script> 2> /tmp/A.bin`).
    - Point B: CC bash tool stderr-to-disk via redirect, then xxd to
      disk (`node <script> 2> /tmp/B.bin && xxd /tmp/B.bin > /tmp/B.hex`).
    - Point C: CC chat panel rendered output, copied by operator
      into local file, then xxd locally.
    - Pairwise diffs: A-vs-B isolates bash-tool capture; B-vs-C
      isolates chat-rendering plus clipboard; A-vs-C is end-to-end.

  Three findings:
    1. CR (0x0D) → LF (0x0A) in-place byte substitution at the
       chat-display-to-clipboard layer. Single-byte transform,
       identical file size, observed at byte offset 0x44b in
       TEST 25 carriageReturn. Reproducible. Severity: moderate.
       The only character-level transform observed across the
       entire 33-test battery.
    2. Line-level structural corruption in CC's bash-tool-result-
       to-chat-display layer. Observed once when CC reported
       `head -40 /tmp/B.hex` to chat: line 0x60's right-side
       ASCII gloss column showed line 0x10's gloss content while
       the hex column remained correct. The on-disk /tmp/B.hex
       was correct (the operator verified locally). One
       observation, NOT deliberately reproduced — characterized
       in the doc as "worth banking, not isolated bug." Severity:
       unknown. The most plausible candidate explanation for
       Session 40's bcrypt-verify failures, but suggestive rather
       than confirmed.
    3. Lazy-render selection truncation in VS Code's CC chat
       panel. Operator's first attempt to select-and-copy the
       full chat output truncated at TEST 18 of 33 because
       off-screen content wasn't in the DOM. Silent data loss,
       no error indicator. Severity: high. Workaround: scroll
       the chat panel to the bottom of the rendered content
       before select-all.

  Notable non-findings (Session 40's hypothesis NOT supported):
    - Base64url alphabet (TESTs 01-04, including hyphen runs and
      underscore runs) survived clean.
    - Smart-typography candidates (TESTs 05-10): no em-dash
      transforms, no curly quotes, no ellipsis collapse, no
      apostrophe smart-quoting.
    - Markdown attractor characters (TESTs 11-16): all preserved.
    - Backslash escapes, multi-space runs, HTML angle brackets,
      control bytes (tab, NUL, ESC) — all preserved.
    - Column-aligned table (TEST 31): full multi-row table came
      through byte-clean.
    - Adversarial cell-edge tests (TESTs 32, 33) targeting the
      "trailing hyphen at base64url cell edge becomes em-dash"
      hypothesis specifically: did NOT corrupt.

  Operational rule (run in operator's local terminal, never via
  CC's bash tool, for sensitive output) reinforced, not loosened.

  No reviewer subagents required (doc-only, no PII, no SQL, no
  routes; permission-by-omission framing). §2A self-merge.

PR #39 — docs/followup-11-smoke-testing → main (MERGED)
  Title: docs(operational-notes): smoke-testing runbook
         (Followup #11)
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/39
  Merged 2026-05-01T16:52:22Z, merge commit 62ab49e.

  Closes Followup #11. New file
  docs/operational-notes/smoke-testing.md (176 lines) consolidating
  operational rules from Sessions 38b/39/40 into a runbook for
  future operators.

  Six body sections:
    1. VITE_API_URL preflight before any local-DB work.
    2. Production data ops via Render psql (one-shot pattern with
       BEGIN/COMMIT + RAISE EXCEPTION preflights, RAISE NOTICE
       post-commit, /tmp/<file>.sql cleanup).
    3. Seed scripts and password handling (the local-terminal
       rule; cites cc-chat-rendering.md for the WHY, this doc is
       the HOW). Section explicitly says do NOT redirect stderr
       to a file (`2> passwords.txt` and equivalents); plaintexts
       must stay in volatile terminal scrollback. Includes Cmd-K
       scrollback clearing as post-delivery hygiene.
    4. Roster template-name verification (the
       `SELECT name FROM intervention_templates WHERE tenant_id
       IS NULL ORDER BY name` pre-flight before drafting any
       roster's INTERVENTIONS array).
    5. Password rotation when chat exposure happens (UPDATE
       skeleton in BEGIN/COMMIT envelope; hashes only in SQL;
       plaintexts hand-delivered out-of-band).
    6. VS Code chat panel lazy-render selection-truncation gotcha
       (cites cc-chat-rendering.md Finding 3).

  Plus a scannable pre-flight checklist (8 items mapping to the
  body sections) and a Related section. Master-index aspirational
  name SMOKE_TESTING.md resolves to this kebab-case path via a
  one-line note at the top of the doc.

  Establishes "note" vs "runbook" genre split inside
  docs/operational-notes/: cc-chat-rendering.md is a "note"
  (postmortem / findings); smoke-testing.md is a "runbook"
  (procedure / checklist). Title parallel ("Cowork-side
  operational note" vs "Cowork-side operational runbook") signals
  the intended division of labor. Convention going forward.

  No reviewer subagents required (doc-only, no PII, no SQL, no
  routes; permission-by-omission framing). §2A self-merge.

CATCHES (Cowork-side and Claude Code-side observations worth banking)

  1. CC discipline: surface-rather-than-silently-execute moves.
     Two examples this session:
     - Caught my own bash redirect-order bug pre-execution
       (`2>&1 > /tmp/B.bin` would have produced an empty
       B.bin because bash processes redirects left-to-right
       and `2>&1` snapshots fd1's previous destination; the
       correct order is `> /tmp/B.bin 2>&1`). Surfaced before
       running rather than silently producing an empty
       capture and a misleading shasum comparison.
     - Self-corrected the audit count from "3 occurrences of
       the prospect's school name" to "2 occurrences" with
       receipts after a re-grep. Surfaced the count error
       rather than quietly tightening it. The third candidate
       I had vaguely flagged as "indirectly via Followup #11
       reference" turned out not to exist on re-inspection.
     Six sessions running with this discipline. Worth banking
     as load-bearing pattern.

  2. §2A self-merge framing sharpened. CC's audit on PR #37
     produced the more precise framing: "permission-by-
     omission, §4B cleanliness as the load-bearing
     precondition." Used verbatim in PR bodies for #37, #38,
     #39. Going forward this is the canonical framing for
     chore/activities-log and docs-only self-merge PRs;
     "chore/activities-log self-merge convention" is the
     less-precise shorthand and should be deprecated.

  3. Followup #34 diagnostic narrowed the original hypothesis.
     Session 40 attributed the password mismatch to "smart-
     quote substitution, em-dash transformation of trailing -
     in base64url passwords, or column-alignment-induced
     copy-paste error." NONE of these reproduced in the
     33-test diagnostic. The actual finding was much narrower
     (single-byte CR→LF substitution + a one-time line-level
     structural observation). The operational rule didn't
     change, but the explanation banked in the codebase is
     now sharper than the original hypothesis. Worth banking
     as a discipline note: a hypothesis stated in one session
     is not load-bearing until a follow-up diagnostic
     confirms it; the rule it justifies can stand even if the
     hypothesis is wrong, but the codebase explanation should
     be tightened to match the evidence.

  4. Genre split in docs/operational-notes/: "note" =
     postmortem / findings (cc-chat-rendering.md), "runbook" =
     procedure / checklist (smoke-testing.md). Title parallel
     ("Cowork-side operational note" vs "Cowork-side
     operational runbook") signals the intended division of
     labor. Convention going forward; future operational
     content should declare its genre via the title prefix.

  5. Lazy-render selection truncation discovered as a separate
     finding during the diagnostic execution itself. The
     operator's first attempt to copy CC's chat output for
     Point C captured only TESTs 1-18 of 33 because VS Code's
     chat panel renders lazily (off-screen content not in DOM
     until scrolled). Documented in cc-chat-rendering.md
     Finding 3 and smoke-testing.md Section 6. The diagnostic
     workflow itself surfaced a hazard the diagnostic was not
     looking for — a useful instance of the "instrument with
     enough surface area that adjacent bugs surface" pattern.

  6. Session 36 activities-log gap accepted permanently
     during P6. PR #28 (Session 36 underlying work — Humble
     ISD sandbox provisioned at tenant_id=9) is on main; the
     entry that documented it (commit 34a4a90 on
     chore/activities-log-session-36) never landed.
     Backfilling would have required a §4B audit on a
     pre-discipline-era draft. Discarded; gap accepted.
     Future session entries that need to reference the
     underlying work cite tenant_id=9 directly without
     expecting a corresponding activities entry to exist.

PR #6 — fix/resource-1-4-calendar-underscores → main (MERGED)

  Cosmetic markdown escape: the Resource 1.4 calendar section was
  rendering paired underscores as italic spans because the upstream
  Word-doc-export pipeline left bare underscores between calendar
  date tokens. The fix backslash-escapes the underscores so the
  markdown renderer treats them as literal characters.

  Disposition: MERGED via merge commit fdcb707 with --delete-branch
  on both local and remote. Self-merge per the standard convention
  for cosmetic / docs PRs that don't touch §4B or §5 surfaces (the
  PR contained a single-file markdown content change, no logic, no
  data model).

  Followup #35 (introduced earlier as the underscore-italics bug
  observation) CLOSED with this merge.

      fourth production tenant, not the prospect-provisioning tenant

PR #41 — fix/seed-roster-validation-hardening → main (PARKED, human-reviewer-pending)

  Five-commit bundle of seed-script polish followups identified
  during PR #36's review (Session 40). All commits scoped to
  operator-only tooling — no request-path code, no auth
  middleware, no route handlers touched.

  Per-commit summary:

    - 2ec9245 (#29) fix(seed): reject \r/\n in validator string
      fields (defense-in-depth). Closes the security-reviewer M1
      from PR #36 — TENANT.name flowed raw into a "-- " SQL
      comment header, an embedded \n could terminate the comment
      and inject SQL ahead of BEGIN. New hasLineBreak() helper
      on four reviewer-named string fields.

    - 57d600c (#30) fix(seed): truncate validator error JSON
      output to 80 chars (privacy LOW-1). Bounds accidental PII
      spill in stderr if an operator paste-mistakes a real
      record into a roster scalar field. Try/catch handles
      circular refs + BigInt; String() fallback handles
      functions/symbols/undefined.

    - 1ae76dc (#32) feat(seed): allow ADMINS roster entries to
      override role (defaults to district_admin). Recognized at
      Session 40 prospect-provisioning time as a hardcoded-default
      artifact: hardcoded district_admin doesn't fit charter /
      international tenants. Backward-compat verified — existing
      reference roster (no role on ADMINS) emits byte-identical
      SQL pre/post change modulo bcrypt salt.

    - 6e49712 (#31) feat(seed): warn on unknown roster keys
      (validator hygiene). Adds collectRosterWarnings() walking
      Object.keys against ALLOWED_KEYS table; non-fatal stderr
      WARN per unknown key. Plus collateral cleanup: stripped
      per-row campus field from 18 synthetic student rows in the
      reference sandbox roster — campus was previously the
      "extra-fields-allowed" pattern, now warned. The campus
      column was never persisted by the schema, so emitted SQL
      from the reference roster remains byte-identical to
      baseline post-strip.

    - 46aa6a2 (#33) fix(seed): resolve student references by
      external_id via RETURNING (fixes name-collision data
      integrity bug). Production-incident provenance: Session 40
      prospect provisioning had two (first_name, last_name)
      collision pairs in a 270-row synthetic roster. The pre-#33
      WHERE-by-name pattern matched both colliding rows per
      INSERT and attached interventions/notes to the wrong tier
      of student — required surgical post-commit DELETE to clean
      up. #33 replaces name-lookup with a JS-side
      varByExternalId map and per-student PL/pgSQL v_student_N
      variables populated via INSERT ... RETURNING id INTO. The
      dropped WHERE s.tenant_id = v_tenant_id clauses become
      defense-in-depth-only because every v_student_N is
      provably set by an INSERT that explicitly used
      v_tenant_id. Tenant scoping preserved by provenance.

  Diff stat: 3 files changed, +174 / -50.

  REVIEWER SUBAGENTS (three; reports archived in session
  transcript, full verbatim text not duplicated here):

    - security-reviewer: APPROVED WITH NOTES. 0 CRITICAL / 1
      WARN / 5 INFO. The single WARN is a maintainability note
      about validator/buildSql coupling — if a future code path
      calls buildSql() without first running validateRoster(),
      the RAISE NOTICE line at the bottom of the emitted DO
      block uses single-quote escaping but no \r/\n gate (that
      gate currently lives only in the validator). Tracked as
      Followup #36 (out of scope for this PR, but worth closing
      next pass).

    - privacy-reviewer: APPROVED WITH NOTES. 0 CRITICAL / 0
      WARN / 4 INFO. All five commits classified as PII-neutral
      or PII-reducing relative to main. Notable INFO #3:
      reviewer counted total PII surface (renderings of
      student names in emitted SQL) and found a net DECREASE
      on #33 — names are now declared once in a DECLARE
      comment + once per INSERT VALUES, instead of being
      repeated in 1 INSERT + N WHERE clauses across the
      intervention/notes/parent-link blocks. Privacy-positive
      side effect of a data-integrity refactor.

    - tenant-isolation-auditor: APPROVED. 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH
      / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 5 INFO. Auditor independently
      traced the emitted SQL against /tmp/tia-emitted.sql and
      verified: 36 v_tenant_id references, all reads after the
      single tenant-creating write at line 77; 18/18 student
      INSERTs use VALUES (v_tenant_id, ...); 34/34 downstream
      student_id binding sites reference a v_student_N and
      only that; zero FROM students s WHERE patterns; zero
      integer-literal student_ids. No escape hatch found.
      Auditor went beyond the task brief and constructed an
      independent 3-way name-collision stress test (three rows
      with the same first+last but distinct external_ids) to
      stress-test the fix — interventions / notes routed to the
      correct v_student_N per external_id, confirming the
      pre-#33 WHERE-by-name pattern would have miswired all
      three.

  PR opened on origin: https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/41
  GitHub state at session end: mergeable=MERGEABLE,
  mergeStateStatus=CLEAN, statusChecks pass (Vercel preview,
  Vercel tiertrak deployment), no auto-applied labels, no
  CODEOWNERS-driven reviewer assignments.

  CRITICAL: PR #41 is NOT eligible for self-merge per §2A line
  79 ("Do not merge your own PR on any change that touches
  Section 4B (PII) or Section 5 (multi-tenancy). Request human
  review."). #33's RETURNING refactor modifies the exact
  tenant-scoping mechanism in the script's emitted SQL.
  Precedent: PR #36 (predecessor commit that introduced this
  same script) was approved by Owner's ed tech specialist as
  the non-Owner human review. PR #41 inherits the
  classification. GitHub considering the PR mergeable is
  necessary but not sufficient — the §2A line 79 requirement
  is human-enforced, not branch-protection-enforced. Banner
  at the top of the PR body makes this explicit.

PRODUCTION DB WRITES

  None this session. PR #41 is operator-tooling that emits SQL
  for paste into Render's psql console — no DB writes occurred
  during the session itself. PR #6 was a markdown content
  change. The cross-tenant probe was read-only navigation.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW

  PR #6: render-only markdown change; no DB queries, no
  tenant scoping changes. tenant-isolation-auditor not
  required.

  PR #41: APPROVED by tenant-isolation-auditor (5 INFO, all
  positive). Independently traced provenance argument, ran a
  3-way collision stress test, confirmed no escape hatch in
  the new INSERT...RETURNING control flow. The dropped
  WHERE s.tenant_id = v_tenant_id defense-in-depth clauses
  are eliminated on a pathway that no longer traverses any
  SELECT from students — the new pattern eliminates the
  SELECT-from-students entirely.

  Cross-tenant probe (in-browser): PASS. No cross-tenant data
  observed across five surfaces. tenant_id field in inspected
  API payload matched logged-in tenant exactly.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)

  PR #6: render-only markdown content change; no PII fields,
  no logging changes. privacy-reviewer not required.

  PR #41: APPROVED WITH NOTES by privacy-reviewer (0 WARN, 4
  INFO). All five commits PII-neutral or PII-reducing
  relative to main. The reference sandbox roster's per-row
  campus field stripped (#31 collateral cleanup) — campus
  was a previously-decorative attribute the schema never
  persisted, so removing it is data minimization in the
  right direction with no SQL behavior change. The new
  v_student_N variable-declaration comments in the emitted
  SQL re-render student names that were already being
  embedded as INSERT VALUES literals — no new PII surface,
  net volume decrease per privacy-reviewer's INFO #3
  reasoning.

  Cross-tenant probe: §4B-clean. The seeded user account
  used for the probe is a synthetic identity attached to a
  real production DB row — per Cowork-side Session 41 sub-
  rule, synthetic names attached to real production rows are
  §4B PII with no carve-out. This entry refers to the user
  generically ("a seeded user from a production tenant") and
  to the tenant by ordinal ("the fourth production tenant"),
  not by name or slug.

  This activities entry: per-entry §4B grep performed on the
  draft before staging (Session 41 PR #40 precedent). Receipt
  banked alongside this entry's commit message in the
  chore/activities-log-session-42 PR description. The §4B
  STOP rule is applied symmetrically: the entry does not name
  what was scrubbed even when describing the scrub.

CATCHES (Cowork-side and Claude Code-side observations worth banking)

  1. Surface-rather-than-silently-execute discipline now
     documented through ~15 instances across Session 41 and
     Session 42 combined. Worth treating as a load-bearing
     CC behavioral pattern, not a session-by-session
     judgment call. Examples in this session: surfacing the
     pre-existing settings.json drift before any commit;
     surfacing the reference-roster failure on Test 4 during the #31
     smoke run before showing the diff; surfacing the
     "could not run psql parse-only" verification limitation
     in the PR body honestly rather than bluffing; surfacing
     the synthetic-name-on-real-DB-row §4B reasoning during
     the cross-tenant probe write-up.

  2. Tenant-isolation-auditor stress-test discipline
     (constructing an independent 3-way name-collision test
     beyond what the agent was asked to verify) is the
     canonical example of audit-not-rubber-stamp behavior
     for subagent reviewers. The auditor went past the
     stated brief to verify the fix would handle a stress
     case the original collision-pair smoke (built from a
     2-pair scenario) didn't cover. Future reviewer-
     subagent invocations should expect this level of
     independent verification on substantive changes; if a
     reviewer just rubber-stamps the PR body claims, treat
     that as a smell worth flagging.

  3. Privacy-reviewer's PII-volume reasoning on #33 (INFO
     #3) is the canonical example of substantive PII review.
     The reviewer counted total PII renderings in the
     emitted SQL pre and post change, found that the new
     v_student_N DECLARE comments DO add new comment-
     header surfaces — but that the dropped WHERE-by-name
     clauses removed MORE renderings than the new comments
     introduced, producing a net DECREASE. A surface-level
     review would have flagged "new PII in comments, bad" and
     stopped; a substantive review measured the volume both
     ways and found the change privacy-positive. Bank as the
     standard-of-care for future privacy-reviewer reads.

  4. GitHub MERGEABLE / mergeStateStatus=CLEAN ≠ policy-
     approved. The §2A line 79 non-Owner-review requirement
     is human-enforced, not branch-protection-enforced. PR
     #41 reports MERGEABLE/CLEAN at session end and is
     explicitly not authorized for self-merge per the banner
     at the top of its body. Branch protection rules are not
     wired to enforce §2A directly. Worth banking for future
     PRs that touch §4B or §5 surfaces — the discipline must
     hold even when GitHub says "go."

  5. §4B per-entry standard continues to be load-bearing.
     Applied this session to PR #6's merge audit (cosmetic-
     change-on-shared-tenant-resources still gets a §4B
     per-entry check on the diff vs main, even though the
     content is markdown text), to PR #41's body audit (the
     PR body itself was §4B-clean as drafted), and to this
     session's activities entry (per-entry §4B grep
     performed on the draft before staging — receipt
     captured in the chore/activities-log-session-42 PR
     description). Pre-existing content on main is governed
     by its own at-the-time audit; this session does not
     retroactively re-audit prior session entries.

  6. Synthetic-name-on-real-DB-row §4B reasoning (Session
     41 sub-rule, applied here): a synthetic seed identity
     attached to a real production tenant's row is §4B PII
     with no carve-out. The cross-tenant probe entry refers
     to "a seeded user from a production tenant," not by
     synthetic name. Likewise, the reference sandbox
     roster's synthetic student names — even though the
     roster file itself is committed and the names are
     fictional — should not be enumerated in operational
     write-ups when paired with descriptions of real DB
     behavior. The "stripped per-row campus field from 18
     synthetic student rows in reference sandbox roster"
     framing in the #31 description follows this rule:
     table + row count, not a list of names.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  --- Net new from Session 42 ---

  - #36 NEW (security-reviewer WARN on PR #41) — Move
    hasLineBreak() into a shared helper and assert it from
    BOTH validateRoster() AND buildSql() comment-emitter +
    RAISE NOTICE sites as defense in depth. Currently the
    \r/\n gate lives only in the validator; if a future
    code path calls buildSql() without first running
    validateRoster(), the RAISE NOTICE line's single-quote
    escape is not sufficient — embedded \n would terminate
    the statement. Maintainability fix, not a defect at
    head; the security-reviewer explicitly classified it as
    non-blocking.

  - #37 NEW (candidate, surfaced during cross-tenant probe)
    — The active-form-set endpoint introduced by PR #35 is
    being polled with 404 responses on tenants that have no
    form_set registered. Followup #26 (the 504 tab hide)
    correctly hides the tab in those tenants; the leftover
    polling is cosmetic / perf, not security. Either gate
    the polling on the registered-form-set state or
    suppress the 404 noise in the FE network log. Promote
    to NEW followup if the noise is observed by other
    operators; otherwise hold as candidate.

    — CC bash-tool collapsed-output ("+N lines (ctrl+o to
    expand)") in the IDE-extension chat panel can hide

    3 from Followup #34's diagnostic — same family of

    NEW followup if the friction recurs in a future session;
    otherwise hold as candidate. Workaround already
    available (manual expand).

TASK SUMMARY
  Single workstream: a security retrofit landed as the required
  precursor to the upcoming feat/note-only-intervention-flag PR.
  The original audit identified 4 vulnerable routes in
  routes/interventions.js (POST /assign + 3 PATCH endpoints) plus
  1 in routes/mtssMeetings.js. During implementation of the
  interventions.js commit, a full file read surfaced 3 additional
  same-pattern gaps (PUT /:interventionId/archive,
  PUT /:interventionId/unarchive, DELETE /:interventionId). Owner
  approved scope-expansion to all 7 same-file routes; mtssMeetings.js
  remained at 1 route per stated scope discipline. Net effect: 8
  routes locked down (auth + tenant scoping) across two backend
  files plus one stale FE comment refreshed.

PR #43 — sec/intervention-routes-tenant-scope → main (MERGED d02d721)

  Four-commit bundle. All commits scoped to backend auth + tenant
  scoping plus a single 6-line FE comment refresh. No request-path
  behavior changes for legitimate FE callers — URLs unchanged via
  Express positional matching; body fields like assigned_by /
  archived_by silently ignored where server-derived values are now
  used (FE still sends them; server now uses req.user.id instead).

  Per-commit summary:

    - ed38bf6 Add requireWriteAccessByInterventionId middleware
      export (no-op until wired up). 21-line addition to
      middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess.js. Mirrors the
      existing requireWriteAccessByBody helper but sources the
      intervention id from req.params.interventionId.

    - ffc8181 Lock down all 7 mutation routes in
      routes/interventions.js with auth + tenant scoping. POST
      /assign: requireAuth; role check moved from spoofable
      x-user-role header to req.user.role; parent role explicitly
      rejected; assigned_by server-derived from req.user.id;
      inline tenant verification of student_id. PATCH/PUT/DELETE:
      requireAuth + requireWriteAccessByInterventionId +
      defense-in-depth tenant-bound SQL clauses
      (student_id IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE tenant_id) for
      UPDATEs; explicit tenant-bound subquery for the DELETE
      cascade across weekly_progress, intervention_assignments,
      mtss_meeting_interventions). URL params renamed
      :id -> :interventionId in 6 routes to match middleware
      convention. FE-facing URLs unchanged.

    - c6bb692 Lock down GET
      /student/:studentId/interventions-summary in
      routes/mtssMeetings.js. Adds requireAuth +
      requireStudentReadAccess + JOIN-based tenant binding
      mirroring the computeWeeklyProgressSnapshot precedent in
      the same file.

    - 224a08f Update MTSSMeetingFormModal comment to reflect
      data-shape rationale (auth-posture distinction obsolete
      after auth-gate retrofit). 1-file FE comment refresh
      (4 -> 6 lines): kept the durable per-row vs aggregates
      distinction, dropped the now-stale auth-gated vs
      unauthenticated framing.

  Reviewer subagents (security-reviewer + tenant-isolation-auditor)
  both approved per Owner. Non-Owner human review per §2A line 79
  obtained. Merged 2026-05-02T14:05Z as d02d721 with --delete-branch
  (local + remote).

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  --- Net new from Session 43 ---

  - #39 NEW (high-severity) — routes/mtssMeetings.js: GET
    /student/:studentId, GET /:id, GET
    /student/:studentId/count all lack requireAuth and
    tenant scoping. The first two return student PII
    (first_name, last_name, grade) cross-tenant; the third
    returns counts only (lower severity but same gap).
    Same retrofit pattern as PR #43 — different file scope.
    Held narrow per Owner scope discipline; track for
    separate PR.

  - #40 NEW (high-severity, next-up per Owner explicit
    flagging) — routes/mtssMeetings.js: DELETE /:id is a
    destructive cross-tenant operation with no auth and no
    tenant scope. Anyone with a meeting id can delete it,
    cascading to mtss_meeting_interventions. Higher
    severity than the read leaks in #39. Should be the
    next sec/ PR.

  - #41 NEW (low-severity, hardening) — Multiple handlers
    in routes/interventions.js still return 500 responses
    with res.status(500).json({ error: error.message }).
    Can leak DB error text (constraint violation messages,
    column names, etc.). Pre-existing pattern, untouched
    in PR #43. Replace with generic error messages +
    console.error for the actual stack.

  - #42 NEW (medium-severity, hardening) — DELETE
    /student-interventions/:interventionId in
    routes/interventions.js performs 4 cascading DELETEs
    without a transaction wrapper. Mid-cascade failure
    leaves orphaned rows in 1-3 of the dependent tables.
    Pre-existing — untouched in PR #43. Wrap in
    BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK using pool.connect() per the
    routes/mtssMeetings.js POST / precedent.

  - #43 NEW (high-severity) — routes/interventions.js
    templates CRUD: GET /templates/tenant/:tenantId
    (lines 18-44), POST /templates (lines 47-60), DELETE
    /templates/:id (lines 63-82) lack requireAuth and
    tenant scoping. Surfaced by security-reviewer
    (MEDIUM) and tenant-isolation-auditor (LOW) during
    PR #43 review; both classified as
    out-of-scope-but-track. Same pattern as the 7
    mutation routes locked down in PR #43, different
    table (intervention_templates). Recommend bundling
    as next sec/ PR. (Followup-number / PR-number
    collision is coincidental — namespaces are
    independent.)

  - #44 NEW (medium-severity, verification) — Confirm
    global rate limiter coverage on /api/interventions.
    Security-reviewer flagged POST /assign accepts
    external input without explicit per-route rate
    limiting; CLAUDE.md §5 requires rate limiting on
    endpoints accepting external input. Verify
    app.js/server.js applies a global limiter; if yes,
    mark verified; if no, add one.

TASK SUMMARY
  Single workstream: the Phase-1-planned feat/ work, queued during
  Session 43's sec/ pivot, landed as PR #45. Adds a
  no_progress_monitoring_required boolean to BOTH
  student_interventions and mtss_meeting_interventions
  (Option α — snapshot to meeting at save time, mirroring
  Migration 020's weekly_progress_snapshot "frozen at first save"
  contract). Lets staff document interventions that don't require
  ongoing weekly progress logs (e.g., preferential seating)
  without surfacing them in missing-logs notifications or
  triggering the amber "no logs recorded" warning in MTSS meeting
  views. Rides directly on the auth/tenant foundation laid by
  PR #43 — the new PATCH /:interventionId/monitoring-flag endpoint
  uses requireAuth + requireWriteAccessByInterventionId without
  any middleware changes.

PR #45 — feat/note-only-intervention-flag → main (MERGED f654813)

  Four-commit bundle: the originally-planned 3 commits (migration,
  backend, frontend) plus a reviewer-prompted PRIVACY_REVIEW.md
  addendum (privacy-reviewer's WARN asked for the new column to
  be inventoried before merge).

  Per-commit summary:

    - 3f17dd6 Migration 023: ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
      no_progress_monitoring_required BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT
      false on BOTH student_interventions and
      mtss_meeting_interventions. Idempotent. schema.sql updated
      for student_interventions only — mtss_meeting_interventions
      does not live in schema.sql (intentional asymmetry, matches
      Migration 020's weekly_progress_snapshot precedent).

    - 795aced Backend wiring across 3 route files.
      routes/interventions.js: POST /assign extends to accept
      the new field with strict-coerce (=== true; anything else
      becomes false); new PATCH /:interventionId/monitoring-flag
      mirrors /progress, /status, /goal — requireAuth +
      requireWriteAccessByInterventionId, typeof === 'boolean'
      validation, defense-in-depth tenant guard.
      routes/weeklyProgress.js: missing-logs filter gains
      AND si.no_progress_monitoring_required IS NOT TRUE
      (null-safe by choice — the migration's NOT NULL DEFAULT
      false makes nulls structurally impossible, but free
      defense against direct-SQL drift).
      routes/mtssMeetings.js: interventions-summary SELECT
      extended; new readNoProgressMonitoringFlag helper mirrors
      computeWeeklyProgressSnapshot's tenant-bind
      defense-in-depth. Meeting POST always reads the flag fresh
      from student_interventions inside the transaction; meeting
      PUT preserves prior snapshot value for already-present
      interventions and reads fresh only for newly-added ones
      (preserve-on-edit symmetry with weekly_progress_snapshot).
      New PATCH endpoint deliberately uses generic 500 messages —
      does NOT inherit Followup #41's legacy error.message
      leakage pattern.

    - f9469c5 Frontend wiring. Checkbox in Add Intervention

DISPOSITION: merged:20ac1f8 (PR #47 — feat/multi-screener-foundation)

TASK SUMMARY
  PR1 of the multi-screener support effort landed: schema foundation + route
  factor-out + minimal frontend backward-compat plumbing. Adds an
  assessment_type column to screener_results, per-tenant backfills it to
  'STAR' for existing rows, and reshapes the uniqueness contract from
  (student_id, school_year) to a 6-column key
  (tenant_id, student_id, assessment_type, subject, screening_period,
  school_year) so future screener types (AIMSweb, iReady, etc.) can coexist
  with STAR rows for the same student/year — and so the same student can
  carry distinct rows per subject and per screening period within an
  assessment type. Screener routes lifted out of server.js into a
  dedicated routes/screener.js with auth + tenant scoping retrofit.
  Frontend upload modal now sends assessmentType=STAR explicitly to keep
  PR1 backward-compatible while PR2's UI work is staged.

PR #47 — feat/multi-screener-foundation → main (MERGED 20ac1f8)

  Four-commit bundle:

    - 4c06859 Migration 024: multi-screener support foundation. Six steps:
      (1) ADD COLUMN assessment_type VARCHAR with default 'STAR';
      (2-4) per-tenant backfill loop (idempotent, tenant-scoped UPDATEs);
      (5) NOT NULL tightening once backfill verified;
      (6) DROP old UNIQUE (student_id, school_year), ADD new UNIQUE
      (tenant_id, student_id, assessment_type, subject, screening_period,
      school_year) with NULLS NOT DISTINCT semantics so future NULL
      student_id fingerprints can't collide.

    - cb6241f Factor screener routes out of server.js into
      routes/screener.js. Auth + tenant-scoping retrofit applied to every
      lifted route. No behavior change beyond the structural move.

    - d622761 ScreenerUploadModal: send assessmentType=STAR explicitly so
      existing upload flow continues to land in the new column without
      the PR2 picker UI. Belt-and-suspenders pairing with the migration's
      backfill default.

    - 9a3d7b0 Privacy-reviewer WARNs addressed: catalog screener_results
      in PRIVACY_REVIEW.md field inventory; tighten sr.* projection in
      GET /student/:studentId to an explicit column list.

  Reviewer verdicts (pre-merge):
    - privacy-reviewer: APPROVED WITH NOTES (2 WARNs, both resolved by
      9a3d7b0).
    - tenant-isolation-auditor: APPROVED.
    - security-reviewer: APPROVED.

MIGRATIONS RUN
  024_multi_screener_foundation.sql — applied to prod with a partial-
    failure recovery path (see "Production migration outcome" below).

PRODUCTION MIGRATION OUTCOME (must-read for next session)
  Migration 024 was applied to prod and PARTIALLY succeeded:
    - Steps 1–5 ran clean across all tenants. assessment_type column
      present, backfill complete, NOT NULL constraint in place.
    - Step 6 (DROP old UNIQUE + ADD new 6-column UNIQUE with NULLS NOT
      DISTINCT) ERRORED on 1 prod tenant due to pre-existing NULL-
      student_id duplicate rows in screener_results that violated the
      new uniqueness contract under NULLS NOT DISTINCT semantics.
    - Manual recovery: added the new UNIQUE
      (tenant_id, student_id, assessment_type, subject, screening_period,
      school_year) constraint WITHOUT NULLS NOT DISTINCT semantics so
      the migration could complete. Prod is now in a non-canonical
      uniqueness state vs. the migration file's intent. Migration 025
      (Followup #49) will dedup the offending NULL-student_id rows and
      re-add the constraint with NULLS NOT DISTINCT.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  - routes/screener.js retrofit: every lifted handler now resolves
    tenant from the auth token, never from request body / query.
    Spot-checked all SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE paths for a
    school_id-scoped WHERE clause.
  - Migration 024 backfill loop: per-tenant UPDATE structure preserved;
    no cross-tenant write surface introduced.
  - GET /student/:studentId tightening (commit 9a3d7b0): sr.* replaced
    with an explicit column projection — eliminates the risk that a
    future ALTER TABLE adds a column unintentionally exposed via this
    route.
  - New 6-column UNIQUE includes tenant_id as the leading component,
    making any cross-tenant collision structurally impossible at the
    constraint level (defense-in-depth alongside route-level scoping).

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: screener_results rows reference student_id (FK
    to students). No new PII columns added — assessment_type is a
    short enum-style code (e.g., 'STAR', 'AIMSweb'), not PII.
  Logging audit: verified no PII in logs. Migration 024 logs row
    counts only. Smoke-test cleanup logged the affected row id list
    (synthetic test rows), not student data.
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified. Backfill is per-tenant; new
    routes/screener.js handlers carry tenant scoping on every query;
    new 6-column UNIQUE leads with tenant_id.
  Catalog update: screener_results table inventoried in
    docs/ai-context/PRIVACY_REVIEW.md per privacy-reviewer WARN.

VERIFICATION
  - npm test — passed (PR #47 CI green pre-merge).
  - npm run lint — clean.
  - Migration 024 applied to local dev DB — success.
  - Migration 024 applied to prod — partial (Steps 1–5 success, Step 6
    failed on 1 tenant; manual recovery documented above).

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  --- New / re-scoped this session ---

  - Followup #48 (RE-SCOPED, was: pre-existing scope) — schema.sql
    drift on screener_results. Audit identified five categories of
    drift between schema.sql and the live prod table:
      * varchar vs TEXT divergence on multiple columns
      * nullability flips (columns NULLable in schema.sql that are
        NOT NULL in prod, and vice versa)
      * FK ON DELETE rules differ
      * missing indexes in schema.sql relative to prod
      * UNIQUE constraint semantics drift (now compounded by the
        Migration 024 partial-apply outcome — schema.sql shows
        NULLS NOT DISTINCT, prod currently does not).
    Owner to decide whether #48 fixes schema.sql to match prod, or
    whether Migration 025 (#49) gets prod back into canonical shape
    first and #48 then re-asserts schema.sql against the canonical
    state.

  - Followup #49 (NEW) — Migration 025: dedup pre-existing NULL-
    student_id duplicate rows in screener_results, then re-add the
    UNIQUE (tenant_id, student_id, assessment_type, subject,
    screening_period, school_year) constraint with NULLS NOT
    DISTINCT semantics so prod matches Migration 024's original
    intent. Sequence matters: dedup must run before the constraint
    flip. Affected scope: 1 prod tenant identified during Step 6
    failure; full inventory of NULL-student_id rows across tenants
    pending in #50.

  - Followup #50 (NEW) — Two-part data-shape investigation surfaced
    by the Migration 024 backfill loop:
      (a) tenant_id=3 catch-all backfill anomaly. Tenant_id=3 had
          screener_results rows that landed in a catch-all bucket
          during the per-tenant backfill — needs investigation to
          determine whether this represents legacy data, a bug in
          a prior import path, or expected behavior under the
          backfill loop's tenant resolution logic.
      (b) tenant_id=10 zero-screener-rows surprise. Tenant_id=10
          backfilled zero rows. Confirm whether this tenant simply
          has no screener data yet, or whether their rows are
          mis-attributed to a different tenant_id (which would be
          a §5 cross-tenant-attribution finding).
    Both items are investigation-only at this stage; remediation
    scope TBD pending findings.

TASK SUMMARY
  Closes Followup #53 from the Session 46 master index — PR-1 of the
  multi-PR #54 router-sweep campaign. Locks down routes/csvImport.js
  with authentication, parent-role exclusion, and tenant-from-JWT
  enforcement. Before this session, any unauthenticated caller could
  POST a CSV of student records into any tenant via URL parameter.

  Strict two-PR merge order, kept as a bright line for the #54 sweep:
  PR #49 (fix/csvimport-fe-credentials, FE-only, 1 line) merged first
  to add `credentials: 'include'` to the upload fetch so the auth
  cookie travels with the request once the BE gates in. PR #50
  (sec/csvimport-require-auth, BE-only, 3 commits) merged second to
  add requireAuth + parent-role gate + tenant-from-JWT parity check
  on all three routes.

  Mid-session prod accident: while preparing local fixtures for the
  PR #50 smoke test, a parent-creation form submission was made
  against the prod URL instead of localhost. One row created in prod
  tenant 3 (Lincoln demo), cleaned up atomically via psql with
  row-count safety guard. No FK fallout, no external PII leak.
  Render DB credential rotated as belt-and-suspenders due to partial
  password fragment surfacing in error output.

  Followed by a mid-session prod OUTAGE: deletion of the rotated-out
  `tiertrak` credential — misjudged as unused based on a "0 open
  connections" snapshot — took down tiertrak-api because that
  service's DATABASE_URL env var was a hardcoded string pointing at
  the deleted credential, not a Render `fromDatabase` reference.
  Resolved by Owner pasting the new Internal Database URL into the
  env var; Render auto-redeployed; SSO verified working post-fix.
  No data loss, no writes attempted during the outage window.
  Tracked as Followup #62 to convert the hardcoded DATABASE_URL on
  tiertrak-api to a `fromDatabase` reference so future credential
  rotations auto-propagate.

PR #49 — fix/csvimport-fe-credentials → main (MERGED 78ca257)

  One-commit FE prep for PR #50:

    - 6f3085c fix(csv-import): include credentials on FE upload fetch.
      Adds `credentials: 'include'` to the POST /api/csv/students/:tenantId
      fetch in handleCsvUpload. Matches the pattern already used by
      every other authenticated fetch in App.jsx (lines 213, 471,
      505, 521, 539).

  Reviewer verdicts: not required. Not a §2A trigger (no PII change,
  no tenant logic change, no auth/JWT change — adds a flag to a
  fetch). Owner self-merge.

PR #50 — sec/csvimport-require-auth → main (MERGED e14a70d)

  Three-commit bundle, each one logical change, monotonically
  tighter than the previous, never loosens:

    - 7c27638 sec(csv-import): require auth on all 3 routes. Imports
      requireAuth from middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess (the
      shared module already used by parent504, student504,
      mtssMeetings, weeklyProgress, interventions, screener) and
      applies it to GET /template, GET /template/download, and
      POST /students/:tenantId. On the POST, requireAuth is placed
      BEFORE upload.single('file') so anonymous bytes are never
      buffered. Same ordering pattern as routes/studentDocuments.js:115.

    - 4d4d6c7 sec(csv-import): block parent role on POST. Adds named
      guard blockParentRole at module level returning generic 403
      when req.user.role === 'parent'. Inserted on the POST chain
      BETWEEN requireAuth and upload.single — parent callers never
      reach multer.

    - 073828c sec(csv-import): enforce tenant-from-JWT on POST.
      Adds named guard requireMatchingTenant on the POST chain that
      403s when parseInt(req.params.tenantId, 10) is NaN OR does
      not equal req.user.tenant_id. Handler body now binds tenant_id
      from req.user.tenant_id, not from the URL. Existing INSERT at
      line 174 unchanged — bind variable name preserved.

      Surfaced and approved deviation from approved plan: parity
      check landed as middleware running BEFORE upload.single
      instead of inside the handler body, to prevent orphaned temp
      files in uploads/ on cross-tenant probes (§4B violation
      otherwise). Same defense-in-depth posture as blockParentRole.

  Reviewer verdicts (pre-merge):
    - privacy-reviewer: APPROVED (0 CRITICAL, 0 WARN, 3 INFO).
    - security-reviewer: APPROVED WITH NOTES (0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN —
      both pre-existing posture: rate limiting absent + multer
      relative-path fragility. Not regressions; tracked as
      followups for next session).
    - tenant-isolation-auditor: APPROVED (0 CRITICAL, 0 WARN,
      3 INFO).
    - Non-Owner human reviewed and approved per §2A (PR touches
      PII, tenant isolation, and auth — cannot self-merge).

PROD DATA OP (mid-session)
  Render DB, prod tenant 3 (Lincoln demo). One row deleted from
  users table (table+count only per §4B; no email, no name, no
  user_id recorded here):

    Phase A (read-only): BEGIN; SELECT (with parent_student_links
      link_count enrichment via subquery); ROLLBACK;
    Phase B (atomic delete): \set ON_ERROR_STOP on; BEGIN; DO block
      with DELETE + GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT + RAISE EXCEPTION if
      count != 1; COMMIT; post-COMMIT verification SELECT.

  The DO-block pattern guaranteed prod would be unchanged unless
  exactly 1 row was deleted AND no FK constraint blocked it. Phase
  A confirmed exactly one matching row with link_count=0 (no
  parent_student_links to cascade). Phase B succeeded clean —
  "Deleted 1 row(s)" notice, COMMIT, post-COMMIT SELECT returned
  0 rows.

  Owner ran the SQL files against prod psql; Claude Code wrote the
  files (/tmp/cleanup_phaseA.sql, /tmp/cleanup_phaseB.sql) but
  never touched the prod URL. Render DB credential rotated as
  belt-and-suspenders due to partial password fragment surfacing
  in error output.

PROD OUTAGE (mid-session, must-read for next session)
  - Mid-session prod outage: tiertrak-api web service began failing
    all requests after we deleted an old `tiertrak` credential we
    misjudged as unused (it was the credential the deployed app's
    hardcoded DATABASE_URL was using). Outage bounded by deletion
    timestamp through Owner's manual env-var update on tiertrak-api.
    Resolved by pasting the new Internal Database URL into the
    DATABASE_URL env var and letting Render auto-redeploy. SSO
    verified working post-fix. No data loss, no writes attempted
    during outage window.
  - Sunday morning Pacific — likely zero or near-zero active users
    affected. Service was completely unavailable during the window
    (every request returned the role-cannot-login error from Render's
    Postgres layer).
  - Root cause: tiertrak-api had its DATABASE_URL hardcoded as a
    string env var pointing at the deleted credential. Render's
    auto-redeploy didn't fire because the env var wasn't a
    `fromDatabase` reference. Followup #62 converts this to the
    canonical `fromDatabase` form so future rotations auto-propagate.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  - PR #50 closes the pre-existing cross-tenant write CRITICAL on
    POST /api/csv/students/:tenantId. Every guard layer enforces
    server-derived tenant identity:
      * requireAuth populates req.user.tenant_id from a re-queried
        users row on every request (not from the JWT claim alone)
        — so demoted/disabled users cannot continue to act on a
        still-valid token.
      * requireMatchingTenant enforces parity between URL :tenantId
        and req.user.tenant_id; mismatch returns generic 403 before
        multer runs.
      * Handler-body tenant bind sources from req.user.tenant_id,
        NOT req.params. Defense-in-depth — even if the middleware
        were dropped in a future refactor, the INSERT would still
        land in the caller's own tenant.
  - 403 bodies are byte-identical between blockParentRole and
    requireMatchingTenant (verified via ETag match in smoke output).
    No probe info distinguishing wrong-role from wrong-tenant.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: students table accepts CSV-derived rows
    (first_name, last_name, grade) bound to a server-derived
    tenant_id on every INSERT. No new PII fields persisted by this
    PR.
  Logging audit: 0 new console.* statements added by PR #50. The
    pre-existing console.error for unlink failures logs only an fs
    error object. No PII surfaced.
  Error response audit: All 401/403 responses use generic strings
    ("Not authenticated" / "Not authorized"). No PII, no probe info.
  Cross-tenant risk: Pre-existing CRITICAL closed (anonymous CSV
    write into any tenant). New posture is server-derived tenant
    binding throughout.
  Temp-file lifecycle: requireAuth + blockParentRole +
    requireMatchingTenant all run BEFORE upload.single, so rejected
    requests never have their CSV bytes written to uploads/.
    Empirically verified — uploads/ contained only .gitkeep after
    smoke (b)/(c)/(d) probes with file payloads attached.
  Prod data op privacy: synthetic test fixture only (.test TLD
    email, undeliverable). No real student or staff PII involved.
    Render DB credential rotation noted under PROD DATA OP.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  --- New / re-scoped this session ---

  - Followup #57 (NEW) — Schema drift: local users.password_hash
    is NOT NULL but prod allows NULL (parent accounts pre-password-
    setup). A migration should have relaxed this constraint;
    investigate which migration is missing from the local apply
    path. Local DB was patched ad-hoc this session via ALTER TABLE
    to unblock smoke testing. Followup PR should add a proper
    migration file so future fresh local DBs are consistent with
    prod.

  - Followup #58 (NEW) — frontend/.env defaults to prod API URL
    even in dev mode. Caused a multi-step accident this session
    where the FE pointed at prod while running on localhost:5173.
    Add frontend/.env.development (or document the pattern of
    frontend/.env.local override) so dev mode is local-by-default.
    Currently mitigated locally via .env.local override (not
    committed).

  - Followup #59 (NEW) — Additional schema drift: routes/students.js
    queries reference rm.notes (column does not exist locally) and
    si.log_frequency (column does not exist locally). Surfaced in
    BE logs during this session's local debugging. Likely same
    migration-apply gap as #57. Bundle into one investigation
    followup or split per-table — Owner to decide.

  - Followup #60 (NEW process) — Mid-session prod accident: parent
    fixture creation intended for local was executed against prod
    (browser was on tiertrak.scholarpathsystems.org, not
    localhost:5173). One row accidentally created in prod tenant 3
    (Lincoln demo). Cleaned up atomically via psql with row-count
    safety guard, no FK fallout, no PII external leak (synthetic
    test fixture, undeliverable email). Owner-side process
    hardening: explicit URL-bar verification before any form
    submission against "local" UI. Sub-rule banked: Cowork-mode
    instructions should ask Owner to confirm URL bar reads
    "localhost:5173" before treating "I'm there" as ground truth.
    Treat as banked observation in the master index, not as code
    work.

  - Followup #61 (NEW) — Multer destination uses relative path
    'uploads/' in routes/csvImport.js:17-29. Currently safe because
    server.js does not serve uploads/ via static middleware, but
    relative-cwd footing is fragile (a future change running from
    a different cwd, or any mount of express.static near project
    root, would expose stale CSVs). Additional concern: the
    unlink-on-success and unlink-on-catch paths don't cover process
    crashes mid-parse — partial files can survive. Fix: pin
    destination to absolute path via path.join(__dirname, '..',
    'uploads'), and add a startup sweep for files older than N
    hours in uploads/ during server boot. Surfaced by
    security-reviewer on PR #50, not introduced by that PR.
    Standalone followup PR.

  - Followup #62 (NEW) — Convert tiertrak-api's DATABASE_URL env
    var from a hardcoded string to a Render `fromDatabase`
    reference linked to tiertrak-db.
    SUPERSEDED by #83 (Session 53, 2026-05-05) — the "Add from
    Database" UI control is a one-shot literal copy at click time,
    not a live datastore reference. See #83 for Path B (render.yaml
    IaC bootstrap), the only mechanism that creates a true live link.
    Currently the URL is hardcoded, so credential rotations don't
    auto-propagate to the deployed service — exactly the failure
    mode that caused the Session 47 prod outage. Use Render's
    "Add from Database" workflow on tiertrak-api → Environment →
    DATABASE_URL. After the switch, future credential rotations
    on tiertrak-db will trigger automatic redeploys with the
    correct URL. Verify by triggering a no-op rotation in a
    maintenance window after the switch.

  --- Already-committed deferred follow-ups (no number assigned) ---

  - Add rate limiter to POST /api/csv/students/:tenantId. Reviewer
    recommendation: express-rate-limit keyed on req.user.id, e.g.

    security-reviewer on PR #50 as an existing-posture WARN.
    Could be re-scoped under #61 or numbered separately next
    session.
  - Consolidate the duplicate requireAuth in

TASK SUMMARY
  Closes Followup #58 (FE env default to local) via PR #52. Investigated
  Followups #57 (users.password_hash NOT NULL drift) and #59 (rm.notes /
  si.log_frequency missing locally) per Priority 4 of the Session 47
  master index — landed a migration in PR #53, then closed it without
  merge after tenant-isolation-auditor returned 3 CRITICAL findings on
  the routes/students.js handlers that would consume the new schema.
  Both #57 and #59 remain OPEN and now bundle with the route fixes
  under new Followup #66 for Session 49.

  Investigation surfaced systemic migration-system bifurcation — half
  the migration chain lives in top-level migration-NNN-*.sql files,
  half lives in-process inside server.js as CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
  / ALTER TABLE blocks labeled `console.log('Migration NNN')`. The two
  systems share a numbering namespace and collide (#013 used twice for
  unrelated changes — file is password-reset, server.js is archive/
  delete-interventions; #015 is skipped; #007/#018/#020-022 are
  duplicated in both places). No migration ledger table exists.
  Tracked as new Followup #63.

  Also surfaced: CLAUDE.md has no dedicated migrations discipline
  section — concerns are scattered across §2A (branch naming), §3
  (raw SQL), §7 (failure conditions), §8 (ask-first triggers). No
  explicit rule requiring every prod schema change to be captured as
  a checked-in migration file. Tracked as new Followup #64.

PR #52 — chore/fe-dev-env-default-localhost → main (MERGED 183ed53)

  One-commit additive config-only change closing Followup #58:

    - 3757375 chore(fe-env): add committed dev default + .env.example.
      Adds frontend/.env.development (committed, single line
      VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api) so `npm run dev` defaults
      to a local backend regardless of any per-machine .env files.
      Adds frontend/.env.example documenting the Vite env-loading
      order and the full set of vars devs may need (VITE_API_URL,
      VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID).

  Pre-investigation reframed the problem: the original Followup #58
  framing implied `frontend/.env` was a checked-in file affecting
  all devs. Audit found it is gitignored at root .gitignore:6 and
  has never been in git — the prod URL was a per-machine artifact,
  not repo state. The frontend code already has a localhost fallback
  (`import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3000/api'` in
  AppContext.jsx, App.jsx, TemplateEditorModal.jsx,
  PlanTemplatePreviewModal.jsx). The fix was therefore to add a
  committed `.env.development` that wins over `.env` per Vite's
  documented loading priority (highest-to-lowest: .env.development.local
  > .env.development > .env.local > .env), giving fresh clones a
  zero-config localhost default.

  Verification via curl of the Vite dev server's transformed
  /src/context/AppContext.jsx and /src/App.jsx — both modules show
  `import.meta.env = {... "VITE_API_URL": "http://localhost:3000/api" ...}`
  injected at line 1, proving .env.development won the Vite priority
  race in dev mode despite the user's pre-existing per-machine
  frontend/.env still pointing at prod. Browser-tab verification was
  not performed (no browser available in the agent environment); the
  Vite-injected literal is the same value the browser would request,
  so the curl-based proof is equivalent.

  Reviewer verdicts: not required. Not a §2A trigger (no PII change,
  no tenant logic change, no auth/JWT change — pure FE config docs).
  Owner-approved self-merge.

PR #53 — migration/local-schema-drift-fix (CLOSED WITHOUT MERGE)

  One-commit migration capturing three out-of-band prod schema changes
  that were never written as repo migrations:

    - 0379d05 migration: capture out-of-band prod schema changes
      (#57, #59). Adds migration-025-capture-out-of-band-prod-changes.sql
      with three idempotent sections:
        1. ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN password_hash DROP NOT NULL —
           prod allows NULL to support token-only parent accounts
           pre-password-setup; closes the #57 half locally. Idempotent
           by PG semantics (DROP NOT NULL on a nullable column is a
           silent no-op).
        2. ALTER TABLE referral_monitoring ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
           notes TEXT, plus a DO $$ block adding CONSTRAINT
           referral_monitoring_student_id_key UNIQUE (student_id) only
           if no unique index/constraint on student_id already exists.
           The DO block uses a column-based pg_index check (not a
           name-based pg_constraint check) so the no-op works
           regardless of what name prod's existing constraint may
           have been given via out-of-band DDL. Closes the rm.notes
           half of #59 locally.
        3. ALTER TABLE student_interventions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
           log_frequency VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'weekly' — default
           matches routes/interventions.js:97. Closes the
           si.log_frequency half of #59 locally.

  Local apply (psql against localhost DB):
    - 4 statement completions ("ALTER TABLE", "ALTER TABLE", "DO",
      "ALTER TABLE"), 0 NOTICEs, 0 errors
    - users.password_hash → is_nullable=YES (no-op state, already
      nullable from Session 47 ad-hoc patch)
    - referral_monitoring → 6 columns (notes TEXT, nullable, added)
      + UNIQUE constraint referral_monitoring_student_id_key on
      student_id (added; pg_index cross-check confirms two unique
      indexes on the table, _pkey on id and the new _student_id_key
      on student_id)
    - student_interventions → log_frequency VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT
      'weekly' added (column 25, was 24-column table)

  Reviewer verdicts:
    - privacy-reviewer: APPROVE WITH NOTES — 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN, 3
      INFO. WARNs are pre-existing in routes/students.js
      (referral-monitoring handlers lack auth + tenant scoping;
      error responses echo error.message which could leak rm.notes
      values via pg errors). Both flagged as out-of-scope for the
      migration but unblocked by it.
    - security-reviewer: APPROVE WITH NOTES — 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN, 3
      INFO. WARNs are pre-existing — input validation/sanitization
      gap on POST /referral-monitoring (req.body.notes free text,
      req.body.tenant_id trusted from client); rate-limiter gap on
      auth.js password endpoints. Verified the password_hash NULL
      drop is safe for the current auth code: routes/auth.js:117
      and :595 both short-circuit on `if (!user.password_hash)`
      before bcrypt.compare; Google login at :243 explicitly rejects
      role=='parent', so a NULL-hash parent cannot mint a JWT. NULL
      password_hash cannot bypass auth in the current codebase.
    - tenant-isolation-auditor: BLOCKED — 3 CRITICAL, 2 WARN, 2 INFO.
      All 3 CRITICAL are in routes/students.js handlers that consume
      the new schema:
        a. POST /referral-monitoring (lines 285-304) trusts
           req.body.tenant_id; combined with the new UNIQUE(student_id)
           + ON CONFLICT (student_id) DO UPDATE, an authenticated
           Tenant A user can overwrite Tenant B's monitoring row by
           POSTing { student_id: <Tenant B's student>, tenant_id:
           <Tenant A>, ... } — a cross-tenant write primitive on
           PII per §4B (rm.notes = staff-authored monitoring notes
           on a tenant-scoped student table).
        b. GET /referral-monitoring/:tenantId (lines 226-258) reads
           tenant from URL param without requireAuth — FERPA
           exposure path. The new rm.notes column expands what
           leaks (full PII row including student first/last name,
           grade, tier, monitoring notes, monitor's full name,
           ratings).
        c. DELETE /referral-monitoring/:studentId (lines 307-316)
           has no tenant clause and no requireAuth — any caller
           can delete any tenant's row by guessing sequential
           SERIAL student_ids.
      WARN #1: constraint shape should be UNIQUE(tenant_id,
      student_id) to match the rest of the schema's pattern (users
      at schema.sql:24, screener_results at schema.sql:97) and
      provide DB-layer defense-in-depth — under that shape, the
      cross-tenant write primitive in CRITICAL #a fails at the SQL
      layer (no matching unique constraint for the ON CONFLICT
      target).

  Owner closed PR #53 without merge per the auditor's structural
  argument: codifying UNIQUE(student_id) as the official schema
  shape would lock in a vulnerability that UNIQUE(tenant_id,
  student_id) prevents at the DB layer. The migration + the three
  route fixes will be folded into a single sec/ PR
  (sec/students-referral-monitoring) in Session 49 so the
  cross-tenant write primitive never exists in any merged state.
  Tracked as new Followup #66.

  Branch migration/local-schema-drift-fix preserved both local and
  remote per Owner direction — Session 49 will start fresh from
  main with the corrected constraint shape, but the existing
  branch's commit (0379d05) remains as a reference for the
  verification output and the pg_index defensive idempotency
  pattern that worked correctly. Migration filename "migration-025-"
  is also preserved as a slot reference; see "MIGRATION 025 NUMBER
  SLOT" note below.

MIGRATION 025 NUMBER SLOT — collision warning for Session 49

  PR #53's migration file was named migration-025-... because 024
  (multi-screener-support.sql) was the highest existing number on
  main. The file did not land in main (PR closed). Session 47's
  master index also lists Followup #49 as "Migration 025: dedup +
  re-add the 6-column UNIQUE with NULLS NOT DISTINCT" for the
  multi-screener PR2 work — i.e., two distinct planned migrations
  were both targeting the 025 slot. Whichever lands in main first
  claims 025; the other will need to renumber to 026. Session 49
  should pick the order explicitly (Owner judgment) before either
  PR is opened so the renumber is intentional, not a merge-conflict
  surprise.

NEW Followup:
#68 — sec/referral-candidates-tenant-scope (CRITICAL).
  routes/students.js:160-225 (the candidates handler that
  returns students NOT yet being monitored) has the same
  exposure shape as the GET /referral-monitoring/:tenantId
  handler that PR #55 just closed: no requireAuth, URL-trusted
  tenantId, returns student PII. Surfaced by tenant-isolation-

  category in the system.

  tenant binding via local helper (loadFormAndAssertTenant) +
  actor-identity derivation from req.user instead of body
  (server-derived referred_by, referring_staff_name,
  counselor_id, counselor_name).

  (unlike PR #55 which bundled migration-025 for referral_
  monitoring). Pure route work.
- Role gates: parent blocked on every route except /options
  (static config). Approve/request-changes restricted to
  {counselor, school_admin, district_admin}. Archive/delete
  restricted to {school_admin, district_admin}. Narrower-then-
  loosen reasoning documented in commit 5 message — if product
  reality needs wider sets, widen via followup after
  discovering it.
- All 3 reviewer subagents APPROVED. 0 CRITICAL across the
  board. security-reviewer 0 WARN, tenant-isolation-auditor
  0 WARN, privacy-reviewer 2 WARN both flagged as repo-wide
  pre-existing patterns and explicitly "do not block" —
  captured as new followups #70 and #71.
- 56-scenario smoke matrix passed clean across six sections:
  6 read happy paths, 13 unauth probes, 13 parent probes
  (1 happy on /options + 12 blocked), 9 cross-tenant probes
  with byte-identical 403, 6 wrong-role probes, 9 actor-
  identity + write happy/status-mismatch verifications. Section
  F actor-identity scenarios proved end-to-end body silent-drop:
  POST claims of tenant_id:99 / referred_by:999 / counselor_id:
  fake landed in DB with caller's actual JWT-derived values.
- Local-only artifact: 5 test-user passwords on local DB reset
  to a known-shared smoke value via direct bcrypt UPDATE for
  cookie acquisition during smoke testing. Local-only, never
  written to prod, documented in the PR Verification section.
- Non-Owner human reviewed and approved per §2A.
- 2 NEW followups filed (#70, #71).

NEW Followups:
#70 — Repo-wide console.error(prefix, error) pattern leaks
  pg error.detail in server logs. pg error.detail can carry
  row values from FK or UNIQUE violations — including columns
  like parent_email, medical_diagnoses, mental_health_diagnoses.
  Add a safeLogPgError(prefix, err) helper that strips
  err.detail, err.where, err.table before logging. Affects
  every route with try/catch on a pg query — broad refactor PR.
  HIGH severity (log-side PII leakage is one of the easier
  paths for medical PII to escape into log aggregation tools).
  Surfaced by privacy-reviewer on PR #59.
#71 — SELECT pf.* on prereferralForms rows 2, 4, 6 returns
  more columns than the FE actually consumes. Replace with
  explicit column lists. Defense-in-depth: prevents accidental
  future PII column adds from leaking. MEDIUM severity, narrow
  scope, tiny PR. Surfaced by privacy-reviewer on PR #59.

TASK SUMMARY

Session 52 (May 4, 2026):
- PR #61 (sec/parentLinks-require-auth) opened at
  https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/61, awaiting
  non-owner human reviewer per §2A. 5 commits closing the last
  remaining CRITICAL exposure from the Session 46 audit on
  routes/parentLinks.js.
- Patch shape: mirrors PR #59's canonical sec/ pattern with two
  structural deviations driven by parent_student_links lacking a
  tenant_id column — JOIN-based tenant derivation in helper +
  dropped status column from the helper's SELECT shape.
- Net-new mechanisms:
  * loadLinkAndAssertTenant helper (commit b515f4d) — mirrors
    PR #59's loadFormAndAssertTenant pattern with JOIN-adapted
    tenant assertion. Two failure branches collapse to byte-
    identical 403.
  * Two-tenant EXISTS-form cross-check on POST (commit db29f80) —
    single atomic SELECT with role='parent' clause to prevent
    linking teacher/admin/counselor users as "parents." 5 failure
    modes collapse to one byte-identical 403.
  * DELETE uses USING form for atomic defense-in-depth independent
    of helper. PG MVCC snapshot guarantees TOCTOU safety even on
    concurrent tenant moves.
- Order of operations on POST is load-bearing and documented inline:
  requireAuth -> ADMIN_ROLES -> body validation -> cross-check ->
  cap query -> INSERT. The cap-before-INSERT order collapses cap-
  saturation and ON CONFLICT 409 paths for a saturated student —
  desirable probe-resistance discovered during smoke G.1b reframe;
  banked as feedback_cap_before_insert_collapses_error_paths memory.
- Smoke matrix: 56 scenarios + 9 byte-diff probe-resistance
  assertions, all pass, 0 net DB mutations across local-only
  synthetic test data. No production tenants probed at any point;
  3 synthetic users seeded + cleaned post-matrix; 1 link row
  remained in pre-matrix-pre-seed state on the local dev DB at
  close. Two local tenants used — both synthetic local-DB fixture
  names, neither maps to a real-staff-onboarded production tenant
  per master index roster.
- Reviewer phase: 3x APPROVED.
  * tenant-isolation-auditor: 0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN — every SQL
    query tenant-bound through req.user.tenant_id; both net-new
    mechanisms hold under hypothetical bypass scenarios.
  * privacy-reviewer: 0 CRITICAL / 2 WARN — both pre-existing on
    main, not regressions introduced by this PR. 5xx response
    bodies generified per commit 5; no PII in error responses.
  * security-reviewer: 0 CRITICAL / 3 WARN — all pre-existing
    repo-wide (no rate-limiter, no CSRF, parity-check guard
    inconsistency). None introduced by this PR.
  Total 5 WARN — all pre-existing repo-wide gaps, all filed as
  followups; no regressions introduced.
- Memory banking during session:
  * feedback_cap_before_insert_collapses_error_paths (probe-
    resistance pattern surfaced during smoke G.1b reframe — when
    POST has both row-count cap + uniqueness constraint, cap MUST
    fire before INSERT/ON CONFLICT for probe-resistance).
  * feedback_answer_all_confirmations (process discipline — when
    prompt asks for confirmations + additional work, answer every
    confirmation explicitly even if brief).
  * feedback_deviation_first_line_diff_summary (process discipline
    — in-flight implementation deviations: deviation goes in line 1
    of diff summary, not buried in body).

NEW Followups:
#83 — render.yaml IaC bootstrap (supersedes #62 Path A). Path A
  was attempted 2026-05-05 via Render dashboard's "Datastore URL"
  control; discovery: that UI feature is a one-shot literal copy
  at click time, NOT a live datastore reference. Visually
  identical to a plain env var in both edit and view modes — no
  chip, no badge, no link indicator. Auto-propagation goal NOT
  achieved by Path A; literal value matches the original, so no
  functional regression but no progress on the original goal
  either. Path B — render.yaml blueprint at repo root with
  fromDatabase: { name: tiertrak-db, property: connectionString }
  — is the only mechanism that creates a true live link. Scope
  includes full Render dashboard inventory (every env var, build
  command, instance type, region, resource name) + blueprint
  draft + sync verification, since the first blueprint sync
  overwrites dashboard reality with blueprint reality. §8
  ask-first per Session 35 deferral (activities.txt:2013-2023);
  §7 hosting/deployment-assumption change. Not picked up
  speculatively. HIGH.
#84 — Vercel→Render doc drift cleanup. CLAUDE.md §2 line 31 and
  STACK_ARCHITECTURE.md lines 16-34 still describe the backend as
  Vercel-hosted; reality (per memory user_db_workflow.md and
  activities.txt) is that tiertrak-api runs on Render. Drift
  first named in Session 35 (activities.txt:1758-1765,
  2013-2023) but never formally filed as a numbered followup.
  Resurfaced in Session 53 §0.4 doc-conflict flag on 2026-05-05
  during the #62 audit. Scope: update CLAUDE.md §2 line 31 and
  STACK_ARCHITECTURE.md lines 16-34 from Vercel-hosted-backend
  language to Render-hosted-backend language. Doc-only PR, no
  code changes, self-mergeable. LOW (doc hygiene).
#85 — Section 504 tab 404 on /api/student-504/active-form-set.
  Frontend Section 504 tab fires GET /api/student-504/active-
  form-set on page load and receives HTTP 404 (three times per
  load); console error labeled '[Section504Tab active form set]'.
  Document upload on the same page works correctly — only the
  active-form-set fetch fails. 404 is HTTP-level from the backend,
  not a CORS or auth-layer reject; backend is reachable. Diagnostic
  narrowing 2026-05-05: reproduced on a Tier 3 / Social-Emotional
  student with an existing 504 plan document on tenant 3 (Lincoln);
  non-504-flagged students on the same tenant load clean, so the
  404 is Section-504-tab-specific, not systemic. Other 504-flagged
  students not yet tested. Open question for Phase 1 audit: is the
  404 (a) "no active form set assigned to this student" with the
  frontend treating empty state as a hard error → frontend UX bug,
  or (b) route or query broken on the 504 active-form-set lookup →
  backend bug? Audit should start by reading the route definition
  (or confirming its absence) plus the frontend handler in the
  Section 504 tab component. Test a 504-flagged student that DOES
  have a form set assigned — if it loads clean, scenario (a); if
  it also 404s, scenario (b). Reviewers when fixed: privacy-
  reviewer (§4B 504-record sensitivity), tenant-isolation-auditor
  (any touched route), security-reviewer if the route definition
  changes. MEDIUM (functional bug, no user-visible crash; document
  upload on same page works).

PRODUCTION DB WRITES (Render psql one-shots, BEGIN/COMMIT, DO blocks)

  Vandercook sandbox tenant provisioning (tenant_id=11):
    - Date: 2026-05-05.
    - Mechanism: scripts/seed-tenant-sandbox-template.js, second
      production use after Mazapan (PR #36 / Session 40). No code
      change to repo; the script is general-purpose by design.
    - Roster file: ~/tiertrak-sandbox-rosters/vandercook-roster.js
      — kept outside the repo per the privacy banner discipline.
      Not committed; lives in the operator's home directory.
    - tenants: 1 row created. name='Vandercook Demo',
      type='district', subdomain='vandercook-demo'.
    - users: 4 rows created. 1 district_admin (admin@) + 3 staff
      (counselor@, teacher@, parent@) — all under
      vandercook.invalid (RFC 6761-reserved fictional TLD).
    - students: 36 rows. K-8, 4 per grade. Tier distribution
      6 / 14 / 16 (T3 / T2 / T1). Area mix per tier (Tier 3:
      4 Academic / 1 Behavior / 1 Social-Emotional; Tier 2:
      9 Academic / 3 Behavior / 2 Social-Emotional; Tier 1:
      16 Academic). Synthetic literary compound surnames; no real-
      Michigan-cluster surnames.
    - student_interventions: 20 rows (one per Tier-2/3 student;
      Tier 1 stays clean). Templates drawn from 6 distinct
      system-default intervention_templates: Phonics Intervention,
      Small Group Instruction, Check-in/Check-out, Social Skills
      Group, Self-Monitoring Checklist, Mentor Program. Author
      split: counselor handles behavior + SE + CICO (7); teacher
      handles academic instruction (13).
    - progress_notes: 46 rows. 2 per Tier 2 student (28) + 3 per
      Tier 3 student (18). Authored by the staff member who
      assigned the relevant intervention.
    - parent_student_links: 1 row (parent@vandercook.invalid →
      one Grade 4 Tier 3 Academic student).
    - tenant_intervention_bank: 61 rows auto-seeded (matching
      the system-default is_starter=TRUE template count; same
      shape as Mazapan's 61).

    Single atomic DO block, three preflight RAISE EXCEPTIONs all
    passed on first attempt — subdomain available, all 4 seed
    emails not in use, all 6 system-default intervention template
    names matched exactly one row each. No Preflight raises
    (contrast with Mazapan's first-attempt Preflight 3 mismatch).
    Final RAISE NOTICE confirmed tenant id=11 post-commit.

    Mid-flight refinement: teacher account initially had empty
    student-list view despite intervention authorship. Root cause:
    TierTrak's visibility model requires either school_wide_access
    = TRUE or explicit roster assignment for non-school-wide
    teachers; intervention authorship alone does not grant student
    visibility. Resolved with a tenant-scoped UPDATE:
      UPDATE users SET school_wide_access = TRUE
       WHERE email = 'teacher@vandercook.invalid'
         AND tenant_id = 11;
    Single-row update verified post-COMMIT. Sandbox-seed enhancement
    candidate: default the teacher account's school_wide_access to
    TRUE for sandbox tenants (or surface a per-account flag in the
    roster template) so future sandbox demos don't repeat this
    hand-fix. Worth considering as a seed-tenant-sandbox-template.js
    enhancement followup at session close.

    Verification:
      - Pre-seed (script preflights): subdomain availability + 4
        seed emails not in use + all 6 system-default intervention
        template names present — all confirmed clean before COMMIT.
      - Post-seed counts: users=4 / students=36 / interventions=20
        / progress_notes=46 / parent_student_links=1 /
        tenant_intervention_bank=61 — all matched expected.
      - Tenant-isolation cross-check: SELECT DISTINCT tenant_id
        FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@vandercook.invalid'
        returned single row [11]. No cross-tenant leakage.
      - Live login (fresh incognito window): all 4 accounts
        verified — admin, counselor, teacher (post-fix), parent.
        Tenant view confirmed scoped to tenant_id=11 only.

  No §4B PII in this entry. Tenant id (integer 11) is an internal
  database identifier per established convention
  (activities.txt:2737). All student names are synthetic
