FOLLOWUP #62 PATH A AUDIT (the discovery that drove #83)

  Goal of #62: convert tiertrak-api's DATABASE_URL env var on
  Render from a hardcoded string to a fromDatabase reference
  linked to tiertrak-db, so future credential rotations auto-
  propagate (preventing recurrence of the Session 47 prod
  outage).

  Path A as scoped in the original #62 entry: "Render UI
  workflow, no code change" — use Render's "Add from Database"
  affordance in the env-var editor on the tiertrak-api service.

  Discovery during attempt: Render's "Datastore URL" UI control
  is a one-shot literal copy at click time, NOT a live datastore
  reference. After the click, the env var contained the same
  string as the Internal Database URL — but as plain text, with
  no auto-refresh on credential rotation. Visually identical to
  a plain env var in both edit and view modes — no chip, no
  badge, no link indicator distinguishing it from a literal
  value.

  Net result: no functional regression (literal value matches
  the original); but the auto-propagation goal was NOT achieved.
  Path A as originally scoped does not exist in Render's UI;
  the affordance that LOOKED like it would create a live link
  is actually a one-shot copy.

  Path B (render.yaml IaC blueprint with fromDatabase: { name:
  tiertrak-db, property: connectionString }) is the only
  mechanism that creates a true live link. Filed as Followup
  #83 with the SUPERSEDED note prepended on #62 (both on main
  from PR #63). §8 ask-first per Session 35 deferral; not
  picked up speculatively this session.

CATCHES (Cowork-side and Claude Code-side observations worth banking)

  1. Render UI feature affordance gap. The "Datastore URL"
     control in Render's env-var editor presents an action verb
     ("Add from Database") that suggests a live reference but
     produces a one-shot literal copy. There is no UI affordance
     (chip / badge / link indicator) distinguishing the result
     from a plain env var. Worth banking as a Render-platform
     gotcha for future operators: clicking the affordance does
     not produce what the wording implies.

  2. Sandbox seed teacher visibility. The seed script creates
     teacher accounts with school_wide_access=false (mirroring
     production posture for real teacher accounts). For sandbox
     demos, this produces an empty student-list view on first
     login — intervention authorship alone does not grant
     student visibility under TierTrak's model. Resolved inline
     this session via tenant-scoped UPDATE (recorded on main
     from PR #64). Implicit enhancement candidate flagged in
     PR #64 body but NOT yet filed as a numbered followup —
     candidate to file early in Session 54 if confirmed worth
     pursuing.

  3. Multi-PR Session 53 entry pattern. Three PRs landed on
     main mid-session (PR #61 + PR #63 + PR #64) plus this
     close PR. PR #63's framing established the convention
     ("AUGMENTS this Session 53 entry rather than replacing
     it"); each subsequent activities-touching PR extends the
     same Session 53 entry in place rather than opening a new
     entry per PR. Worth banking as the working convention for
     activity-heavy sessions where multiple branches land
     between session start and session close.

  4. Empty WIP branch deletion at session start. sec/safe-log-
     pg-error-sweep was carried into the session local-only
     with zero commits ahead of main and a tip date predating
     the Session 53 PR #61 merge. Audit confirmed the branch
     was strictly behind main; safe-deleted with `git branch
     -d` after switching to main. No work lost. Worth banking
     as the safe-by-default cleanup pattern: audit (zero ahead
     vs main + tip-date check) BEFORE deletion; never escalate
     to `-D` to mask a surprise.

      approvals dismissed on new commits, force-push blocked, admin
      bypass for pull requests retained as break-glass). PR #68 was
      the live test of the new mechanism — owner self-reviews
      recorded as state=COMMENTED did NOT flip reviewDecision;
      non-author collaborator's state=APPROVED review was the entry
      that satisfied the gate. Mechanism working as designed.

      origin/main covering Followups #80 (rate-limit) and #81
      (CSRF). All five layered limiters in place (auth-IP,
      auth-login compound, contact, mutation-user, csv-import);
      CSRF middleware mounted in MONITOR MODE; trust proxy set to
      1 for Render single-LB topology with explicit Cloudflare
      caveat in code; eager boot validation for three env vars
      (RATE_LIMIT_REDIS_URL, LOG_IP_PEPPER, CSRF_SECRET) with

      X-CSRF-Token wiring deferred to next branch
      (sec/csrf-fe-wiring) per the agreed PR-1 / PR-2 split.
      Branch pushed; PR opens Session 56.

      boots in 2s with all three eager-validation warnings, no

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  no DB query changes. The new middleware is orthogonal to
  tenant-scoping concerns; rate limiters key on IP / user.id
  (per-route variant), never on tenant_id (deliberate per the
  agreed config — tenant-scoping is not a useful key for K-12
  quotas at our scale). CSRF middleware does not query the
  database. All §5 invariants preserved.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: none. The new middleware controls request
    pipeline behavior (rate-limit + CSRF gate); does not read,
    write, or surface any student/staff fields.
  Logging audit: all rate-limit and CSRF log lines use the
    safePathForLog() helper (returns route templates, NOT
    resolved URLs with path-param values), hashIp() (sha256 +
    LOG_IP_PEPPER, never raw IPs), and never echo token values,
    request bodies, or headers. Same family as Followup #70's
    lesson on pg error.detail leak. 429 + 403 response bodies
    are constants ({"error":"Too many requests"} and
    {"error":"Invalid CSRF token"}); no method/url/body echo.
  Cross-tenant risk: none introduced.

    via `perl -i -pe 'chomp if eof'`, re-verified clean.

    escaping inside a single-quoted heredoc where shell
    expansion was already suppressed). Commit NOT amended
    per CLAUDE.md no-amend rule. Tooling rule captured for
    future commits: in <<'EOF' heredocs, plain backticks
    render as plain backticks; no escaping needed.

  Master-index drift correction (most consequential
  observation banked this session):
    Prior sessions' "rate limiters live in prod" / "CSRF
    middleware live in prod (monitor mode)" framing was
    wrong from PR #71 (Session 56) through this session.
    Production was running pre-rate-limit code throughout,
    masked by Render's "old healthy instance keeps serving
    on new-deploy port-bind failure" zero-downtime
    behavior. ded78fe is the first successful Render
    deploy of post-PR-71 code; PRs #71 through #76 went
    live together as one cumulative delta. Discipline:
    verify "X live in prod" claims against deploy logs /
    Render Events page, not just "PR merged."

  Library-behavior facts banked:
    - express-rate-limit ^7 default is fail-CLOSED via
      passOnStoreError: false (NOT fall-open; the comment
      in PR #71 and propagated through PR #75 was
      materially wrong; corrected in PR #76 commits
      50205e4 + baf0ece).
    - rate-limit-redis ^4 RedisStore constructor calls
      SCRIPT LOAD synchronously at module-load time,
      requires ioredis enableOfflineQueue: true to
      survive the brief initial-connection window
      (typically <100ms post-boot for DNS + TCP + AUTH).
    - ioredis maxRetriesPerRequest scope: bounds retries
      inside the command queue (sent commands awaiting a
      reply), NOT the offline queue. Offline-queue
      accumulation is bounded separately by ioredis's
      flushQueue-on-disconnect.

  Process / harness observations banked:
    - Recovery sequence cascade: peeling layers reveals
      more bugs. Don't claim victory on a fix until a
      single full clean boot is verified end-to-end.
    - Render keeps old healthy instance alive on deploy
      failure (zero-downtime feature, but masks silent
      failures if Events page isn't watched after each
      merge).
    - Audit-before-commit gate slip pattern recurred once
      this session (PR #74 commit 7372230). CC-side
      feedback memory written. Cowork-side pair-bank:
      split STOP gates into separate prompts rather than
      relying on internal halt language inside multi-step
      prompts.
    - Subagent-output delivery format risk: one
      truncation/omission incident on PR #76 round-1
      reports. CC-side feedback memory written about
      defensive delivery format.
    - Post-push GitHub PR-state indexing lag observed
      at ~2-3 seconds; verify steps right after git push
      should tolerate one re-query before reporting a
      discrepancy.
    - cwd-drift recurrence (5+ banked total). Discipline:
      chain `cd /path && ...` in every bash invocation
      regardless of confidence; bash's working-dir
      persistence between calls is unreliable in this
      harness.
    - §0.4 conflict-flag mechanism (Code-side correcting
      Cowork-side mental model) fired multiple times this
      session: LOG_IP_PEPPER eager-vs-lazy validation,
      fall-open vs fail-closed posture, Store-uniqueness
      vs single-store-still-works.

  Two new followups filed:
    - Followup W: server.js:14 eager-validation hook
      redundancy (server.js explicitly calls
      initializeRateLimitStore() at boot for env
      validation; the same call also fires when the first
      limiter constructor runs at module load — one of
      the two calls is redundant once the cached-init
      guard is in place).
    - Followup X: rate-limit fail-closed vs fail-open
      product decision. Current behavior is fail-closed
      via express-rate-limit's default passOnStoreError:
      false. Whether to flip each limiter to
      passOnStoreError: true for fail-open during Redis
      outages is a deliberate product decision —
      tradeoff between availability (fail-open) and
      bypass-prevention (fail-closed) under Redis
      failure.

  Existing followups preserved unchanged: A, C-I,
  J/#95 (RESOLVED), K/L/M, N, O, P (RESOLVED), Q,

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  CLOSED THIS SESSION:
    Followup #69 (MTSS backend IDOR — body-supplied tenant_id /
      student_id / created_by trusted; no requireAuth on POST/PUT/
      DELETE) — closed end-to-end by PR #81. The in-code "Followup
      67" reference also resolves here. CSRF flip-enforce (PR #80,
      S59) was the temporary de-facto narrowing; this is the
      proper backend fix.

  NEW (surfaced this session):
    mtss-meetings-intervention-reviews-length-cap (MEDIUM): per
      security-reviewer M1 on PR #81. POST/PUT pre-flight loops
      iterate over body.intervention_reviews with one DB roundtrip
      per entry, plus up to two more roundtrips per entry inside
      the transaction (snapshot helper + flag helper). express.json
      default limit is 100kb, which fits ~2500-3500 entries;
      combined with mutationUserLimiter at 300/min, a malicious
      in-tenant counselor can drive ~750k-1M DB roundtrips/min
      from one cookie before the rate limit hits. Cap at
      MAX_INTERVENTION_REVIEWS = 50 (~10× legitimate envelope)
      before the pre-flight loops run, or collapse to a single
      `SELECT … WHERE si.id = ANY($1) AND …` query. Bounded by
      requireAuth + role gate so not anonymous-reachable; strictly
      less exposed after PR #81 than before.

## PR #84 — sec/mtss-meetings-intervention-reviews-cap

MEDIUM-severity DoS amplifier mitigation, closes the followup filed at S60
close from PR #81 security-reviewer M1.

### Background

PR #81 (S60) closed the backend IDOR on /api/mtss-meetings POST/PUT/DELETE
with requireAuth + role gate + server-resolved tenant binding + per-row
authorization check. The per-row check introduced a DB-roundtrip amplifier:
~4N+3 roundtrips per POST (1 pre-flight + ~3 intra-transaction per entry)
and ~N+(0..2)N+4 per PUT. Bounded by mutationUserLimiter (300/60s per user)
and write-role gate (authenticated only), so not anonymous-reachable, but
unbounded per-request without a length cap.

### Implementation

- Branch: sec/mtss-meetings-intervention-reviews-cap off main@e138a22
- Commit: f263f69 — "sec(mtss-meetings): cap intervention_reviews.length at
  50 (PR #81 M1)"
- Diff: +22 lines in routes/mtssMeetings.js
  - 1 const MAX_INTERVENTION_REVIEWS = 50 with 2-line comment citing PR #81
    M1 origin
  - 7-line validation block inserted in POST handler (after missing-field
    400, before tenant gate)
  - 7-line validation block inserted in PUT handler (after body destructure,
    before per-row pre-flight)
- Validation guard order: !== undefined && !== null (allows field omission)
  → Array.isArray (catches type-confusion) → length > MAX (catches DoS
  amplifier). Each layer fires before the layer it protects.
- Error responses match in-file convention { error: <string> }; cap value
  baked into message via template literal (non-security-sensitive).
- Cap fires pre-BEGIN by construction on both handlers — a 400 cannot leave
  a half-written row.

### Array.isArray() scope expansion

Beyond original M1 framing. The existing per-row loop guard
(`if (intervention_reviews && intervention_reviews.length > 0)`) is true on
a string body, which then iterates `for...of` over characters and calls
`review.student_intervention_id` (undefined) on each → N junk pre-flight
roundtrips bounded only by string length. The isArray check closes this
type-confusion side channel that a pure numeric cap would have missed.

Mid-flight scope expansion flagged in PR body for review transparency.
Obviously correct expansion; banked.

### Review and merge

- Reviewer triad: none required per master-index categorization (security-
  only follow-up, no PII surface change, no SQL/auth/tenant logic touched)
- Collaborator approval: gh pr review --approve
- Gate verification: see Discipline observation below
- Merge: squash sha ac54c3d at 2026-05-10T22:09:12Z
- Render auto-deploy: dep-d80g42hoagis73ek4o3g, LIVE 2026-05-10T~22:09:42Z
  (deploy event timestamp 3:09 PM PT; ~30s post-merge per PR #82/#83
  baseline; operator-captured from Render Events)
- Post-merge state: working tree clean (same drift as session start —
  .claude/settings.json modified-unstaged, GR_SampleClass.pdf untracked,
  all 4 stashes intact); branch deleted both local and origin

### Smoke test

Operator-side verification post-deploy. DevTools Network panel approach
(per S58 PR #74 CSRF FE wiring; FE handles cookie + CSRF token transparently
through normal form submission).

Positive allow path:
- POST /api/mtss-meetings via the New MTSS Progress Review Meeting form
- Logged in as school_admin in Lincoln tenant (1 test student row,
  legitimate intervention_reviews envelope ~2 entries)
- Response: 201 Created, Content-Length 230, application/json
- Full path traversed: auth + role + cap validation + tenant gate + per-row
  intervention check + BEGIN → INSERT → COMMIT → 201

Deny-path tests (string body → 400 isArray; length=51 → 400 cap; length=50
junk → 403 off-by-one check) were deferred — paths are simple enough in
code that review caught them, and triggering them via DevTools Edit-and-
replay or curl was disproportionate to the change size. Positive smoke
confirms the validation block doesn't reject legitimate traffic, which is
the actual regression risk.

Discipline preserved per S60 banked: 201 verified at the response-body
bytes level via DevTools, not inferred from absence of errors.

## Discipline observations banked

### Mechanical reviewDecision halt fired correctly (S61 — major, first post-S60-banking activation)

PR #84 first merge attempt: Cowork issued a merge prompt prefixed "Gate
verified — proceed" based on operator-supplied GitHub UI screenshot showing
"Changes approved + 1 approval." Claude Code's belt-and-suspenders mechanical
check at execution time (`gh pr view 84 --json reviewDecision`) returned
REVIEW_REQUIRED, not APPROVED. Claude Code halted at the boundary, surfaced
the empty reviews: [] array, and waited for formal approval. Second attempt
cleared (reviewDecision: APPROVED, mergeStateStatus: CLEAN).

Why it matters: this is the inverse failure-shape of the S60 PR #81 slip.
In S60, Cowork accepted a verbal "Reviewer has approved" relay and the
operator caught it. In S61, Cowork issued a UI-screenshot-based "gate
verified" framing in the prompt, and Claude Code's mechanical check caught
the gap. UI rendering and reviewDecision JSON can diverge — by stale state,
formal-vs-comment ambiguity, or timing. The mechanical check at execution
time is the canonical state.

Discipline working as designed. First confirmed activation of the S60
banked discipline by Claude Code holding to the mechanical gate against a
re-issued "proceed" instruction. Important data point — confirms the
playbook's gate enforcement is bidirectional (Cowork→Claude Code as well
as operator→Cowork).

### Smoke-test scope-right-sizing for small-PR security follow-ups (S61 — minor)

For a 22-line input-validation cap with code review approving the operator,
positive-allow smoke alone (case iii) is acceptable evidence. The deny paths
are simple in code and code-review-covered. Operator burden of running
deny-path tests via DevTools Edit-and-replay or curl was disproportionate
to the change size. Distinguishes from auth/tenant gates (PR #81) where
S60-banked discipline requires both deny + allow paths positively verified.

### §2A merge-flow ran without slip on PR #83 (S61 — baseline observation)

PR #83 walked the full §2A merge-gate flow cleanly: collaborator approval
via gh pr review --approve → mechanical reviewDecision check → merge.
No verbal-claim shortcut, no UI-screenshot-vs-JSON divergence. Baseline
discipline shape working.

### Render deploy id capture gap on PR #83 (S61 — minor housekeeping)

PR #83's Render deploy id was not captured at merge time; LIVE timestamp
captured (21:54:31Z). Discipline note: when grabbing deploy LIVE timestamp
from Render Events, also capture the deploy id (format dep-<rand>) in the
same pass. Avoids the "go back and find it later" friction. Applied
correctly for PR #84 (dep-d80g42hoagis73ek4o3g + timestamp captured
together).

  reported "+269" from misreading the hunk-size field of the unified
  diff header (@@ -8019,3 +8019,269 @@) as the insertion count, when
  in fact the hunk spans 269 new-version lines of which 3 are unchanged
  context, 266 are insertions. Corrected explanation surfaced before
  commit. No false normalization claim banked.

PR #86 — sec/csrf-mismatch-log-fidelity
  Defect-fix + enrichment closing the S59 NEW HIGH followup. The
  pre-existing CSRF-mismatch log line had TWO silent defects, not just
  a fidelity gap:
    - userId=null ALWAYS (req.user populated only inside requireAuth,
      which is downstream of CSRF middleware mount)
    - path=/api ALWAYS (CSRF middleware mounted at /api prefix fires
      BEFORE sub-router route resolution, so req.route is undefined
      and req.baseUrl is just /api)

  These two field-shape collapses are exactly what crippled the S59
  PR #79 CSRF debug arc. PR #86 closes both.

  ### Implementation

  - Branch: sec/csrf-mismatch-log-fidelity off main@6ece9bf
  - Commit: 536d444 — "sec(csrf): enrich mismatch log fidelity +
    pepper-hash peeked userId"
  - Diff: +118 / -13 across 3 files
    - middleware/rateLimiters.js (+77/-6): safePathForLog enrichment
      with numeric-segment redaction (regex /\/\d+(?=\/|$)/g → /:id);
      new LOG_USER_PEPPER machinery (DEV constant, cached, validated,
      getLogUserPepper, hashUserId) mirroring the LOG_IP_PEPPER
      pattern; exports updated
    - middleware/csrfProtection.js (+35/-5): jwt require + hashUserId
      import; new peekUserIdFromCookie helper with destructure-and-
      discard pattern (binds ONLY decoded.id, never email or
      tenant_id even transiently); log-line swap from userId=null
      to hashedUserId=<8hex>|none
    - server.js (+4/-2): import getLogUserPepper + boot-time eager-
      validate call adjacent to getLogIpPepper(); comment block
      updated to list LOG_USER_PEPPER alongside other prod-required
      vars

  ### Design framings (six locked before implementation)

    1. Defect-fix + enrichment framing (not pure fidelity), to reflect
       that the userId=null was a silent always-null defect.
    2. Numeric-segment redaction is the PRIMARY path-logging strategy
       at the CSRF site, not a fallback. Route-template was investigated
       and found infeasible at the prefix mount.
    3. Cookie-peek destructure-and-discard pattern explicitly documented
       at the helper.
    4. safePathForLog change enriches both CSRF-mismatch and rate-limit
       429 logs. Drive-by helper expansion intentional, not scope
       creep. No log line regresses.
    5. Boot-time FATAL for LOG_USER_PEPPER mirrors LOG_IP_PEPPER exactly.
       Dev fallback constant 'dev-user-pepper-not-for-prod' with
       warn-level log.
    6. jwt.decode (not jwt.verify) is explicit; CSRF failures are
       pre-auth events. Diagnostic-only, never authorization. All
       decode failure modes silently caught, return null.

  ### Reviewer dyad (not triad)

  - privacy-reviewer: APPROVED. 7 of 7 questions OK. Two non-blocking
    notes (UUID/slug regex limitation; pre-existing raw userId in
    mutationUserLimiter). BLOCKS MERGE? no.
  - security-reviewer: OK. 0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 2 LOW (null-
    handling and sentinel-string symmetry deltas vs LOG_IP_PEPPER),
    1 INFO (same pre-existing mutationUserLimiter raw userId).
    BLOCKS MERGE? no.

  No tenant-isolation-auditor since no SQL, no DB queries, no tenant
  logic touched. Reviewer dyad, not triad — lingo precision banked
  (see Discipline Observation 6).

  Both reviewer reports posted VERBATIM into the PR body and
  conversation for owner-side and Cowork-side eyeballing.

  ### Review and merge

  - Collaborator approval: gh pr review --approve, formal, submitted
    2026-05-10T23:18:32Z against commit 536d444
  - First merge attempt: mechanical reviewDecision halt fired correctly
    (see Discipline Observation 1)

    time
  - Merge: squash sha 2a10abd at 2026-05-10T23:19:04Z
  - Time-from-approval-to-merge: ~32 seconds

  ### Render deploy — fail-fast + recovery

  First deploy attempt: FAILED at boot with the expected FATAL —
    "FATAL: LOG_USER_PEPPER must be set in production. Aborting
    startup."
  Process exited 1. Render auto-retry: same failure. Previous deploy
  continued serving traffic — zero downtime.

  Operator-side recovery: LOG_USER_PEPPER set in Render web service
  env vars via dashboard. Second deploy attempt:
  dep-d80h75rtqb8s7386nu90, LIVE 2026-05-10T23:24Z (~5 min post-merge
  — consistent with FATAL-retry + env-set + redeploy cycle).

  POSITIVE prod confirmation banked: error message matches the code
  literal verbatim; process.exit(1) fired as designed; Render auto-
  retry behavior worked as expected; previous deploy continued to
  serve traffic so no downtime occurred during the ~5-minute recovery
  window; isProdLike() classified Render correctly. The boot-time
  fail-fast guard mechanism works exactly as designed in prod — first
  time confirmed end-to-end on a real Render deploy.

  Working tree baseline restored (same drift as session start —
  settings.json modified-unstaged, GR_SampleClass.pdf untracked, all
  4 stashes intact). Local branch + remote branch deleted by gh's

  log-fidelity pruned surgically with git branch -dr (was 536d444).

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: none directly. PR #86 changed the shape of the
    CSRF-mismatch log line — the userId field went from always-null
    to a pepper-hashed cookie-peeked diagnostic identifier. Net
    direction: PII surface SHRINKS, not grows. No raw IDs, no email,
    no tenant_id, no names appear in any log surface.
  Logging audit: verified no PII in logs. Destructure-and-discard at
    peekUserIdFromCookie binds ONLY decoded.id; email and tenant_id
    from the JWT payload are never bound to a local variable. SHA-256
    pepper-hashing truncated to 8 hex chars is non-reversible without
    the server-side LOG_USER_PEPPER secret. Privacy-reviewer subagent
    APPROVED with 0 CRITICAL.
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified. No SQL, no DB queries, no
    tenant-scoping code touched.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS BANKED

  1. Mechanical reviewDecision halt fired correctly on PR #86's first
     merge attempt (operator-side step-1-skip variant). Third
     consecutive session of the gate firing across different gap-
     shapes:
       - S60 PR #81: verbal "Reviewer has approved" relay variant
       - S61 PR #84: UI-screenshot "gate verified — proceed" framing
         variant
       - S62 PR #86: operator-side step-1-skip variant (formal
         approval not yet submitted at the moment of the merge
         instruction)
     The discipline is now confirmed bidirectional (operator-→Cowork
     AND Cowork-→Claude-Code) and skip-shape-agnostic. The mechanical
     check at execution time is the canonical state regardless of
     framing source. Banked as a stable habit, not a one-time
     observation.

  2. Named subagent auto-loading stable for third consecutive session.
     Both privacy-reviewer and security-reviewer resolved by exact
     name from a single parallel-Agent dispatch with no anomalies. No
     name-disambiguation prompts, no fallback to general-purpose, no
     parallel-execution cross-talk.
       - S60 PR #81: security-reviewer (single)
       - S61 PR #84: security-reviewer (single)
       - S62 PR #86: privacy-reviewer + security-reviewer (parallel
         dyad)
     Followup G/#92 ready to retire the general-purpose workaround on
     next CLAUDE.md edit.

  3. NEW GAP banked: env-var-introducing PRs need an explicit pre-
     merge step "set the env var in Render's prod environment BEFORE
     merging." Caught post-merge on PR #86; deploy correctly fail-
     closed (positive evidence) but the ~5-minute recovery cost was
     avoidable with a pre-merge env-set step. Workflow item to fold
     into future sec/-branch playbook: when boot-time FATAL guards
     are added for new env vars, set the prod env var BEFORE the
     merge-button push. Sister principle to the "diff before commit"
     gate — set the env before the FATAL fires.

  4. NEW GAP banked: Render service-precision in planning prompts.
     Future references should specify "web service → Events tab" (or
     "redis service → Logs tab", etc.) rather than just "Render
     Events" — the TierTrak stack on Render has three services (web,
     postgres, redis) and ambiguity between them slows down operator-
     side captures. Minor terminology hardening for the docs.

  5. POSITIVE prod confirmation banked: boot-time FATAL guard mechanism
     works exactly as designed end-to-end on a real Render deploy.
     The shape of the discipline:
       - Error message matches code literal verbatim
       - process.exit(1) fired
       - Render auto-retried with same result
       - Previous deploy continued serving traffic; no downtime
       - isProdLike() classified Render correctly (not 'development',
         not 'test', therefore prod-strict path fired)
       - Recovery via env-set + redeploy was clean; second deploy
         succeeded on first try
     First time this guard pattern has been triggered for real in prod
     — banked as a verified working safety net for all three guards
     (LOG_IP_PEPPER, LOG_USER_PEPPER, CSRF_SECRET) since they share
     the implementation pattern.

  6. Reviewer DYAD vs TRIAD lingo precision: PR #86 was a dyad (privacy
     + security), not a triad. Tenant-isolation-auditor was correctly
     skipped because there's no SQL, no DB queries, no tenant logic in
     the diff. Going forward, use "dyad" when 2 reviewers, "triad"
     when 3, "single" when 1, to avoid muddling the discipline
     communication. Minor vocabulary tightening, banked for future
     PRs.

  7. Diff-vs-summary discipline: PR #86's pre-commit summary substituted
     for the full diff Cowork had requested. Cowork specifically asked
     for the full csrfProtection.js diff to eyeball the destructure-
     and-discard pattern and the comment block; the other two files
     were accepted at summary-level. Banked: when full diff is
     requested, send the raw diff verbatim. Summary-on-top before the
     diff is fine, but the diff text itself is the deliverable. Don't
     substitute. Sister to the "stop at the stat checkpoint before
     commit" discipline — the literal artifact, not a paraphrase.

  8. Operator-side hold-for-explicit-proceed discipline: Cowork quoted
     a sample prompt back to Claude Code without an explicit "go."
     Claude Code correctly held and surfaced the ambiguity rather than
     proceeding on inferred intent. Same family as the mechanical
     reviewDecision gate discipline — don't infer approval from framing
     source. Bidirectional: applies to operator-→Cowork prompts AND
     Cowork-→Claude-Code prompts. The "say the word" instruction in
     the prior message is the contract; if the contract isn't
     explicitly satisfied, hold and surface.

      reviewer feedback, all single-file in middleware/rateLimiters.js:
        (a) mutationUserLimiter 429 handler still logs raw userId —
            swap to hashedUserId via hashUserId helper now exported
            from this file. Both reviewers (privacy N2, security INFO)
            independently flagged. Pre-existing pattern, not introduced
            by PR #86, but the natural drive-by cleanup now that
            hashUserId exists.
        (b) hashUserId null-handling symmetry — security-reviewer Q9
            LOW. Use `if (!userId)` instead of `if (userId == null)`
            to fold 0/'' into 'none' for exact mirror with hashIp's
            `if (!ip)`.
        (c) Sentinel string symmetry — security-reviewer Q9 LOW.
            hashIp returns 'unknown', hashUserId returns 'none'. Pick
            one for symmetric greppability; security reviewer's call
            which way to align.
      Reviewer dyad needed (privacy + security). Single-PR bundle in
      middleware/rateLimiters.js.

    - safePathForLog-uuid-slug-regex-extension (DORMANT). Activates
      when the first UUID-shaped or slug-shaped path-param route is
      introduced into TierTrak. Today the regex /\/\d+(?=\/|$)/g
      redacts pure-digit segments only; UUIDs and slugs would survive
      into logs. Inline code comment at middleware/rateLimiters.js
      (the safePathForLog function block) serves as a trip-wire — the
      next route author who introduces a non-numeric path param
      should file this followup at that time.

    Detection: "Google Authentication failed" user-facing,

      DATABASE_URL (which was hardcoded to a non-default
      credential created S58/S59).

      post-restart self-login smoke succeeded.
    Duration: ~5-10 min from detection to verified recovery.
    No data loss. No partial-write window because the failure
    mode was connection-refused, not partial-commit.

      exclusive per row given role-string CHECK constraint.

      Closes Followup #76 PARTIAL for this route too.

UNPLANNED OPS TASK — HUMBLE (TENANT 9) ACCESS REVOCATION

  Trigger: operator request mid-session to revoke Humble ISD's
  access to their TierTrak sandbox today while keeping the
  tenant + sandbox data intact for re-use by other prospects.

  Lightest-path decision: scramble credentials on Humble user
  accounts; accept the JWT-session window (existing tokens stay
  valid until expiry; effective revocation within ~1 hour).

  Audit-first read-only audit:
    Local psql via Render CLI (render psql) failed to negotiate
    SSL in --command non-interactive mode (FATAL: SSL/TLS
    required; PGSSLMODE=require not honored; --
    -v sslmode=require not propagated as connection param).
    Pivoted to Render Web Shell on backend service container,
    using $DATABASE_URL as already present in the container env.
    SELECT (PII-safe shape — non-PII columns only) returned 6
    users in tenant 9 with role distribution captured above.

  Mutation transaction (in one psql session via Render shell):
    BEGIN;
    UPDATE users SET
      password_hash = NULL,
      google_id = NULL,
      password_reset_token = NULL,
      password_reset_expires = NULL,
      email = 'revoked_' || id || '@revoked.tiertrak.local',
      full_name = 'Revoked User'
    WHERE tenant_id = 9
    RETURNING id, role;
    -- (6 rows returned, roles per the distribution above)
    COMMIT;

  Smoke test: attempted login with original Humble email +
  arbitrary password → 'Invalid email or password'. Verified
  revocation effective for password-based login.

  Carry-forward: Humble portion of master-index tenant table
  needs tag update from "Internal demo, synthetic" to "Revoked
  Humble access 2026-05-11; sandbox available for new
  prospects". Not done this session; flag for next master-index
  edit.

RENDER DB DEFAULT-CREDENTIAL ROTATION + OUTAGE + RECOVERY

  Context: during the Humble psql connectivity troubleshooting
  phase, the default DB credential string was exposed in chat.
  Standard post-exposure response: rotate credential.

  Pre-rotation check that SHOULD have happened (didn't this
  time): grep for hardcoded DATABASE_URL references in
  application env, not just check "is rotation linked." Render
  auto-syncs only services that pull from the DB's "Connection
  Strings" UI directly; services with a hardcoded DATABASE_URL
  env var (pointing at a specific credential by name) won't
  auto-update on rotation.

  What happened: rotate clicked. Default credential rotated
  cleanly on the DB side. Backend's DATABASE_URL was hardcoded
  to tiertrak_app_2026_05_03 (a non-default credential, likely
  created in S58/S59 for the connection-pool-bug investigation;
  not the default that just got rotated). Render did NOT
  auto-update DATABASE_URL because it wasn't pointing at the
  default. Connection-string mismatch surfaced as
  connection-refused on the next pool acquisition. User-facing
  symptom: "Google Authentication failed" on attempted login,
  because the backend couldn't query the users table to validate
  the OAuth-resolved email.

  Recovery: Render Dashboard → tiertrak-api → Environment →
  DATABASE_URL → re-point to new Internal Connection String
  (default credential, freshly rotated). Save → auto-restart.
  ~30s post-restart, self-login via Google OAuth succeeded
  end-to-end.

  Duration: ~5-10 min from rotation click to verified recovery.
  No data loss. No partial-write window — failure mode was
  connection-refused, not partial-commit.

  Banked discipline (see also Memory Candidates section below):
  - Before rotating a Render DB credential, grep env vars across
    all services for hardcoded references to that specific
    credential name. Render's "is rotation linked" UI only tells
    you about default-credential consumers, not about services
    pointing at non-default credentials by explicit name.
  - Post-rotation smoke test as yourself, not just by triggering
    the revoked-user failure path. The revoked-user path returns
    "Invalid email or password" for both "user record cleared"
    AND "backend can't talk to DB" — indistinguishable to the
    operator without an additional control.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS BANKED / SURFACED THIS SESSION

  Continuation of the numbered series carried in the activities
  log. Banked into memory at session level (operator's call at
  S64 opening per the candidates list below).

  Obs #8 (continuing series): explicit-verb-at-each-boundary
  pattern reinforced. Across 3 PRs this session, every
  state-transition (commit / push / open PR / merge) gated on
  an explicit "proceed" verb from the operator. PR #88 P2's
  commit-stage halt on stat discrepancy (+3/-3 claim vs +5/-5
  actual) showed the discipline is load-bearing: silent
  proceed-anyway would have shipped a self-contradicting commit
  body. Memory feedback_stats_from_git_diff_stat banked at that
  halt; cited correctly in the subsequent PR #89 staging step.

  Obs #9 (NEW this session): pre-deploy-flag for unexpected-
  401-spike pattern. Before merging PR #89 the operator flagged
  the deploy-time risk that previously-unauthed routes would
  start 401-ing un-tokened callers; called out as informational,
  not blocking. Post-deploy no 401 spike observed (no integration
  partners on the surface; FE was already sending auth tokens).
  Pattern: when adding auth to previously-open routes, surface
  the deploy-time 401-spike risk explicitly in the merge prompt
  so the operator knows to watch Render logs.

  Obs #10 (NEW): convergent-grep-across-reviewers as verification
  primitive. PR #88 pass-2 review: both privacy-reviewer and
  security-reviewer independently ran a whole-repo grep for the
  old 'hashedUserId=none' literal and reported 0 hits. The
  agreement across two independent agents (each running their
  own grep, in their own working session, with no cross-talk) is
  the verification — not either grep in isolation.

  Obs #11 (NEW): summary-as-decision-grade-triage-table as Obs #7
  nuance. The PR #89 reviewer-triad summary I returned to the
  operator was accepted in lieu of fully-verbatim quoting
  because the format was a triage table with severity + source +
  recommendation columns — a decision-grade artifact, not a prose
  summary. Obs #7 ("paste verbatim, don't summarize") still
  applies for individual reviewer reports; the per-PR cross-cutting
  synthesis table is a separate accepted format.

  Obs #12 (NEW): full-router-enumeration as verification primitive.
  PR #89's module-load smoke loaded the router and enumerated
  all 8 mounted routes with their middleware chains. The single
  load-bearing assertion "every route now has requireAuth in its
  middleware chain" + "zero unauthed routes remain in this
  router" is more rigorous than spot-checking the 4 changed
  routes. Use for any auth-gate-adding PR going forward.

  Obs #13 (NEW): stat-preview-vs-stat-authoritative distinction.
  Banked across two PRs this session. In PR #88 the post-edit
  audit summary said "+3/-3" (semantic count of logical changes,
  wrong by line-count standards); --cached --stat at staging
  time showed +5/-5 (physical line count). Halt fired correctly.
  In PR #89 the post-edit audit explicitly prefixed the
  estimate with "I am not quoting this as the final stat" and
  cited the banked discipline; the staging-time --cached --stat
  confirmed +33/-22 and was reported as authoritative.

  Obs #14 (NEW): NOTE-not-INFO severity calibration. Security-
  reviewer pass-2 on PR #88 returned a finding asking that the
  operator note be preserved in the merge commit body. Calibrated
  it as NOTE (audit-trail recommendation, not a finding) not INFO
  (which would imply a code-level observation). Correct
  calibration — the recommendation acts on PR metadata not code.

  Obs #15 (NEW): pre-rotation env-var sweep for hardcoded
  credential names. The Render DB rotation outage is the
  load-bearing instance. Banked as a hard pre-check before any
  future credential rotation.

  Banked observations worth memory triage at S65 opening:
    1. "Self-merge" terminology clarification: in this project,
       "self-merge" = operator-as-merger, NOT operator-as-
       approver-and-merger. GitHub-side ruleset blocks
       self-approve mechanically (you cannot approve your own
       PR via gh pr review --approve). External reviewer is
       required even for doc-only chore PRs. The "doc-only,
       no reviewers required" wording in PR bodies refers
       only to the human-review gate for §4B/§5 PRs — the
       mechanical APPROVED state is still required by branch
       protection. Worth banking as feedback or as
       clarification under existing §2A memory.
    2. Doc-only-chore-floor merge-to-LIVE baseline now
       confirmed at ~37s across 3 data points (PR #87 37.6s
       S63, PR #90 36.7s S64, PR #91 37.6s S64). Tight cluster.
       Useful for future doc-only PRs as a sanity-check
       expected-time. Sub-floor or super-floor delta would
       indicate pipeline change. Worth banking as a project-
       memory or reference baseline.
    3. "Check existing file before specifying NEW file" —
       small operator-side planning discipline surfaced in P2
       item #6 (operator specified "NEW reference file" but
       existing reference_production_urls.md was semantically
       same-topic; Claude flagged + extended rather than
       creating a sibling). The convention dedup rule
       overrode the literal item name; operator's flexible
       phrasing ("use whatever filename/location convention
       you use") authorized the call. Worth banking as
       feedback for symmetric coverage with
       feedback_audit_plan_deviation.md (which covers the
       inverse: deviating from approved plan — this would
       cover deviating from a literal item-name when the
       memory-dedup rule applies).
    4. Audit-first phase saved scope drift on P4: initial
       single-substitution §2 fix (line 31 Vercel→Render)
       would have left §2 internally inconsistent (line 32
       still Supabase, line 44 still Redis-NOT-in-stack
       despite production reality). Pre-implementation
       audit caught lines 32 + 44 as same-migration-
       narrative drift; operator approved expanded scope.
       Pattern: doc-edits get a structural pre-read, not
       just a string-search. Worth banking as a discipline
       for future CLAUDE.md / docs/ai-context edits.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)

  PII fields touched: none persisted, written, or transmitted by
    code; one tenant-slug filename reference caught + anonymized in
    activities-log text (see failure modes below).

  §4B failure modes caught this session (NEW; three in one session):

    1. Activities entry pre-commit leak: the S64 entry being landed
       via PR #92 referenced a real tenant slug embedded in a
       filename (scripts/ops/[date]-[tenant]-revoke-audit.sql).
       Caught by the operator-imposed per-entry grep BEFORE staging.
       Anonymized via in-line string swap to [tenant] marker
       pre-commit. Final pre-commit greps (narrow + wide §4B
       patterns) both returned zero matches against the staged diff.

    2. PR body leak: the SAME literal slug appeared in the §4B
       audit-trail prose of PR #92's description (i.e., the
       remediation narrative ITSELF leaked the slug it was
       documenting the remediation of). Caught by post-creation
       gh-pr-view-grep. Remediated via gh pr edit --body-file with
       the literal slug replaced by the phrase "literal tenant slug
       → [tenant] marker". Body now permanently clean. Lesson
       banked: §4B applies to the FULL artifact surface (PR title,
       PR body, commit message, audit-trail prose), not just the
       staged file diff.

    3. Activities historical contamination (PRE-EXISTING, surfaced):
       3 occurrences of the same slug in activities.txt total
       across all session entries; lines 9234 + 9283 belong to the
       S63 entry that landed on main via PR #90 in S64, before the
       [tenant] marker convention existed. Per [feedback_4b_per_
       entry_standard] these are out-of-scope for the current PR's
       diff (per-entry standard, not whole-file standard). Filed
       as Followup #99 for a separate historical-audit pass.

  [tenant] anonymization marker convention ESTABLISHED this session.
    Going forward: any file-path, slug, or reference in activities-
    log or PR-body text that embeds a real tenant identifier MUST
    use the [tenant] marker. Tenant_id integers ("tenant 3") remain
    acceptable per skill bottom-guardrail. Vendor/assessment names
    (STAR, MAP, etc.) are also acceptable — these are not tenant
    identifiers.

    Pre-PR §4B sweep (narrow + wide grep on staged diff): both
      passes returned ZERO MATCHES after [tenant] anonymization.
    Staged stat: activities.txt | 299 +++... | 1 file changed,
      299 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-) — matches predicted
      +299/-0 (in-line char-delta from string swap; no line-delta).
    reviewDecision: APPROVED — flipped not by operator self-approve
      (mechanically blocked twice with "Review Can not approve your
      own pull request" from GitHub's addPullRequestReview GraphQL)
      but by an external collaborator account submitting gh pr
      review --approve at 20:27:05Z on commit d8b791a. THIS
      CORRECTS S64's BANKED "self-merge" TERMINOLOGY: doc-only
      chore PRs DO need external collaborator review for the
      mechanical APPROVED state, regardless of the "Docs or README
      only — None needed" human-review-gate semantics. Banked
      observation #1 from S64 entry predicted this; this session
      is the empirical confirmation.
    Merge style: --squash --delete-branch (matched PR #87/#90/#91
      precedent). Initial spec was --merge; precedent check from
      git log caught the deviation and surfaced before execution;
      corrected to --squash on second verb. Worth banking as a
      verification primitive (merge-style precedent check before
      gh pr merge).
    Merge commit: 1a3173e at 2026-05-11T20:30:07Z (13:30:07 PDT).
    Render LIVE timestamp: NOT CAPTURED — no RENDER_API_KEY in

      Read migration-024-multi-screener-support.sql (173 lines,

        WARNING on PG <15.
      Read migration-025-capture-out-of-band-prod-changes.sql (112
        lines) — confirmed the BEGIN/COMMIT transactional-wrapper
        pattern and the pg_index-based dynamic constraint discovery
        approach. Pattern mirrored in migration-027 design.
      Read migration-026-screener-types-lookup.sql (78 lines) —
        confirmed no FK from screener_results.assessment_type to
        screener_types.name in v1 (membership enforced in route

        INSERT 131, SELECT 208). Only ONE route file touches
        screener_results; surprisingly minimal surface.
      Activities-log historical grep: 22 hits across prior entries

    Prod recon via Render's web shell (operator-run; not from this
      Claude shell — no RENDER_API_KEY in shell, prod URL only
      lives in Render dashboard per [user_db_workflow]):
        PG major version: 16 (PG 15+ branch eligible for NULLS
          NOT DISTINCT — confirms migration-027's design path).
        Current constraint shape: 6-column UNIQUE on (tenant_id,
          student_id, assessment_type, subject, screening_period,
          school_year) WITHOUT NULLS NOT DISTINCT semantics.
          This is the post-Step-6-PG<15-fallback state captured
          in S45 activities entries — confirms prod has the
          wrong-semantics version of the constraint as predicted.
        Affected scope: 146 rows where student_id IS NULL in
          tenant 3. Operator inspection confirmed all 146 rows
          are test data; no customer data in affected scope.
        No additional NULL-student_id rows in any other tenant.

    "Option A" framing from S64 reframed as framing-only (not
      a substantive proposal); substantive 4-step design completed
      this session.

    4-step locked design (in migration-027-design.md):
      Step 1: DELETE FROM screener_results WHERE student_id IS
        NULL AND tenant_id = N — guarded by a 200-row ceiling
        check before execution; ABORT transaction if scope
        exceeds 200 rows. (Current scope is 146; ceiling gives
        ~37% safety margin for re-run scenarios.)
      Step 2: ALTER TABLE screener_results DROP CONSTRAINT IF
        EXISTS screener_results_unique_per_period_assessment.
      Step 3: ALTER TABLE screener_results ADD CONSTRAINT
        screener_results_unique_per_period_assessment UNIQUE
        NULLS NOT DISTINCT (tenant_id, student_id, assessment_
        type, subject, screening_period, school_year). PG 16
        eligible per prod recon Step 2; no version-gated
        fallback branch needed.
      Step 4: VERIFY block — SELECT against pg_constraint joined
        to pg_index to confirm indnullsnotdistinct=true on the
        new constraint; SELECT COUNT(*) of affected-shape rows
        (tenant 3, student_id IS NULL) confirms zero.
      Wrapper: BEGIN; ... COMMIT; — atomic, no half-applied
        intermediate state committed; if any step fails, full
        rollback.

    Reviewer triad (privacy + tenant-isolation + security)
      required for the SQL-authoring PR per Followup #49's
      MEDIUM/HIGH classification: DELETE + constraint reshape
      touches student-PII-bearing table even though all
      affected rows are test data; defense-in-depth.

  Code-surface verification: N/A. No backend or frontend code
    modified. Backend has no test suite (Followup #73), no lint
    config (Followup #74); frontend untouched.

NEW PROVISIONAL FOLLOWUPS (S65)

  Three new followups surfaced this session, provisional pending
  master-index assignment in S66 P3 housekeeping:

    #99 (provisional) — activities.txt historical PII audit.
      Lines 9234 + 9283 of activities.txt on main contain 2 real
      tenant-slug references from the S63 entry (landed via PR
      #90 in S64, before the [tenant] marker convention existed).
      Decision needed for S66+: retroactive in-place anonymization
      (history-rewrite via amended commit, riskier; loses the
      audit trail of the contamination) vs. forward-only via a
      new chore-PR that anonymizes the lines (additive but leaves
      the slug in git history). Either path requires tenant-
      isolation-auditor review before execution.

    #100 (provisional) — upload path allows NULL student_id rows.
      routes/screener.js:131 INSERT path accepts CSV uploads where
      the student doesn't resolve to an existing students row, and
      persists with student_id=NULL. This is the root cause of
      the dup-cluster shape migration-027 reshapes around. After
      #100's upload-time guard lands, migration-027's dedup
      becomes a one-time cleanup rather than ongoing risk.
      Sequencing call: migration-027 first (cleanup + constraint
      shape) THEN #100 (upload-time guard against new NULL
      writes), OR #100 first THEN migration-027 — operator/
      reviewer call in S66.

    sibling-to-#100 (provisional) — test CSVs don't auto-create
      students. Related to #100's root cause: when CSV upload
      encounters an unmatched student (by name/external_id), the
      current path writes a row with NULL student_id rather than
      either (a) rejecting the CSV row with a hard error or (b)
      auto-creating the students record. Product/UX call on
      which semantic is right BEFORE #100 implementation lands.

  Banked observations worth memory triage at S66 opening:
    1. Self-merge terminology empirically corrected: doc-only
       chore PRs DO need external collaborator approval for
       mechanical APPROVED state. The "Docs or README only —
       None needed" rule governs the human-review-gate
       SEMANTICS, NOT the mechanical state. S64 banked
       observation #1 predicted this; S65 confirmed empirically
       (two self-approve attempts both failed with the same
       GitHub error). Worth folding into existing memory or
       writing fresh.
    2. Merge-style precedent check is a permanent verification
       primitive: prior-PR pattern (--squash vs --merge) should
       be checked from git log BEFORE running gh pr merge.
       Caught a deviation this session (initial --merge spec
       reverted to --squash). Worth banking as a feedback
       memory.
    3. PR number vs Followup number collision: PR #92 landed
       with the same integer as Followup G/#92 (closed via
       PR #91 S64). Pure auto-increment coincidence; creates
       cross-reference ambiguity in future "ref #92" mentions.
       Probably not banking-worthy on its own but worth noting
       for S66 master-index sync.
    4. Render LIVE timestamp capture gap: first session in the
       recent run that didn't capture merge-to-LIVE for a
       doc-only-chore PR. Worth either (a) adding Render-watch
       helper with operator-side RENDER_API_KEY, (b)
       standardizing operator-side capture with paste-back, or
       (c) banking the gap and continuing without. Worth a
       project memory note.
    5. §4B applies to PR body audit-trail prose, not just the
       staged file diff. The literal slug leaked into PR #92's
       body even after the file diff was clean. Lesson: §4B
       grep passes apply to the FULL artifact surface (PR
       title, PR body, commit message, audit-trail prose).
       Worth banking as a feedback memory for symmetric
       coverage with the per-entry standard.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  Followup #103 — Doctrine/schema divergence (operator-named
    during reviewer triad acknowledgement):
    schema.sql:7 still permits tenants.type='district' even
    though §5 doctrine now says districts live in the districts
    table. Aggregates three convergent reviewer signals:
      tenant-isolation MEDIUM #1 (legacy type='district' rows
        become stranded shape — no districts.id link, no
        user_school_access viability).
      privacy WARN #3 (§5 line "Role alone never determines
        school access — the access table is the source of
        truth" describes target state; Session 1 ships table
        empty).
      security LOW #3 (§5 implies but doesn't state the
        accessible-school set must be resolved server-side on
        every request, not cached in JWT payload).
    Resolution path: either schema-side (narrow the CHECK to
    type='school' after a one-time data migration) or doctrine-
    side (soften §5 with a deprecation note on type='district').
    Recommend addressing in Session 2 or as a paired chore PR.

  Followups #104–#106 — operator-named range, exact mapping
    deferred to master-index sync. Candidate items from the
    reviewer triad that need durable cross-session tracking:
      (a) DDL locking pattern at district scale — security MEDIUM.
        Inline ADD COLUMN + ADD CONSTRAINT UNIQUE pattern in M028
        is safe today (small tables) but does not generalize to
        district-scale tables (50k+ users). Migration-template doc
        should add a CONCURRENTLY-aware split for large-table
        cases. Not a code change to this PR.
      (b) ON DELETE RESTRICT operator footgun — security LOW #1.
        Operator who tries DELETE FROM districts hits
        restrict_violation; risk is they reach for manual
        cascade workarounds. Belongs in a "district decommissioning"
        runbook, not the schema.
      (c) user_school_access audit-trail gap — security LOW #2.
        ON DELETE CASCADE on user deletion erases historical
        access record. Session 2 grant/revoke surface should write
        to a separate append-only audit table on grant/revoke/
        cascade events.

  Session 2 prep notes (NOT Followups — expected to be folded
    into the Session 2 PR description and verification plan):
      FK error messages leak integer scope IDs and table
        structure if Session 2 routes echo err.message/err.detail
        verbatim. Mitigation: catch 23503/23505 and return
        generic 400 ("invalid grant"). Convergent signal from
        privacy WARN #1 + security INFO #2.
      district-label column is customer-identifying metadata —
        OK in tenant-scoped API responses, NOT OK in logs / error
        bodies / cross-district surfaces. Session 2 routes that
        SELECT it must keep it out of hot logging paths.
      EXPLAIN ANALYZE the access-set resolution query at
        Session 2 implementation time to confirm idx_user_school
        _access_user is used and the plan doesn't degenerate.
        Security WARN — procedural, not a code defect.
      Four Session-2 hazards from tenant-isolation auditor:
        Hazard A (disjoint legacy-vs-district semantics — need a
          single resolveAccessibleTenantIds helper, branch on
          users.district_id IS NULL).
        Hazard B (users.tenant_id reconciliation — decide
          explicitly whether to clear it when district_id is set
          or treat it as ignored).
        Hazard C (legacy type='district' rows — Session 2 must
          emit one-time audit SELECT and reconcile).
        Hazard D (JWT must NOT carry accessibleTenantIds list —
          scope resolved server-side every request).

    PLATFORM_ADMIN_USER_IDS comma-separated, parsed once at module
    load. Empty/unset → fail-closed 403. Number-coerces req.user.id
    at the Set.has comparison for shape-drift resilience.

    Atomic INSERT districts row per legacy district-typed tenant
    → UPDATE each to type='school' via correlated sub-SELECT keyed
    on name → DO-block guard (RAISE EXCEPTION on any remaining
    district-typed row) → DROP CHECK → ADD CHECK. All in
    BEGIN/COMMIT. Idempotent. Revised mid-session from per-id to
    per-shape after pre-flight audit on prod (see PIVOT below).
  scripts/ops/audit-legacy-district-tenants.sql — NEW. Operator
    pre-flight read-only SELECT.
  CLAUDE.md — §5 paragraph documenting the dual-path access

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS (S76)
  §A: S75 §6 Mode 4 caught audit-citation drift pre-edit on the
      helper-block byte-range. The 1a commit-plan prompt required
      sed -n verification of source line ranges before copying. The
      S74 audit's L139-L152 citation was 14 lines; on-disk reality was
      38 lines of helper inside a 42-line block (with isPositiveInt
      dependency at L111-L113 plus blank L114 plus JSDoc + helper at
      L115-L152). CC surfaced the deviation pre-edit; Cowork confirmed
      Option A (copy the full 42-line block). Second deviation
      (screener.js missing resolveAccessibleTenantIds import) caught by
      the same verify-on-disk discipline. Banking lesson: per-action
      verify-on-disk catches semantic bugs cheap and well before they
      reach the diff.
  §B: Forecast variance accounting. Original D-2 forecast was ~+42 net
      midpoint, range +35-55, per S75 §7 calibration (sec/-class PRs
      run +37-40% over mechanical baseline) applied to the S74 audit's
      ~+30 mechanical estimate. Actual: +228 net across three target
      files. Root cause: the S74 mechanical baseline was estimated
      BEFORE Followup #125 was resolved (S75 P1) to per-school binding;
      the pre-#125 estimate captured a simpler row-derived-tenant_id
      replacement shape, not the helper-and-doctrine fan-out that
      per-school binding requires per file. §7 percentage inflator
      (37-40%) is unchanged; the mechanical baseline it was applied to
      was stale. Banking methodology refinement: for multi-file per-
      school sweeps, the right mechanical baseline is per-file shape
      (~D-1's +62 actual, single file with 4 POSTs) × file count, NOT
      Pattern-E-line-replacement count. D-3 and D-4 single-file each
      and should re-test the §7 band cleanly.
  §C: Third consecutive sec/-class PR landed without test or lint
      coverage per Followups #73 / #74 (carry-forward). Safety gate at
      commit time was node --check only. Behavior-equivalence verified
      by code-reading trace rather than runtime test. Known structural
      gap; elevation candidate for S77+ slot alongside Followup #22's
      settings.json drift. Cumulative across S74 + S75 + S76, three
      sec/-class PRs have landed on this gate.
  §D: Re-grep + classify discipline (introduced in 1b prompt for
      Pattern B verification) surfaced stale handler-internal comments
      at mtssMeetings.js:309 + :349 and screener.js:131 + :136 as a
      side-effect of the binding-source verification work.
      Documentation debt promoted from invisible to visible without
      additional discipline cost. The discipline pattern caught a
      documentation surface that the audit didn't enumerate.
  §E: New Followup banked: handler-comment-staleness from coupled
      wire-in changes. Specifically about handler-internal comments
      going stale because a PR's wire-in change invalidated the
      binding-source description but didn't update the comments.
      Sibling to Followup #130 line-citation-drift class but at the
      prose level rather than the line-number level. Sites tracked:
      mtssMeetings.js:309 + :349 (describe tenant_id as
      "req.user.tenant_id for POST") and screener.js:131 + :136
      (describe tenant as "NOT from req.body.tenantId," which is
      literally still true but contextually misleading under the new
      contract that admits req.body.target_tenant_id). Number to be
      assigned at S77 master-index sync. Recommended resolution: a
      chore PR sweeping these four sites + similar across other files
      post-D-4.

      consolidates resolveAndBindTargetTenant now also covers
      isPositiveInt, since they're paired by design (resolveAndBind
      calls isPositiveInt internally). Three near-identical file-local
      copies of BOTH helpers now exist (in mtssMeetings, screener,
      prereferralForms — plus the original in student504). Banked at
      S75 as future scope; S76 confirmed the pairing.
  §G: Behavior-equivalence trace as test-substitute discipline. 1b
      Step 4's code-reading trace — for each handler, walk: req.body
      absent → helper return → destructure → downstream consumers —
      was the load-bearing assurance for "legacy callers bit-for-bit
      identical to pre-PR." Clean across all three handlers; verified
      by tenant-isolation-auditor + privacy-reviewer independently.
      In the test/lint-absent state (#73/#74 carry-forward), this
      trace pattern is the operational substitute for an integration
      test. Worth banking as a reusable discipline template for future
      behavior-live commits in this codebase. The trace is
      substantively rigorous when paste-anchored at every site;
      weaker but still useful if site-by-site coverage is incomplete.
  §H: Phantom-line drift class broader than the S75 §5 sample. S75
      banked the watch as tenant-iso-auditor-specific recurrence;
      actual recurrence in S76 was in security-reviewer's report (two
      INFO citations with line-range drift — L46-48 cited for "helper
      copies" actually being doctrine-block ranges; POST location
      citations off by 1-9 lines). CC self-flagged on second read of
      the reviewer's report — exemplary CC discipline at report-relay
      time. The drift is line-precision, not substance; verdict and
      substantive findings unaffected. The broader watch language for
      future dispatch prompts should cover all reviewer classes
      (tenant-iso + security + privacy), not just tenant-iso.
  §I: §8 enforcement architecture has a SendMessage hole. Tenant-
      isolation-auditor cited feedback_helper_consume_dont_inline_5_branch
      un-paste-anchored despite the explicit hard-stop dispatch-prompt
      language. Cowork drafted a SendMessage continuation prompt; CC
      surfaced the tool's unavailability in CC's environment cleanly
      via ToolSearch paste-anchored negative result, then proposed
      three resolution paths with cost/benefit framing. Resolved via
      Option 3 (CC-side paste-anchor of the cited memory file). Sub-
      observation: S75 §2 zero-memory-citation posture worked cleanly
      when adopted — 2 of 3 reviewers (security + privacy) cited zero
      memory files and the §8 surface stayed quiet for those rounds.
      Tenant-isolation was the one violator. Banking recommendation:
      future dispatch prompts should either (a) verify enforcement-
      path tool availability before promising "re-dispatch" language,
      OR (b) emphasize the S75 §2 posture stronger (recommended) —
      the discipline architecture works best when reviewers opt out
      of memory citations entirely.
  §J: Cross-PR cross-corroboration on string-encoded integer rejection
      now triply-confirmed. S75 §3 banked the cross-reviewer
      corroboration pattern on PR-S3-D-1 (security INFO + privacy
      INFO both surfaced "target_tenant_id must be JSON number, not
      string"). S76 PR-S3-D-2 security-reviewer independently re-
      verified via Number.isInteger("5") === false. The by-design
      strictness property is now confirmed across two PRs and three
      reviewer rounds (D-1 security + D-1 privacy + D-2 security).
      Future FE-coupling work (Followup #131) must treat this as a
      load-bearing wire-contract: forms send target_tenant_id as JSON
      number, not stringified.
  §K: Empty user_school_access table is fail-closed by design,
      resolving the master-index latent-risk concern carried since
      S72. Privacy reviewer traced: helper's district branch queries
      user_school_access by user_id + district_id; empty result →
      empty array → accessible.includes(bodyTarget) is false → 403
      fires before any INSERT. The empty-table state IS the correct
      privacy state for the not-yet-exercised district-user path. A
      first district user with no grants + a bare target_tenant_id
      body field gets clean 403, never cross-tenant write. Operator
      backfill (still gated on Followups #122 + #123) can proceed at
      its own pace without per-PR worry about the empty-state
      introducing exposure. Master-index can drop "latent privacy
      risk for unexercised path" from carry-forward.
  §L: Pre-existing logging inconsistency at prereferralForms.js:437 —
      console.error logs full error object, not just .message.
      Inconsistent with mtssMeetings.js:494 and screener.js:240 (both
      use .message). PG error objects can include where/detail fields
      echoing column values. Pre-existing (not introduced by PR #117);
      surfaced by privacy reviewer as out-of-scope INFO. Chore-PR
      candidate. Recommended: fold into Followup #114 elevation
      (broader error-message redaction debt) as a concrete scoped
      site rather than its current somewhat-abstract scope.
  §M: S75 §6 Mode 4 paid off on its first post-banking test in the
      PR #117 merge ceremony. The "paste-anchor any state assertion
      to bytes-of-state in the same turn" rule eliminated the audit-
      trail defect class that PR #116's close entry locked in (the
      false local-branch-presence claim). PR #117 merge ceremony's
      local-branch-deletion claim was explicitly paste-anchored to
      empty `git branch --list` output; remote-branch deletion paste-
      anchored to empty `git ls-remote` output. Both clean; both
      eliminated entire failure mode at audit-trail composition time.
      Banking lesson: §6 Mode 4 is now proven-as-effective, not just
      banked-as-rule. Worth elevating from "guideline" to "standing
      pre-commit step for any state assertion in audit-trail sections."

after handler return violates §4B lifecycle requirements.

fs.unlink(req.file.path, () => {}) between the bindError check
opener and the return statement, mirroring outer-catch cleanup
pattern. Addressed in this PR via new 1c commit (not
git commit --amend) per Option 1 honest-disclosure discipline
from S76 §B; preserves discovery sequence in commit history.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS

§A. Conditional-greenlight phrasing read as unconditional.
PR-S3-D-3 / 1a commit landed at 2997eac before the gated diff-
byte review fired. Cowork prompt language was "Once I see the
diff bytes, you have 'ship 1a' greenlight: stage, commit"; CC
interpreted the second clause as standalone authorization,
short-circuiting the conditional clause. Subsequent gates in
the same PR adopted explicit standalone STOP markers and
unambiguous "wait for separate Cowork confirmation" framing;
the corrected pattern held across 1b, 1c, PR open, and merge
ceremonies. Generalizable banking: conditional-greenlight
phrasing where the conditional precedes the directive is
structurally ambiguous and should be replaced with explicit
two-step framing ("STOP after diff; await separate ship
confirmation"). Pattern observed once, immediately calibrated,
held thereafter.

§B. Verbatim stdout vs narrative summary paste latency.
Multiple turns in early 1a/1b cycles had CC describing command
outputs in narrative form rather than pasting verbatim stdout
("Three hunks visible above" with no diff bytes in the message,
or "all gates pass" with no grep stdout). Cowork-side layer-2
relied on verbatim bytes for byte-level verification; narrative
summaries arrived but the underlying bytes did not. Remediated
mid-session via explicit "paste verbatim stdout, no narrative
summary" framing in subsequent prompts. After that calibration,
verbatim paste discipline held. Banking: prompts requiring
byte-level layer-2 should include explicit "verbatim stdout"
framing; narrative-summary defaults are not sufficient for
multi-step verification.

§C. Post-action structural anchors derived from pre-action
context. The 1b plan-kickoff turn included a structural-anchor
paste describing requireMatchingTenant at L43-L49 and helper
at L51 — those line numbers were post-1a (pre-1b) state
carried forward without re-grepping the post-1a file. The
correct post-1a positions (requireMatchingTenant at L83,
helper at L91) had already been confirmed via earlier grep
in the same session. Remediated via fresh grep at the next
turn. Banking: structural anchors at action-class shift points
must be re-derived from a fresh grep against the post-action
file, not carried forward from prior-action context.

§D. Rendering-collapse of blank lines in chat code blocks.
CC's old_string and new_string drafts for Edit calls displayed
without visible blank lines that were present in the actual
Edit tool input. Cowork could not verify byte-equivalence from
the displayed text alone. Resolved via explicit [BLANK]
markers when authoring old_string/new_string for layer-2
review; once introduced, blank-line ambiguity was eliminated.
Banking: when authoring multi-line tool arguments for layer-2
review, use explicit blank-line markers (or cat -An style
output) so blanks are unambiguous in the displayed text.

§E. Relay-side §H phantom-line drift inversion. The tenant-
isolation-auditor's report cited helper at L111-L114 and POST
at L158; Cowork's relay-time §H flag claimed the citations
were drifted and that the post-1b positions were L103-L115 and
L161. Fresh on-disk grep confirmed the auditor's citations
were accurate; Cowork's relay claim was the actual drift
(derived from arithmetic projection rather than disk verification).
Banking refinement for S76 §H: phantom-line drift at relay time
is symmetric — it can land in the reviewer's report OR in the
Cowork-side counter-claim. The cure (paste-anchored bytes-on-
disk in the same turn as the citation) applies to both
directions. Subsequent relay-side §H passes in this session
adopted fresh-grep verification and held cleanly.

§F. Triad-report internal severity inconsistency. The privacy-
reviewer's report on PR-S3-D-3 / 1b filed the CSV-on-disk
cleanup-gap finding under INFO in the severity slot count but
framed it as WARN-tier in the narrative prose ("this surfaces
as a WARN ... is 'Privacy & compliance failure' per CLAUDE.md
§7"). Slot count was a clerical slip; prose was the authoritative
analytical conclusion. Cowork relay-time reclassified to WARN
per the substance. Banking: triad-report layer-2 should include
a slot-count-vs-prose consistency check alongside the §H line-
citation discipline.

§G. §I posture in dispatch prompts is informational, not
enforcement. The security-reviewer dispatch included the §I
posture ("skip memory citations entirely if disk-source evidence
is sufficient"); the reviewer cited the "ALWAYS import the
helper" memory entry by name anyway. The §I posture serves as
guidance but does not block reviewers from citing memory if
their internal reasoning surfaces it. Enforcement remains via
CLAUDE.md §8 + CC-side paste-anchor remediation per S76 §I
resolution path 3. Banking: §I-posture-in-prompt is a soft
suggestion; §8 + remediation is the hard enforcement gate.

§H. Heredoc escape verification grep-pattern shape. The PR-S3-
D-3 PR body was passed via cat <<'EOF' heredoc with several
backslash-escaped tokens (\$1, escaped backticks around code
references). Single-quoted heredoc preserves backslashes
literally; the rendered PR body had visible backslashes at
the three escape sites. Cowork's initial visual-check grep
pattern matched double-backslash sequences (the source-file
shape) and returned zero hits; the actual landed content was
single-backslash form (the rendered shape) and required a
revised pattern. Banking: when grep-checking for escape
artifacts in rendered content (PR bodies, markdown output,
documentation), the pattern must match the renderer's surface
form (typically single-backslash) not the source-encoding form
(typically double-backslash).

§I. Methodology refinement on per-school binding forecasts.
S76 §B distinguished multi-file per-school sweeps (per-file-
shape × file-count) from single-file sweeps (re-test §7 band).
PR-S3-D-3 was a single-file case; the §7-band hypothesis did
not hold. Helper-and-doctrine fixed cost (~+75 per file)
applies regardless of file count; the §7 percentage inflator
(37-40%) was developed against PRs without helper-and-doctrine
fan-out and does not apply to per-school binding PRs at all.
Refined model: per-school binding PRs use per-file-shape ×
file-count as the mechanical baseline regardless of file count;
no inflator on top. D-1 (+62 single file), D-2 (+228 = +76 avg
× 3 files), D-3 (+79 single file) all fit this single-shape
model within reasonable variance. Supersedes the S76 §B
single-file vs multi-file distinction.

§J. §G behavior-equivalence trace template held across three
commits. PR-S3-D-3 / 1b's wire-in trace (helper return →
destructure-rename → local tenantId → INSERT $1) was paste-
anchored at each site per the §G template banked at S76. Both
tenant-iso and privacy reviewers independently re-walked the
trace in their reports. Test-substitute discipline now triply-
applied (D-1, D-2, D-3) and remains the operational substitute
for missing integration tests under #73/#74 carry-forward.
Worth elevating to standing practice for sec/-class behavior-
live commits while test-config gap remains open.

§K. §M Mode 4 paste-anchoring held across three commit
ceremonies + merge ceremony. PR #119 merge ceremony
paste-anchored branch deletion via empty git branch --list,
empty git ls-remote, and fetch-prune triple-anchor — pattern
banked at S76 §M as standard practice held. No locked-in
defects in this session's audit-trail composition.

§L. Audit-first re-paste-anchoring caught structural deviations
pre-edit. The 1a commit-plan prompt required sed -n verification
of source line ranges; the audit-time recheck surfaced the
missing resolveAccessibleTenantIds import in csvImport.js
(needed by helper body) and confirmed helper-block byte-stability
vs student504.js:111-152. Both surfaces caught pre-edit; no
runtime bugs entered the diff. S76 §A discipline (per-action
verify-on-disk catches semantic bugs cheap) continues to pay
off — third consecutive PR (D-1, D-2, D-3) where audit-first
discipline surfaced structural deviations before they reached
file modifications.

§M. 1c remediation pattern (privacy finding → in-PR fix via
new commit) established as precedent. The privacy-reviewer
triad-pass finding on 1b's bindError-path CSV-on-disk
persistence was addressed in this PR via a single 1c commit
(+1 net, fs.unlink insertion) rather than spawned to a
derivative cleanup PR. Reasoning: §4B non-negotiable; fix
trivial; in-PR atomic; new commit (not amend) preserves
discovery sequence in commit history. Pattern banked for
future triad-surfaced findings: when a reviewer surfaces a
WARN+ finding addressable via a trivially small patch, in-PR
remediation via a new commit (not amend) is the proportionate
response. Preserves audit-trail integrity at the commit-history
level per S76 §B honest-disclosure discipline.

SECURITY REVIEW
------------------------------------------------------------------------

security-reviewer subagent dispatched. Verdict: APPROVED 0/0/0/0/0W/2I.

Eight scope items confirmed:

(a) Helper SQL surface: zero template-literal interpolation
    introduced. Helper executes zero SQL directly; only call is to
    pre-existing resolveAccessibleTenantIds middleware.

(b) isPositiveInt strict validation property re-confirmed:
    Number.isInteger(n) && n > 0 rejects strings, floats, negatives,
    zero, NaN, Infinity, null, undefined, booleans, objects. Applied
    at two gates: L99 (helper bodyTarget) and L418 (student_id at
    POST /referral-monitoring). SEXTUPLY-CONFIRMED across D-class
    series (S75 D-1 + S76 D-2 + S77 D-3 + S77 D-3 security + S78
    D-4 tenant-iso + S78 D-4 security). Property locked as load-
    bearing wire-contract per S77 §K standing.

(c) INSERT param flow: 2 modified INSERTs reviewed (POST / at
    L530-534; POST /referral-monitoring at L437-444); both fully
    parameterized $1-$9 / $1-$4. tenantId flows directly from
    helper return into param array without intermediate string
    concatenation. Pre-flight SELECT at L422-425 also parameterized.

(d) Error response body leak check (new surfaces): four new paths
    reviewed (L100 helper 400 'Invalid target_tenant_id'; L104
    helper 403 'Not authorized for target tenant'; L419 isPositive-
    Int gate 400 'Invalid or missing student_id'; L428 modified
    403 'Not authorized'). All static-string bodies, probe-
    resistant. No row counts, no tenant IDs, no accessible-set
    enumeration signal, no DB internals, no table/column leakage.

(e) Pre-existing error-message surfaces enumerated for Followup
    #114 elevation: 10 sites in routes/students.js leaking raw
    error.message via res.status(500).json({ error: error.message })
    at L199, L223, L243, L517, L538, L575, L607, L672, L711, L735.
    Not introduced by this PR; banked for #114 batch-redaction.

(f) Auth flow integrity preserved: requireAuth on both modified
    POSTs (L406, L522); role-gates upstream of helper invocation
    (parent-role at L408-410; ROLES_WHO_CAN_EDIT at L524-526).
    No regression in auth-then-role-then-helper sequence.

    handling. Zero new fetch/axios; zero new require() (resolveAcc-
    essibleTenantIds was pre-existing import at L6); zero new env
    var reads.

(correctness lens); security as "no regression in auth-then-role-

cross-school-within-district student-reference forgery" (privacy-
tightening lens). All three accurate; each identifies a different
aspect of why D-1 is correct. Convergent conclusion via divergent
framings — §J trace template withstood three independent
interpretations without contradiction. Worth elevating: future §J
traces for D-class-style structural rewrites should be drafted to
support multiple reviewer-lens interpretations rather than narrowly
to one. Banked as feedback_d1_triple_framing_three_reviewer_lenses.
