PRIVACY REVIEW (§4B)
====================

Privacy-reviewer dispatched second per documented sequence.
Subagent reviewed PR #123 at e6c6f09 base (post-9897fc0 push).

Verdict: APPROVED 0/0/0/0/0W/5I.

  CRITICAL (0) — none
  HIGH (0) — none
  MEDIUM (0) — none
  LOW (0) — none
  WARN (0) — none
  INFO (5) — corroborative + two fragile-pattern future-iteration
             risks per S78 §E invitation

Five INFO entries:

  1. §1.1 #122 privacy prose ACCURATE against code. "Privacy is
     preserved by access control, not by structural same-tenant
     assertion. Visibility into a parent-student link is gated by
     the caller's user_school_access membership for the student's
     school." Verified at parentLinks.js:56-57 (helper resolves
     accessible tenants + 403s if link's student.tenant_id not in
     caller's set) and L75, L112, L145, L229 (every read/list path
     scopes by s.tenant_id = ANY($N::int[]) where the array is
     the caller's resolveAccessibleTenantIds output). Prose neither
     overstates (does not claim parent.tenant_id is ignored
     entirely) nor understates (explicitly names the gate as
     user_school_access membership for the STUDENT'S school, which
     is the correct anchor since parent_student_links has no
     tenant_id column).

  2. #122 path-(a) resolution correctly interprets PR #109 W-1.
     Original privacy concern was that cross-school link existence
     might leak student identity across school boundaries. Path-(a)
     resolution correctly identifies that the LINK ROW alone is
     not the leak vector — the leak vector would be surfacing the
     linked student to an unauthorized caller, which the
     s.tenant_id = ANY(accessible) clauses on every read path
     prevent. "Accepted by design" is privacy-defensible under the
     post-backfill condition (non-empty user_school_access), which
     is now the standing invariant per M028.

  3. §1.2 forward-looking caveat ACCURATE and privacy-
     CONSERVATIVE. Verified tier1-assessments.js POST / still
     binds [req.user.tenant_id] at L67 (existence-check SELECT)
     and L89 (INSERT). Per-school in-progress semantics under
     future PR-S3-E binding do not introduce any new PII surface
     — the partial index already keys on tenant_id, so when
     PR-S3-E rebinds tenant_id to a per-school resolved target
     via resolveAndBindTargetTenant, the index naturally enforces
     per-school uniqueness without any schema change. Privacy
     posture under future per-school binding is NEUTRAL to
     STRENGTHENED, not weakened: a multi-school district admin
     cannot accidentally collide an in-progress draft from school
     A into school B's namespace, because the resolved target
     tenant is asserted against accessible-tenant membership at
     bind time.

  4. CLAUDE.md §5 pointer accurate from privacy lens. It does not
     assert a privacy claim; it points to the doctrine doc where
     the privacy framing lives. No overstatement risk.

  5. Fragile-pattern framing per S78 §E invitation: TWO concrete
     future-iteration risks surfaced as non-actionable observable.
     Risk (a): Future read path through parent_student_links that
     forgets the resolveAccessibleTenantIds JOIN (e.g., denormalized
     report or admin dashboard that selects directly from parent_
     student_links without s.tenant_id = ANY(accessible) clause)
     would turn cross-school link existence into the leak vector.
     The "permitted by design" doctrine prose could mislead a
     future contributor into inferring link existence is non-
     sensitive in isolation — it is not, only the joined access-
     control gate makes it safe. Risk (b): Future onboarding
     pattern that creates parent users without ensuring student-
     side user_school_access for the gating staff set could drift
     the membership table and silently flip the gate from "deny
     by default" to "allow because no rows." Neither actionable
     in this PR; banked as new Followup #135.

§4B PII-anchor scan: ZERO PII anchors of any §4B-catalogued shape
across all 4 files. All role/scope/binding-shape language abstract;
no specific anchors. Reviewer verification recorded; per
docs/ai-context/4B_GREP_PATTERNS.md discipline, audit-trail prose
cites that §4B was run, not what was checked for.

Migration 019 line-range citation accuracy independently re-
confirmed post-9897fc0 patch: doctrine cites server.js:450-500;
verified at server.js:497-499 the unique partial index idx_tier1_
assessments_one_in_progress_per_tenant is defined within the cited
line range (surrounding tier1_assessments table block spans
454-499, so 450-500 citation is correct).

Triad convergence summary: tenant-iso 0/0/0/0/0W/5I + privacy
0/0/0/0/0W/5I + security SKIPPED. Two-reviewer convergence at
identical slot shape with corroborative-rather-than-fresh-findings
character — see §E below.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS — Session 79
====================================

§A. Markdown-doc forecast methodology gap — physical vs rendered
    file lines (S79)

CC's forecast for docs/ai-context/DISTRICT_STRUCTURE.md was ~+70
midpoint (range +60-80). Actual landed at +45, 25 below midpoint
and 15 below lower bound. Deviation reason surfaced honestly per
feedback_deviation_first_line_diff_summary discipline: CC
forecasted as if visible-wrap chunks were separate file lines,
but actual markdown content uses paragraph-style writing (each
prose paragraph is one physical file line, no manual wrapping).
What reads as ~70+ rendered lines in a markdown viewer is 45
physical lines in the file. Sibling docs in docs/ai-context/
(PRIVACY_REVIEW.md at 359, SECURITY_REVIEW.md at 205) follow the
same long-paragraph convention but CC didn't internalize that
calibration when forecasting. Banking observation: future
markdown-doc forecasts should be in physical file lines, not
rendered/wrapped lines. Sibling-doc convention check should be
the calibration anchor. Methodology-distinct from §I (per-file
shape × file count for per-school binding sec/-class) — applies
only to docs-class PRs.

§B. Vercel project-slug deviation — operator-side investigation
    partial (S79)

CC's gh pr view output on PR #123 surfaced meridan-global-travel/
tiertrak as the Vercel targetUrl, deviating from S78 PRs #121 +
#122 which showed scholar-path-systems/tiertrak as the targetUrl.
Operator-side dashboard verification confirms the actual tiertrak
project lives correctly under scholar-path-systems team and all
PR #123 deploys (two previews on eb0b2b1 + 9897fc0 + production
on e6c6f09) are properly there. Most likely explanation: the
targetUrl CC surfaced was for an ancillary Vercel check (Preview
Comments or similar) routing through a different team URL, not
the deploy itself. Operator confirms meridan-global-travel should
not be associated with tiertrak in any capacity. Open question
for S80 X-list or interstitial: identify what meridan-global-
travel Vercel team is in operator's account (operator likely has
such a team for their other business identity), what if anything
is attached to it that might be observing the tiertrak repo, and
clean up any stale integrations. Sibling observation: Vercel
dashboard surfaced 2FA enablement nudging; smart account hygiene
regardless of the team-slug question. Banking framing: Vercel
project-slug stability between sessions is not a guarantee;
operator-visibility surface worth attending to when CC surfaces
unexpected URL slugs in gh-output.

§C. Reviewer-audits-remote-not-local at redispatch time (S79)

After the Option A patch commit (9897fc0) landed locally, CC
flagged that PR #123 on GitHub still showed the eb0b2b1 commit;
the remote did not yet have 9897fc0. Privacy-reviewer would have
audited the pre-patch state if dispatched without push. Caught by
CC's own discipline before any privacy dispatch. Push landed
between commit and privacy-dispatch, ensuring privacy reviewed
the patched HEAD. Banking observation: any in-PR remediation patch
between triad reviewers requires push before re-dispatch. Distinct
from S77 §K + S78 §F (§M Mode 4) which catch local-vs-staged
disconnect at commit time; this catches local-vs-remote disconnect
at review-dispatch time. New gate worth adding to triad-redispatch
checklist alongside §M Mode 4.

§D. INFO-tier 1c remediation precedent — extends S77 §M from
    WARN-tier (S79)

Tenant-iso reviewer surfaced one non-blocking INFO observable on
PR #123 (Migration 019 location citation) with an explicit
reviewer-suggested patch shape ("Migration 019 — defined in
server.js createTables(), see server.js:450-500"). Two options
considered: Option A (patch in-PR per S77 §M 1c-style remediation)
vs Option B (defer + bank). Option A selected with three
qualifying conditions: reviewer-suggested-patch + INFO-tier +
trivial-shape. Patch applied as commit 9897fc0 (+1 -1 net on
DISTRICT_STRUCTURE.md only). Tenant-iso RE-DISPATCH explicitly
skipped: reviewer pre-cleared the patch shape by suggesting the
exact parenthetical text; verifying "did you paste what I wrote"
is ceremonial overhead, not substantive review. Distinct from S77
§M D-3 1c pattern which was WARN-tier finding requiring
substantive code-level remediation the reviewer hadn't pre-shaped
(re-dispatch substantively justified there; not here). New
methodology refinement: INFO-tier remediation may follow 1c
pattern when qualifying conditions met, with skip-re-dispatch as
the appropriate sequencing. First application this session;
precedent banked for future INFO-tier reviewer-suggested-patch
opportunities.

§E. Two-reviewer triad-convergence at 5I with security skipped
    (S79)

PR #123 received APPROVED 0/0/0/0/0W/5I from both dispatched
reviewers (tenant-iso + privacy), with security-reviewer skipped
per documented scope (zero auth/SQL/external HTTP/PII-handling/
error-message/secret-handling surface). Both reviewers landed at
identical slot shape with substantively corroborative INFO
observables — five each, mostly cross-verification confirmations
rather than fresh findings. Significant pattern: when triad skips
security per documented scope, the two-reviewer convergence at
identical slot shape is the equivalent of S78 §A triple-
convergence (which had all three reviewers at 0/0/0/0/0W/2I).
Both are clean-PR signals; both indicate doctrine is mature
enough that reviewers cycle into verify-and-confirm mode rather
than surfacing fresh issues. Pattern banking: chore-doctrine-only
PRs that legitimately skip security can use two-reviewer
convergence as the clean-PR benchmark rather than requiring full
three-reviewer convergence (which would be ceremonial when
security has no surface). Distinct from S73 PR #112 which skipped
triad entirely (chore close pattern); this is the first chore-
class PR to run partial triad (tenant-iso + privacy only) on
documented prose-accuracy scope.

§F. Fragile-pattern-engagement validated — S78 §E invitation
    produced concrete risks (S79)

S78 §E established the pattern of explicitly inviting forward-
looking-risk framing alongside enumeration in privacy reviewer
dispatches. S79 PR #123 privacy review tested this pattern with
explicit invitation in the dispatch prompt. Privacy reviewer
engaged the invitation substantively: surfaced TWO concrete
future-iteration risks (Risks (a) + (b) on parent-link doctrine
fragility) with specific scenarios (denormalized report bypassing
JOIN; onboarding flow drift) and explicit "non-actionable in this
PR" framing. Outcome: new Followup #135 banked for code/
architecture risk tracking. Banking: S78 §E invitation pattern
validates as productive (produces substantive observables) rather
than ceremonial (would produce vacuous "nothing to add" returns).
Distinction worth preserving: invitation-pattern works for privacy
lens specifically because privacy concerns frequently have schema-
evolution or onboarding-flow latency that pure enumeration misses;
similar invitation in tenant-iso or security may or may not
produce comparable substantive observables (untested at S79).

external approval BEFORE dispatching triad subagent reviews. Catch-

held the line on external-approval-after-triad-completion. Tenant-
iso APPROVED → operator paused → patch applied → push → privacy-
reviewer APPROVED → operator paused → external scholarpathsystems
approval triggered → external approved → operator paused → merge
ceremony executed. Sequence held cleanly without mid-session

held without recurrence in S79; pattern can be considered
internalized rather than persistent risk. Mid-session reminders
no longer needed by default unless specific pre-triad-approval
signal observed.

§I. PR-vs-Followup namespace coincidence (S79)

GitHub PR #123 codified Followup #122 + Followup #123. The
GitHub PR number happens to coincide with one of the followups
the PR resolves. Mildly confusing in future references — "PR
#123" means the GitHub PR; "Followup #123" means the per-school
tier1 in-progress decision. Half-line disambiguation in future
references ("PR #123 (Followup #122/#123 codification)" or
similar) prevents conflation. Non-blocking; observation banking
only. Pattern frequency: low (cumulative GitHub PR count + cumulative
Followup count have grown roughly in parallel through 2026, increasing
collision probability; expect to see this recur every ~50-100 PRs/
followups). No mitigation needed beyond disambiguation discipline.

pattern now triply-confirmed across three consecutive sessions.

§K. Track 2 P4 scope-expansion caught at planning phase (S79)

S78 master-index framing for P4 (operator backfill of user_school_
access for first district_admin): "Single SQL operation; no PR
needed unless we want an audit-trail chore PR around it." Track 2
pickup in S79 surfaced three Cowork-side clarifications: target
school tenant existence, target user existence, target district
mapping. Clarifications revealed scope expansion from single-row
backfill to multi-step first-district-admin onboarding sequence
(create tenant if not exists + create user if not exists + grant
user_school_access + verify audit trigger + redaction-clean
activities.txt entry if audit-trail PR desired). Caught at
planning phase BEFORE any SQL composition — preventive discipline.
Operator-directed defer to S80 focused session per "first real
district_admin in prod deserves a fresh head with credentials in
hand" framing. Banking observation: master-index P-item framing
should be re-validated at session pickup rather than treated as
authoritative scope; sessions between framing and pickup can
materially shift scope (e.g., target onboarding context wasn't
specified in master index, so default "single-row backfill"
framing was operator-implicit at framing time). Sibling to §F
audit-first-pre-edit catch pattern (which catches structural
deviations) and §G external-approval-before-triad catch (which
catches sequencing skip-ahead) — preventive-discipline class.

§L. Substantial-S79-work-without-code-behavior-change pattern
    (S79)

S79 shipped +69 net code/doc lines across 4 files with ZERO
behavioral code change. Pure doctrine codification + thin code-
side pointers + forward-looking caveat. The session's operational
volume — P1 ratification + P2/P3 product decisions + PR #123
audit (with one mid-PR Option A patch) + full two-reviewer triad
+ merge ceremony with §M Mode 4 paste-anchored at six gates +
this close entry — was substantial without producing any new
behavior in prod. Pattern banking: doctrine-codification work is
real substantive output even with zero behavioral delta. The
session-output measurement should account for both behavioral-
delta sec/-class work AND zero-behavioral-delta doctrine-class
work; both consume session time and discipline. Useful framing
for future session pacing: a "behavioral no-op session" can still
be high-output operationally and architecturally. Distinct from
sessions like S65/S66 (Phase 1 design output with no PR merges
at all) — S79 had a merge ceremony and a Render deploy (no-op);
counts as full operational session, not a planning-only one.

§M. §4B violation in close-entry source caught mid-append — slug
    references that should not have been in committed activities.
    txt artifact (S79)

Cowork composed the S79 close entry without adequately scrubbing
historical carry-forward state items inherited from S78 master-
index OUTSTANDING STATE section. Two slug references slipped into
the source file: one tenant slug in a pending-hand-delivery item
referenced from S53 carry-forward, and one tenant slug as a
parenthetical to a tenant_id reference in a revocation-state
preservation item. Both contain real prod tenant slugs that
activities.txt §4B discipline forbids per the per-entry standard.
CC caught both via §4B-grep at pre-stage (before any commit or
PR-open) and held the line. Cowork acknowledged the violation as
a composition error: master-index files in the Cowork-side
workspace folder carry slugs freely (they are not committed and
not subject to §4B), but activities.txt is a committed in-repo
artifact that does NOT permit slugs. Pattern-matching from master-
index OUTSTANDING STATE section without scrubbing produces the
violation surface. Remediation chosen via Option 1 (source-file
fix + re-append) rather than CC-recommended Option 2 (in-tree
remediation in activities.txt only). Rationale: Option 1
preserves source-as-truth discipline (source file and committed
content stay in sync, preventing divergent Cowork-side reference);
preserves verbatim-append discipline (S78 §F bookend
correspondence pattern); has no time-cost advantage to Option 2
since the substitution-verification work is equivalent either way.
Substitutes applied: both slug occurrences → "[tenant]" marker
substitution per CC discipline catalog (preserves line shape).
Tenant_id integer reference preserved as numeric per playbook
explicit §4B list which names slugs but not tenant_id integer
values. Secondary catch on second-pass scrub: the operator-
artifact filename reference in OPEN ITEMS (untracked working file
referenced as Followup #94) itself contained a tenant slug in its
filename component; the entry text already documented "filename
names a customer tenant" and the §4B rule, but the spelling-out
of the filename itself violated the very rule the entry described.
Same "[tenant]" marker substitution applied to the filename
component. Tertiary catch on third-pass scrub via the recursion-
trap regex class (per docs/ai-context/4B_GREP_PATTERNS.md known-
false-positive shape): audit-trail prose in the PRIVACY REVIEW
section enumerated the §4B categories that had been checked for,
inheriting the matching strings into committed content. Per the
discipline statement ("audit-trail prose cites that §4B was run,
never what was checked for"), the enumeration itself is the
violation regardless of semantic intent. Remediation: replaced
the enumeration with abstract "no PII anchors of any §4B-
catalogued shape" phrasing, citing the discipline doc by path.
Three pass-classes of catch in this single composition cycle —
slug-class (free-prose), slug-class (filename-embedded),
recursion-trap (audit-trail enumeration of categories). New
Followup #136 banked for retroactive cleanup of pre-existing
slug references AND recursion-trap shapes in prior session close
entries in activities.txt (S78 + likely earlier); scope TBD per
first investigation pass. Banking framing: master-index
OUTSTANDING STATE sections frequently contain Cowork-side-OK
slugs that must NOT propagate to close-entry composition, AND
PRIVACY REVIEW sections frequently inherit audit-trail-
enumeration patterns that trip the recursion-trap regex.
Composition-checklist refinement: §4B-grep should be run on
source file pre-append, not only on appended diff post-append,
AND multi-pass on the full source covering tenant-slug-class
+ filename-embedded-slug-class + recursion-trap-class regexes
+ explicit doc-discipline cross-reference. New gate at
"compose → §4B-grep on source → remediate → §4B-grep second pass
→ if clean, append → re-grep on appended → stage" rather than
"compose → append → §4B-grep on appended → remediate if found."
Pattern relationship: sibling to §A (markdown-doc forecast
methodology catch — both are pre-edit/pre-stage catches that
prevent downstream drift) and to S78 §L (template-suitability-
review-before-paste — both are pre-content-commitment review
patterns). Standing-practice graduation candidate: if §M holds
across S80 + S81 composition without recurrence of master-index-
pattern-inheritance slip, the §4B-grep-on-source-pre-append step
joins the standing-practice catalog alongside §M Mode 4 paste-
anchoring.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS — Session 80
====================================

§A. Planning-partner-side §4B slip on initial Phase-2 prompt
    (S80)

The Cowork-drafted Phase-2 starter prompt embedded a tenant-slug
literal in the proposed branch name. Caught by the planning
partner at audit-report-review (post-CC-audit, pre-SQL-
composition). Recovery: git branch -m to the redacted form.
Local-only — never pushed, no remote ref cleanup needed. The
branch carried zero commits at rename time so safe-delete-via-
git-branch-d was not relevant here; rename was the appropriate
recovery.

NEW §M PASS-CLASS CANDIDATE: branch-name-embedded slug. Distinct
from S79 §M Pass 2 filename-embedded slug because the branch
name lives in git refs (local + remote when pushed), which is a
different durability surface than filenames-on-disk or filenames-
cited-in-prose. Standing-practice graduation watch: if branch-
naming three-pass discipline holds across S81 + S82 without
recurrence, joins the standing-practice catalog as §M Pass 4.

§B. Find-and-replace global-replace footgun on operator-side
    SQL-placeholder substitution (S80)

The placeholder string used for the password-reset-token
substitution appeared in TWO places in the composed SQL block:
once on the DECLARE-line literal where it expected substitution,
and once inside the preflight 0 IF-check comparison literal
where it intentionally needed to remain a string-match anchor.
Operator performed a global find-and-replace at substitution
time, replacing BOTH occurrences with the live hex token. Result:
preflight 0's check became "if v_reset_token equals <hex token>"
which always matched (because v_reset_token had just been set to
the same hex token on the line above), always triggered ABORT.

Caught at first psql-execution attempt by the preflight itself.
Recovery: ROLLBACK + restore placeholder in the IF-check line +
re-execute clean. The preflight did exactly what preflights are
designed to do — fail loud at the boundary rather than partway
through the transaction — so the catch was clean even though the
cause was a substitution-mechanics slip.

BANKING: SQL-composition prompts producing operator-side psql
blocks should either (a) use DISTINCT placeholder strings in
DECLARE versus IF-check positions, or (b) replace the string-
equality preflight with a length-only or structural-shape
validation (e.g., length(v_reset_token) <> 64) that does not
depend on a placeholder literal matching itself. Option (b) is
the cleaner doctrine because it makes the preflight robust
against the entire class of global-replace mechanics, not just
the specific dual-placeholder shape. Structural lesson for any
future SQL-composition prompt that emits operator-substitutable
placeholders.

§C. Track 2 P4 scope-expansion at pickup, third consecutive
    iteration (S80)

S78 framed P4 as "single SQL operation." S79 §K caught it at
pickup as "multi-step onboarding sequence — defer to S80." S80
surfaced further expansion at pickup: real district and school
names from the operator side, sandbox-preservation strategy
choice (preserve existing sandbox tenants untouched rather than
reuse them), and first-time-in-prod constraint (no precedent
rows in user_school_access or user_school_access_audit). The §K
guardrail held — all three expansions caught at planning phase
before SQL composition began, not partway through composition.

EXTENDS S79 §K to two-cycle confirmation (S79 pickup + S80
pickup); standing-practice graduation candidate. If a third
P-item pickup in S81 or beyond shows the same clarification-
first benefit, promote §K from candidate to catalog item.

§D. Multi-channel §M three-pass §4B-grep discipline (S80)

S79 §M established the three-pass §4B-grep at close-entry
composition. S80 extended the pattern to multi-channel
application:

  (a) SQL-block composition for psql execution — real PII
      appropriate for this channel because the SQL never entered
      git and never appeared in any tracked artifact; the SQL
      block carried real identifiers as required to actually
      execute.

  (b) Verification-output review — real PII appropriate because
      the SELECT outputs existed only in the operator's terminal
      scrollback for the duration of review.

  (c) activities.txt entry composition — real PII NOT
      appropriate because activities.txt is tracked content that
      persists forever. Redacted via abstract count-and-shape
      form: row counts, role literals, column-shape descriptions,
      never the names.

Channel-separation discipline held without recursion-trap slip
— no PII from (a) or (b) leaked into (c). Pattern: §M three-pass
is multi-channel discipline keyed on the persistence surface of
each channel, not a close-entry-only ritual. Banking as the
multi-channel extension of S79 §M.

§E. Planning-partner pre-psql review of operator-prepared SQL
    as productive gate (S80)

Two issues caught at final-review pass before psql execution
began:

  (1) FRONTEND_URL angle-bracket-pasted-literally — operator
      had retained the placeholder delimiters verbatim through
      the substitution step, which would have produced an un-
      clickable set-password URL in the RAISE NOTICE output and
      downstream hand-delivery. Caught pre-psql by the planning
      partner at final-review.

  (2) The §B preflight-0 global-replace footgun — placeholder-
      pattern collision between DECLARE and IF-check positions.
      Caught in-psql by the preflight itself, not at final-
      review (the find-and-replace mechanics are not visible
      from reading the substituted SQL).

BANKING: planning-partner review of operator-prepared SQL
catches substitution-mechanics errors that CC's compose-time
audit cannot anticipate. CC composes with intent and structural
correctness; it does not observe the substitution execution.
The planning-partner role at this gate is structurally distinct
from CC's compose-side audit and produces non-overlapping
catches. Standing-practice item: pre-psql review of operator-
prepared SQL is its own gate.

§F. Cadence-matching as planning-partner discipline (S80)

At the Phase-2-execution transition point, the operator
requested simpler instruction cadence ("slow down and give me
instructions, one at a time, in simple language"). Planning
partner shifted from comprehensive-summary cadence to single-
action-step cadence for the remainder of P2 execution. The
shift was operator-initiated, planning-partner-honored, and
held through to COMMIT.

Pattern: high-stakes operational gates benefit from cadence-
matching to operator capacity, regardless of how mature the
planning-partner-side framing already is. Correct framing does
not make the cadence right; cadence is its own variable.
Standing-practice graduation candidate; if a similar cadence-
shift signal in a later session is honored without prompting,
promote to catalog item.

§G. Product-name change as known stale-reference vector (S80)

Operator clarified mid-session that the product is no longer
named TierTrak; the current FRONTEND_URL points at a different
domain than the historical product name. CLAUDE.md custom
instructions, master-index files, activities.txt headers, and
the Render psql prompt all still surface the legacy product-
name identifier. Cleanup is non-urgent — none of the legacy
references break anything functionally — but the references are
stale.

The Render service slug tiertrak-api is a legacy backend
identifier sitting at a different naming layer from product
name; leaving the service slug as-is is acceptable (renaming a
Render service mid-flight has known operational risk and the
slug is internal-only). Pattern: product-name change as known
stale-reference vector; analogous to S79 §B (Vercel project-slug
deviation) and the Render service slug — backend identifiers
can outlive product naming with no functional cost. NEW FOLLOWUP
#137: stale-product-name reference cleanup pass across tracked-
content surfaces.

§H. First-row-audit_id as empty-table-confirmation signal (S80)

user_school_access_audit's first-ever rows landed as audit_id=1
and audit_id=2 in S80, confirming the carry-forward observation
from S76 §K (reconfirmed S77 / S78 / S79) that the table was
empty in prod. Sequential identifiers starting at 1 are a
verification-proxy for "no prior rows existed" — if the table
had carried any history (failed migration attempts, out-of-band
inserts, etc.), the BIGSERIAL would have surfaced a higher
starting audit_id.

BANKING: serial-id-of-first-row as a verification-proxy for
table-emptiness in audit and log tables that use SERIAL or
BIGSERIAL identifiers. Useful operator-side verification pattern
at first-write moments. Limitation: the proxy is informational
only — it does not survive any operator-side sequence reset, so
the proxy is strongest when paired with a read of the table's
row count at the same gate.

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS — Session 81
====================================

§A. First UI work since S60-S62 baseline shipped cleanly (S81)

PR #126 is the first user-facing UI PR since the S60-S62
baseline. UI-class discipline that had not been exercised in
many sessions surfaced cleanly without friction: lint scoping to
new files (separate concern from pre-existing FE lint hits),
inline-vs-extracted view component decision (extracted to
frontend/src/views/ following the existing ResourcesView.jsx
precedent), Tailwind utility convention preservation (no new
design tokens, matched existing indigo/slate palette + gradient
summary card pattern from DashboardView).

The UI-class blast-radius audit pre-implementation surfaced one
structural Q (Admin tab visibility for district_admin) that drove
a meaningful product decision (hide rather than show-with-banner)
based on reading inline view dispatch + role gates across the
7146-line App.jsx. Without the audit, the structural silent-
misrepresentation footgun would have shipped.

BANKING: UI-class work after long-baseline-gaps benefits from
explicit pre-implementation blast-radius audits even when the
change appears small. The Admin-tab visibility decision could
not have been made correctly from PR-diff inspection alone; it
required structural read of the existing FE state-machine.
Standing-practice candidate: any UI PR after a >10-session UI
baseline gap should include an explicit blast-radius audit
section in the planning pass.

§B. §M Pass 3 §H-correlation-window catch on PR body composition
    — first real test of multi-channel §D extension (S81)

The multi-channel §D extension adopted at S81 P1 (PR bodies
enter the tracked-content channel set) fired on its first real
test: §M Pass 3 catch on PR #126's Verification section, where
the original draft embedded a count-implying ordinal + session-
tag pair against a singleton row class during the gradient
window established by S80 §H. Redacted to a count-neutral
ordinal-less form via gh pr edit before triad dispatch. Pass 1
and Pass 2 spot-checks confirmed no regression on the edit.

Caught at three-pass-pre-dispatch gate, not after triad ran.
The triad saw the redacted body; the historical record of the
unredacted phrase exists only in CC's transient compose
scrollback, not in any tracked-content channel.

BANKING: multi-channel §D extension validated — PR-body-as-
channel discipline is load-bearing during the §H low-id
correlation-richness window. Pattern: any time CC composes a PR
body that references "the first X user" or "the only Y in prod"
or similar count-revealing ordinals during a known gradient
window, run §M Pass 3 before any external surface (triad
dispatch, gh pr create publish). The catch IS the discipline
working as designed. The structural basis for the catch is S80
§H; the channel extension makes the same discipline applicable
to PR bodies as well as activities.txt.

§C. Reviewer triad convergent-grep verification — first real
    application post-banking (S81)

Triad dispatch on PR #126 returned 3-of-3 APPROVED with zero
CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM. All three reviewers independently
exercised the §5 dual-path checklist (helper consumption, per-
CTE filter, outer district predicate, empty-array short-circuit)
and reached the same conclusion. Per memory feedback_convergent_
grep_verification, convergent grep across reviewers IS the
verification — stronger than any single pass.

The §5 layered defense for read-aggregate surfaces was named
explicitly by the tenant-isolation-auditor as the strongest
tenant-isolation pattern currently in the codebase for a read-
aggregate surface. This becomes a benchmark shape for future
read-aggregate route work.

BANKING: when designing read-aggregate routes that cross §5
surface, the four-layer pattern (helper-resolved accessible
set + per-CTE ANY filter + outer scope predicate + empty-array
short-circuit) is the canonical shape. Subsequent read-aggregate
PRs should mirror this shape rather than reinvent. Recommend
referencing routes/districtDashboard.js as the worked example
in future related-surface planning prompts.

§D. Privacy-reviewer caught FE arg-order bug outside strict §4B
    scope (S81)

PR #126's privacy reviewer surfaced L-2 (logError arg-order swap
at the FE view call site) — a correctness issue, not a §4B PII
concern. The reviewer's lens flagged it because in some
hypothetical worse case the swapped order could log a PII-
bearing arg in the wrong position; this case had no PII, but
the reviewer surfaced the call-shape bug regardless. Fixed in
one-line follow-on commit before merge.

BANKING: the privacy-reviewer lens picks up adjacent correctness
issues on FE code paths even when the strict §4B test passes.
Useful complement to lint and to the security-reviewer's auth-
correctness lens. Not redundant with either. Continue
dispatching privacy-reviewer on FE-touching PRs even when no
PII surface is directly touched. This is a third worked example
of the §F precedent established at S79 (privacy-lens fragile-
pattern engagement validated): privacy-lens findings extend
beyond strict §4B scope.

§E. Reviewer-pre-approved L-2 fix as scope-bounded extension of
    APPROVED state (S81)

Privacy-reviewer's L-2 finding explicitly noted "pre-approved
this exact change in the privacy report" enabling a one-line
surgical fix without re-dispatching the triad. CC executed the
sequence: surgical fix, three-pass §M-grep on the commit message
before push, paste-anchored diff for operator review, then
commit + push. The §M three-pass on the commit message returned
clean (no PII in commit body either).

BANKING: when a reviewer report explicitly pre-approves an exact
fix, the fix can proceed on the same branch without re-dispatch
IF (a) the fix is exactly what was pre-approved, (b) §M
discipline runs on the fix's own commit message and diff, (c)
operator sees the diff before commit, and (d) the operator
greenlights. The reviewer's pre-approval acts as a scope-bounded
scope-extension of their original APPROVED state, scoped to the
exact change they pre-approved and nothing else.

§F. Background-Bash + Monitor mechanism failure on long-poll-
    with-final-emit pattern (S81)

The 5-min 401-spike watch was first attempted as Monitor with
`render logs --tail`, which failed (CLI does not actually stream
in non-interactive mode despite the --tail flag existing). Re-
attempted as a background-Bash poll loop wrapped in a Monitor
task. Both background tasks (the watch loop + an earlier deploy-
status poll) failed to surface their final-emit notifications.
Runtime reported both as "running" 10+ minutes past their
expected exit (Monitor timeout, until-loop termination). Output
files were 0 bytes on disk. Successfully killed via TaskStop.

Cause hypothesis (not load-bearing — verification was done out-
of-band): bash subprocesses spawning short-lived CLI child
processes (render logs, render deploys get) hold stdout open in
a way that prevents the runtime's task-status reporting from
advancing, or the final printf in each script never flushed
before the bash shell terminated.

The mitigation that worked: one-shot out-of-band verification
queries (render logs filtered to the watch window with --start
and --end timestamps) provided the authoritative all-clear
independent of the Monitor task state.

BANKING: for long-watch-with-final-emit patterns, plan to verify
out-of-band. Monitor's terminal emit cannot be trusted to fire
when the watch script spawns CLI subprocesses. Mode-4 §M paste-
anchoring discipline extends: at the end of any background-
watch window, run an out-of-band verification query and treat
that as the authoritative paste, not the Monitor notification.
The Monitor remains useful for in-window event surfacing (a
notification per matching line during the window) but is not
load-bearing for the final all-clear gate.

§G. §M Pass 3 regex generalization — "row" form slipped through
    initial S81 ruleset (S81)

The §M Pass 3 grep regex used during in-session checks was tuned
to the specific phrase shape from the original §B catch ("first
.* district_admin user"). During TASK SUMMARY composition for
this close entry, an analogous "row" form ("first district_admin
row landing in prod") slipped past the same regex because "row"
was not enumerated alongside "user". Caught by operator at pre-
commit diff review, not by CC's three-pass automation.

The structural class is the same: count-revealing ordinal +
singleton-row referent during the §H gradient window. The regex
was tuned to the noun phrase that happened to surface first,
missing the noun-class generalization.

BANKING: §M Pass 3 regex going forward should generalize the
referent noun:

  (first|only|sole|the .* in prod) .* (user|row|grant|entry|
  access|record|tenant|district|backfill)

…rather than tuning to whatever specific noun first appeared.
Pattern: regex tuned to historical catch shape is a known anti-
pattern; the discipline is shape-class, not token-match. Carry-
forward action for S82 P1: ratify this generalized regex shape
and add to the standing §M-grep toolkit.

Also banks: operator-side diff review is a non-redundant
verification gate even when CC's automated three-pass returns
clean. The operator caught what the regex missed; the gate
catches by independent lens. Parallel to S80 §E (planning-
partner pre-psql SQL review as productive gate) — same
structural lesson at a different gate.

         INSERT; new district_admin row would fail the dual-

DISCIPLINE OBSERVATIONS — Session 83
====================================

§A. Internal-triad finds what external-review missed —
    redundancy validation under stale-approval cycling (S83)

PR #129 received external APPROVED at commit 79e29d7. The
internal triad re-dispatch at the same commit surfaced
3 HIGH + 3 MEDIUM findings external review had not caught.
None were §4B-strict; most were security / tenant-iso
findings (role-rank escalation, rate-limit attachment,
cross-tenant email-probe, log redaction, presence validation,
district_id binding). The internal triad lens is NOT
redundant with external review — the two lenses exercise
different verification axes.

The triad-cycle shape played out as: initial work → external
APPROVED → internal triad finds gaps → fix commits →
internal triad re-dispatch clean → fresh external re-review
APPROVED → merge. Branch protection's stale-approval
auto-dismiss rule made the cycle mechanically enforced —
when fix commits 8/9/10 landed, the prior external approval
was auto-dismissed and fresh re-review was required before
the merge button re-enabled.

BANKING: internal triad dispatch as a standing-practice gate
AFTER external APPROVED, not before. The external approval
is the entry condition; the triad lens is the second-pass
net that catches what external review misses on §4B / §5
surfaces. Two-pass discipline is load-bearing for security /
tenant-iso / privacy-sensitive PRs. Parallel to
feedback_convergent_grep_verification (S79 §C / S81 §C) —
but applied across-lens rather than across-reviewer-within-
lens.

§B. Inline-closure pattern — fold pre-banked sec/* PR into
    fix-scope expansion when the fix surfaces the same code
    (S83)

sec/staff-log-redaction was pre-existing in the X-list as a
separate sec/* PR. During PR #129's fix planning for
MEDIUM-6 (PG error.detail leak from 500-catch on the two
POST surfaces), the operator surfaced that the fix scope
already touched the exact two error-body sinks
sec/staff-log-redaction would have addressed. Folded inline
as part of commit 10e rather than landing as a separate PR.

The fold preserved scope discipline: only the two POST 500-
catch sinks were redacted; the 9 remaining non-POST sinks
stayed banked under sec/error-redaction-sweep. The fold did
not silently expand the redaction across the routers — only
the lines the PR #129 fix-scope actually touched.

BANKING: pre-banked sec/* PRs that overlap fix-scope on an
in-flight PR can be folded inline IF (a) the fold preserves
scope discipline (only the lines the in-flight fix actually
touches), (b) the fold is operator-sanctioned at planning
time (not silently expanded by CC), and (c) the inline
closure is explicitly cited in the activities entry so the
followup-list does not show a phantom open item. Parallel
to S81 §E (reviewer-pre-approved scope-bounded fix) — same
discipline at a different gate (operator-side instead of
reviewer-side).

§C. Role-token FP catalogue under wide-pass §M (S83)

The wide-pass §M regex includes "district" as a bare-word
alternation. This entry exercises role-token shapes
(district_admin, district_id, district_tech_admin) and
generic scope references ("the district", "district scope")
extensively. Per the inline per-pattern false-positive notes
in docs/ai-context/4B_GREP_PATTERNS.md, role-token and
column-name shapes are FP under wide-pass — they are schema
/ API identifiers, not customer identifiers. Same catalogue
applies to
"school_admin", "user_school_access", "tenant_id",
"target_tenant_id" — none are tenant slugs.

The "zero matches" criterion documented in the
landing-the-plane skill should be read as "zero unflagged
matches after FP-pruning". The skill's strict reading would
make any audit-trail entry that names role tokens
mechanically uncomposable; the practical reading is FP-prune
with explicit citation of the FP catalogue in the entry's
PRIVACY REVIEW section.

BANKING: when audit-trail prose exercises role-token shapes
that collide with §M wide-pass alternation literals, cite
the FP catalogue in the PRIVACY REVIEW section explicitly.
The pattern is not a §4B violation but the discipline of
naming the FP keeps future reviewers from re-discovering
the same false positive every session. Standing-practice
candidate.

Pass 1c closure note (this session): operator-supplied
tenant-slug list (.4b-tenant-slugs.local) deferred at
compose time; operator-confirmed non-slug for the `GR_`
prefix in the docs/references/GR_SampleClass.pdf citation
at pre-push gate. Pre-push diff review is the standing
surface for Pass 1c when .4b-tenant-slugs.local is not
present in the repo.

Banked from S81 / S80 / S79 / earlier (carry-forward, S83
extensions)
============================================================

- S81 §A (UI-class blast-radius audit standing-practice
  candidate) — NOT EXERCISED in S83 (no new UI primitives;
  AddStaffModal extension reused existing patterns).

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
------------------------------------------------------------------------

The role-branched query preserves WHERE s.tenant_id = $1 on BOTH
branches. The new INNER JOIN intervention_assignments cannot introduce
a cross-tenant pathway because intervention_assignments has no
tenant_id column but inherits tenant scope transitively via
student_intervention_id → student_interventions → students → tenant_id.
A foreign-tenant ia row pointing at the calling user's id but at an SI
in another tenant is structurally rejected by the WHERE s.tenant_id =
$1 clause. Defense-in-depth confirmed by tenant-isolation-auditor.

requireTenantStaffAccess middleware unchanged; the path :tenantId is
still validated against resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) per §5
dual-path doctrine before the handler runs.

Pre-existing schema gap surfaced: intervention_assignments lacks the
M021-style composite-FK tenant binding. Read paths under review are
safe regardless because they bind tenant via students. Banked for a
separate future migration.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Both new query branches retain WHERE sd.tenant_id = $1 and the
composite sd→s tenant binding (s.id = sd.student_id AND s.tenant_id
= sd.tenant_id). The new INNER JOINs to student_interventions and
intervention_assignments cannot introduce a cross-tenant pathway:
both tables lack their own tenant_id column (S87 schema lesson #7)
and inherit tenant scope transitively via the (s.id, s.tenant_id)
anchor — a foreign-tenant si or ia row pointing at the calling
user's id but at a student in another tenant is structurally rejected
by the WHERE sd.tenant_id = $1 clause. Confirmed by
tenant-isolation-auditor on both passes (APPROVED, zero findings
across CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW on each pass).

requireExpiringListAccess middleware unchanged; path :tenantId still
validated against resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) per §5 dual-
path doctrine before the handler runs.

The middleware change in ef1f4bb is a single-row PK lookup with one
extra column projected; it does not change any tenant-scoping clause
anywhere. Tenant boundary remains owned by
resolveAccessibleTenantIds and the upstream
requireExpiringListAccess check; school_wide_access is a per-user
boolean flag (not a tenant identifier) that gates within-tenant
branch selection only.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
------------------------------------------------------------------------

PII fields touched: student_documents.* projection (including IEP /
504 Plan / Medical Record categories — §4B "uploaded documents
containing student information"). These were already returned by the
pre-fix handler; the PR strictly NARROWS which rows a non-elevated
staff caller sees. No new PII column is introduced on either branch.
Privacy-reviewer second-pass framed this as "a strictly privacy-
positive change with no privacy regression possible" — fewer rows
leave the server post-fix, never more.

users.school_wide_access added to the requireAuth users SELECT in
ef1f4bb. The field is a per-user boolean permission flag, not
student/staff PII per privacy-reviewer second-pass verdict. Verified
by grep that no consumer in routes/ or middleware/ serializes
req.user whole-object to a response body or log line; the column is
read by exactly three call sites (routes/students.js,
routes/weeklyProgress.js, routes/studentDocuments.js — now all three
widgets), all of which use it only as a boolean for branch
selection.

Logging audit: zero new console.* calls introduced; pre-existing
error-path console.error logs error.message only with no PII
interpolation. The error response body is a static 'Server error'
string. No PII reaches logs or response error bodies.

Cross-tenant risk: none identified. See TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW.

Pre-existing sd.* over-select in both branches of the expiring-docs
query is sibling to followups #71 / #78. Pre-existing in main; not
introduced by this PR. Banked for a future data-minimization sweep
PR.

§4B self-scan on this entry: narrow and wide grep passes per
docs/ai-context/4B_GREP_PATTERNS.md run against the added-lines diff
before staging.

Vestigial students.teacher_id column — confirmed during S88 audit as
schema with no live populate path (zero FE writes, zero CSV-import
writes, zero seed-script writes; backend POST/PUT students
destructure the field but FE never sends it) and no consulted read
predicate anywhere (it appears only as LEFT JOIN to users for a
display-only teacher_name alias that the FE never reads). Banked as
a new followup candidate: either wire up homeroom-teacher visibility
as a real third path (FE form field + CSV importer column + backfill
+ NOT NULL + index migration) OR drop the vestigial column. Either
way, out of scope for this PR.

Pre-existing secondary_area POST/PUT asymmetry on the student
create/edit flow — FE-side, not BE. Both backend handlers in
routes/students.js include secondary_area in their SQL (POST at
:529-534 destructures and binds to $8 of the INSERT; PUT at
:549-568 binds to $8 of the UPDATE). The asymmetry lives on the
frontend: the POST body at frontend/src/App.jsx:1594-1602 omits
secondary_area, so newly-created students always land with
secondary_area=NULL; the PUT body at frontend/src/App.jsx:1622-1628
correctly includes it. Net effect: secondary_area can only be
populated via a post-create edit, never on initial create. Surfaced
as a side-finding during the S88 students.teacher_id vestigial-
column audit; out of scope for this PR. Data-completeness defect,
not a §4B/§5 issue. Banked as a new followup candidate.

X1 cleanup — closed the school_wide_access role-drift first banked in
S87 (during PR #136 lifecycle) and strengthened to triply convergent
in S88 (during PR #138 lifecycle). The same role-branched predicate
ships at three read sites (routes/students.js,
routes/weeklyProgress.js, routes/studentDocuments.js) and the
underlying allowlist literal was duplicated across the POST recalc,
PUT recalc, server bootstrap UPDATE, and two seed scripts — with
three of those copies drifted out of sync with the canonical 5-role
POST allowlist. PR #140 collapses all six write/read sites plus the
seed paths onto a single shared constant (constants/roles.js
ELEVATED_ROLES) in four commits.

Locked decisions for this PR (per S88 / S89 planning):
  - Allowlist canonicalization only. PUT recalc row-level healing on
    name-only edits is DEFERRED (banked followup); COALESCE shape
    preserved.
  - server-boot DDL at server.js:262 patched here; Followup #117
    (retire server-boot DDL entirely) supersedes later.
  - Seed scripts VALIDATE roster school_wide_access against
    ELEVATED_ROLES (raise on mismatch); do not silently coerce.
  - Invariant CI check / admin observability is OUT OF SCOPE for
    this PR; banked as a sibling chore PR.

Four-commit structure on the work branch:
  c678da5 chore(roles): extract ELEVATED_ROLES constant
  0e46219 fix(rbac): apply ELEVATED_ROLES allowlist to tenant-wide read predicates
  6416020 fix(rbac): close ELEVATED_ROLES drift in PUT recalc and server bootstrap
  a4278b3 chore(seed): validate roster school_wide_access against ELEVATED_ROLES

Total scope vs main: 9 files, +120/-19 per git diff --stat. Branch
sec/school-wide-access-role-drift-cleanup pushed to origin and PR
#140 opened against main with scholapathsystems requested as
reviewer; PR is OPEN / REVIEW_REQUIRED / mergeStateStatus=BLOCKED /
mergeable=MERGEABLE at session-close.

Canonical-intent framing called out in PR #140's body: read-predicate
widening at the three read sites is "users will see MORE" in some
configurations — a user whose role is in the canonical 5-role
allowlist but whose users.school_wide_access row value is FALSE
today will, post-fix, enter the tenant-wide read branch via the
role arm. Per the POST recalc canonical at
routes/staffManagement.js:140, those roles are CANONICALLY meant to
have tenant-wide access; the FALSE row values are drift from the
bootstrap UPDATE allowlist gap (closed in commit 6416020) and from
potential prior PUT-recalc shape gaps. Restoration of canonical
intent, not new access grant. Predicate is always evaluated AFTER
tenant scope is fixed, so no cross-tenant exposure is introduced.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
------------------------------------------------------------------------

tenant-isolation-auditor verdict: APPROVED. 0 CRITICAL, 0 WARN, 5
INFO. Audited 6 queries (5 tenant-scoped, 1 intentionally
cross-tenant — the bootstrap UPDATE).

All three read predicates execute downstream of tenant-membership
verification: routes/students.js calls resolveAccessibleTenantIds
directly at :128-131 and 403s on miss before the predicate runs;
routes/weeklyProgress.js sits behind requireTenantStaffAccess
middleware; routes/studentDocuments.js sits behind
requireExpiringListAccess middleware. Both middlewares themselves
consume resolveAccessibleTenantIds per §5 dual-path doctrine.
Widening the role arm controls the WITHIN-tenant SQL shape
(everyone-in-tenant vs only-my-assignments); it does not relax
the cross-tenant gate. Each query retains WHERE s.tenant_id = $1
/ WHERE sd.tenant_id = $1 with the path-derived tenant value.

The bootstrap UPDATE at server.js:262 is INTENTIONALLY
cross-tenant — it back-fills a non-PII boolean (school_wide_access)
across every user in every tenant whose role is in ELEVATED_ROLES.
Operates only on a permission column; does not read or write
student/intervention/document data; does not surface any
tenant-scoped row to a request session. Idempotent. Auditor
explicitly classified this as correct cross-tenant, not a WARN.

§5 helper-consumed-not-inline doctrine honored: zero new code in
the diff branches on users.district_id. The five consumer sites
that need accessible-tenant resolution all consume
resolveAccessibleTenantIds (directly or transitively via
require* middleware).

string (district_tech_admin) reachable via the same path. WARN-2:

res.status(500).json({ error: error.message }) — raw pg error
text leakage. Fix is one focused PR: add requireAuth + role-rank

'Server error' (match POST :162). Full reviewer trio + §2A.
Cluster sibling of Followup #116.

PUT recalc row-level healing on name-only edits — DEFERRED per
S89 locked decision. routes/staffManagement.js:178 COALESCE
preserves the existing row value when role is absent from the
request; if that existing value is itself drifted (legacy row
born before this PR's bootstrap fix), the name-only PUT does not
heal it. Healing would require an explicit
schoolWideAccess = ELEVATED_ROLES.includes(currentRoleFromRow)
recompute on every PUT regardless of body content, which is
broader scope than this PR's "allowlist canonicalization only"
remit. Banked as separate follow-up.

Invariant CI check / admin observability — banked sibling chore
PR. The check is a SELECT against users WHERE school_wide_access
= TRUE AND role NOT IN (ELEVATED_ROLES list), expected to return
zero rows in steady state; non-zero indicates drift to
investigate. Plus a parallel SELECT WHERE school_wide_access =
FALSE AND role IN (ELEVATED_ROLES). Surfaces drift before users
notice. Out of scope for the drift-closure PR itself; deserves a
focused chore PR with its own reviewer pass.

Closed the two WARN findings from PR #140's security-reviewer pass on
routes/staffManagement.js (banked at S89 close as the natural next
priority): WARN-1 the PUT /:id handler was mounted without requireAuth
and without any role gate, enabling an unauthenticated caller to
promote any user to district_admin or district_tech_admin; WARN-2 the
same handler's catch returned res.status(500).json({ error:
error.message }) — raw pg error text leakage to the FE. A pre-flight
read-only audit of the file surfaced a third gap not called out by PR
#140's reviewer: the same handler's UPDATE ran with no §5
tenant-scoping clause, a textbook IDOR enabling cross-tenant writes
on the users table (and, via the RETURNING clause, cross-tenant PII
reads). Operator decision: close all three in one PR; keep
sec/staff-list-require-auth on the unauthed GET /:tenantId banked as
a separate branch.

Three-commit structure on the work branch sec/staffmanagement-put-
hardening:

INFO. Audited 2 queries in the PUT handler (1 SELECT-gate at :203,

non-scoped, 0 admin-cross-tenant).

§5 dual-path access contract honored: resolveAccessibleTenantIds is
*consumed* at routes/staffManagement.js:213, never inlined. Verified
the helper at middleware/resolveAccessibleTenantIds.js:62-71 branches
on user.district_id (legacy single-tenant returns [tenant_id]; district
user returns user_school_access membership filtered by both user_id
AND district_id). A district_admin in district A cannot reach a user
in district B (user_school_access rows are district-scoped); a
school_admin scoped to school X cannot mutate a user in school Y
within the same district (helper returns only the school_admin's
explicit M028 grants).

UPDATE WHERE clause stays at id = $4 (no AND tenant_id = ANY(...)
defense-in-depth). Auditor disposition: INFO not WARN, matching the
security-reviewer's independently-raised convergent finding. The
SELECT-then-UPDATE TOCTOU is real but unweaponizable in the current
code — no application path mutates users.tenant_id post-creation, so
the race window has no real attack surface. Both reviewers
recommended chore/staff-put-defense-in-depth-tenant-clause as a
banked follow-up (low priority).

Cross-tenant read/write paths through this PUT: closed. The previous
handler accepted req.params.id and ran UPDATE without any tenant
predicate — IDOR. Post-PR the four-layer authz (caller-role,
self-PUT, role-rank, §5 scope) + parent guard + probe-resistant 404
collapse leaves no cross-tenant exposure surface.

Probe-resistance: missing-row (line 207), parent-row (line 210), and
out-of-scope-row (line 214) all collapse to the same 404 'Not found'
body. Tenant-membership signal is hidden — a caller cannot
distinguish "doesn't exist" from "exists in another tenant" from
"exists but is a parent."

0 new tables; 0 schema changes; 0 new foreign keys; 0 composite-FK
audit requirements. No JOIN traversal in the changed queries.

    INTERVENTION_MANAGER_ROLES in constants/roles.js (six non-parent
    staff roles). Chosen via FE consumer-scope grep on staffList —
    locking tighter to Object.keys(CREATE_STAFF_RULES) (two-role
    write-side mirror) or to ELEVATED_ROLES (five-role mirror) would
    silently 403 teacher/counselor/interventionist callers on their
    login-time loadStaffList fetch and break the AssignmentManager
    workflow. Read-access broader than write-access by design — read
    of staff PII to peer staff is FERPA-covered ("school official"
    doctrine) while write is admin-only. Constant named by semantic
    intent (mirrors FE canManageInterventions predicate at
    frontend/src/App.jsx:411) per S89 centralization lesson.

    for out-of-scope :tenantId values. 404 'Not found' on miss for
    probe-resistance, matching PUT :226-229.

    user-with-no-grants; collapses to same 404 as out-of-scope probe.

    in Followup #116" in commit 3's already-amending edit (not a
    separate cleanup commit) — operator agreement during commit-2
    review.

    it, don't fold it in" discipline.

line 97 with the SELECT structurally unreachable. No PII query runs
for out-of-scope :tenantId values. Same 404 envelope as a non-
existent-tenant probe — tenant-ID enumeration cannot distinguish
"tenant doesn't exist" from "tenant exists but I have no access."

Empty-array contract collapses to same 404 with no special-casing:
Array.prototype.includes on [] returns false for every input. A
district user with no user_school_access grants yet (legitimate
pre-onboarding state per helper docstring) sees the same 404 as any
out-of-scope tenant — no information disclosure differentiates the
two failure modes.

No defense-in-depth AND tenant_id = ANY($2::int[]) added to the
SELECT WHERE. Acceptable for this read-shape: single SELECT (no
SELECT-then-mutation TOCTOU as in PUT), zero awaits interleaved
between the §5 gate at line 95 and the pool.query at line 100, local
const tenantId cannot mutate. Auditor disposition: INFO (not WARN).

      that differs from the canonical-spelling reading (typo-shaped
      vs. natural-spelling), the typo-shaped form is operational

      account. Surface, do not silently substitute. Banked for the

BRANCH (this entry): chore/activities-log-session-92
SESSION WORK PR: #146 (sec/users-target-id-require-auth, awaiting external review)
DISPOSITION: pr-opened

------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Closed 2 of the 7 unauthed routes on routes/users.js under Followup
#116 — GET /api/users/:id and PUT /api/users/:id. Pre-PR, both routes
mounted with no auth middleware, no caller-role gate, no §5 tenant
scope check on the URL :id parameter, and raw error.message body leak
in their catch blocks. The PUT specifically allowed unauthenticated
UPDATE on the users table including the role column — the
cross-tenant role-escalation primitive across tenants.

Phase 1 read-only audit shape: full route-table audit on routes/users.js
(9 routes total, 7 unauthed remaining post-PR-#129-and-#115) + FE
consumer-scope grep on each unauthed route. Audit confirmed the S91-
banked working assumption (6 GET + 1 PUT remain) and surfaced two
significant findings that drove the planning-time split decision:
(a) 6 of 7 unauthed routes are FE-dead — only /parents has live FE
callsites; (b) PUT /:id is the highest-severity unauthed route in the
codebase (unauthed UPDATE with role-mutation surface = cross-tenant
role-escalation primitive). Operator-approved split: PR B (this
session) handles the 2 routes using the PR #142 SELECT-target-then-
check pattern. PR A (banked as sec/users-tenant-reads-require-auth)
handles the remaining 5 FE-dead GET routes using the PR #144
URL-or-query-string-IS-scope-target pattern. PR B chosen first
because of PUT /:id severity.

Three-commit structure on the work branch sec/users-target-id-require-
auth (PR #146):
  8325690 sec(users-target): add requireAuth + caller-role gate
                              + int validation
  3144f25 sec(users-target): scope target row to caller's accessible-
                              tenant set (§5)
  a255d80 sec(users-target): redact catch block — generic body
                              + structured log

Headline security delta — all four pre-PR exposures CLOSED:
  - Cross-tenant read oracle on GET /:id (unauthed read of arbitrary
    user PII by ID) — closed by commit 1's auth + commit 2's §5
  - Cross-tenant write oracle on PUT /:id (unauthed UPDATE on the
    users table across tenants) — closed by commit 1's auth + commit
    2's §5
  - Role-escalation primitive on PUT /:id (mutate own role or
    another admin's role) — closed by commit 2's self-PUT block +
    role-rank gate
  - Catch-block body leak on both routes ({ error: error.message }
    returning raw pg error text) — closed by commit 3's redacted
    body + structured error.code-only log

Locked decisions for the work PR:
  - Caller-role allowlist for both routes: Object.keys(CREATE_USER_
    RULES) = ['district_admin', 'school_admin']. Semantic consistency
    with POST and DELETE on the same file. FE-dead means zero UX
    regression risk from the tight allowlist.
  - PR #142's parent-row 404 block deliberately omitted because
    routes/users.js IS the parent-edit surface (staffManagement.js
    documents this at its own L249). Parents legitimately have a
    tenant_id and pass through the §5 scope check like any other
    user role.
  - Approach A (two-SELECT pre-check) for GET /:id §5 — slim
    SELECT tenant_id first → §5 check → full SELECT only if scope
    OK. Mirrors PR #142 byte-for-byte rather than the alternative
    one-SELECT-post-check shape.
  - 3-commit cadence preserved (not split to 4) — commit 2 came in
    at +52/-9, under the 60-line split threshold.

Total work-PR scope vs origin/main: 1 file, +78/-15 per git diff
--stat. Reviewer-trio dispatched single-pass, parallel at operator
direction. Zero CRITICAL across all three reports. PR #146 opened
against main with scholapathsystems requested as reviewer; awaiting
formal §2A approval. reviewDecision: REVIEW_REQUIRED.
mergeStateStatus: BLOCKED.

Three workflow lessons surfaced mid-session, all banked rather than
papered over:
(a) Verbatim-disclosure quotes in PR bodies must be truly verbatim
    — no paraphrasing, no trimming, and (extension caught this
    session) no additions either. The §2A external reviewer reads
    the PR body, not the underlying reviewer reports; trimmed or
    embellished content means they make their merge-or-block call
    without the source material's full framing. Banked as
    feedback_verbatim_quotes_must_be_truly_verbatim with the
    no-additions extension explicit. The specific catch this session
    was a parenthetical added to the W-1 Recommendation paragraph
    that was not in the original privacy-reviewer report —
    informationally a no-op but still paraphrase-shaped.
(b) Count-things-by-bytes-not-by-mental-model: the whitespace
    deviation count drifted 5 → 6 → 7 across three operator
    corrections during this PR. The 5-count missed a blank-line
    cleanup; the 6-count missed one role-validity template edit.
    Mental-model counting fails on byte-level deviation disclosures
    — count by bytes (the actual + and - lines in the diff), not
    by category.
(c) Claim-without-write is the same shape as see-tool-output-above
    paste discipline (S91): in S91 close I claimed to have "banked"
    a memory file via mention in the response body but had not
    actually written the file. Caught this session when operator
    asked to update it and the file did not exist. Banking-via-
    mention is paraphrase of actual banking; the file write is the
    load-bearing artifact.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FILES TOUCHED
------------------------------------------------------------------------

routes/users.js — +78/-15 across three commits on the work branch
(PR #146). (i) GET /:id handler signature (formerly
router.get('/:id', async ...)) now mounts requireAuth per-handler;
handler header comment block rebuilt across three commits to
enumerate the four-clause gate stack (requireAuth + caller-role +
positive-int :id + §5 SELECT-target check). (ii) Inside the GET try:
caller-role gate (Object.keys(CREATE_USER_RULES).includes → 403);
positive-int validation on :id (parseInt + Number.isInteger + > 0 +
≤ INT32 max → 400); slim §5 SELECT (SELECT tenant_id FROM users
WHERE id = $1) → resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) consumed (not
inlined) → 404 'Not found' on missing or out-of-scope; then existing
full SELECT proceeds. Catch redacted to console.error('[users:get]
error code:', error.code) + res.status(500).json({ error: 'Server
error' }). (iii) PUT /:id handler signature now mounts requireAuth
per-handler; header comment rebuilt to enumerate the seven-clause
gate stack (requireAuth + caller-role + positive-int :id + self-PUT
+ role-validity + role-rank + §5). Inside the PUT try: same auth +
caller-role + positive-int gates as GET; self-PUT block (id ===
req.user.id → 403); existing role-validity (VALID_ROLES → 400)
preserved; new role-rank gate (CREATE_USER_RULES[caller].includes
→ 403, fires only when role is being mutated); slim §5 SELECT → 404;
then UPDATE proceeds. Catch redacted same shape (with [users:put]
prefix). 7 whitespace normalizations across the SQL string literals
and one blank line.

activities.txt — this entry (chore/activities-log-session-92, PR
pending).

MIGRATIONS RUN
------------------------------------------------------------------------

none — no new schema, no new column, no new index, no new constraint,

------------------------------------------------------------------------
TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
------------------------------------------------------------------------

tenant-isolation-auditor verdict: APPROVED. 0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH, 0
WARN, 8 INFO. Audited 4 changed queries (2 slim §5-scope-check
SELECTs + 2 unchanged-body queries: GET full SELECT and PUT UPDATE,
both gated by the §5 helper before the operational query fires).

§5 dual-path access contract honored: resolveAccessibleTenantIds is
consumed at both call sites, never inlined. Both routes branch
through the helper (legacy single-tenant returns [user.tenant_id];
district user returns user_school_access membership filtered by both
user_id AND district_id). No inline branching at the call sites.

Placement BEFORE the operational query confirmed: scope-miss returns
404 at the §5 check, with the data query structurally unreachable.
For GET, no PII column (email, full_name, role) is read from the
full SELECT for an out-of-scope :id. For PUT, no UPDATE executes for
an out-of-scope :id.

Empty-array contract collapses to same 404 with no special-casing:
Array.prototype.includes on [] returns false for every input. A
district user with no user_school_access grants yet (legitimate
pre-onboarding state per helper docstring) sees the same 404 as any
out-of-scope tenant — no information disclosure differentiates the
two failure modes.

No defense-in-depth AND tenant_id = ANY($N::int[]) added to the
GET full SELECT or PUT UPDATE WHERE clauses. Acceptable for this
read+write shape: no PII column is fetched for out-of-scope reads
(§5 SELECT runs first and returns only tenant_id); no UPDATE
executes for out-of-scope writes; users.tenant_id is never mutated
post-creation in the codebase (so the TOCTOU race window between §5
SELECT and operational query is unweaponizable). Auditor
disposition: INFO not WARN. Same rationale as PR #142.

Cross-tenant read/write paths through these handlers: closed.
Pre-PR, both routes accepted req.params.id and ran SELECT or UPDATE
without any tenant predicate — textbook IDOR + role-escalation
primitive. Post-PR the four-gate stack on GET (requireAuth +
caller-role + positive-int + §5) and seven-gate stack on PUT
(adds self-PUT + role-validity + role-rank) plus the probe-resistant
404 collapse leaves no cross-tenant exposure surface on either
handler.

Probe-resistance: missing-row (slim SELECT empty), out-of-scope
(slim SELECT row's tenant_id not in accessible), and theoretical
TOCTOU-deleted (full SELECT/UPDATE returns 0 rows after §5 passed)
all collapse to 404 responses. The trailing 404 uses 'User not
found' rather than 'Not found' — banked as
chore/users-target-id-404-body-harmonize per security-reviewer
INFO-1 finding; structurally unreachable in single-request flow so
not load-bearing as a finding.

0 new tables; 0 schema changes; 0 new foreign keys; 0 composite-FK
audit requirements. No JOIN traversal in the changed queries.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)

privacy-reviewer subagent verdict: APPROVED WITH NOTES. 0 CRITICAL,

question banking, not a §4B fix-in-this-PR ask — the role-rank gate
already prevents school_admin → district_admin escalation, and the

chore/users-put-parent-role-mutation-policy, sibling to Followup
#122.

PII fields touched in the work-PR diff: none new. GET's existing
SELECT column list (id, tenant_id, email, full_name, role,
created_at, updated_at) is unchanged from pre-diff and is required
for the admin-management surface the now-gated caller set
(district_admin/school_admin within accessible-tenant scope) needs
to render. PUT's existing UPDATE column list (email, full_name,
role) is unchanged.

Logging audit: both catch blocks now emit only error.code (a pg
SQLSTATE like '23505'), not error.message and not the full error
object. The prior shape (zero log line + body-leak only) added a
new structured log line — net-positive observability AND net-zero
PII exposure, since error.code is a fixed-cardinality protocol
value. Both new log lines (lines 186 and 313 in post-PR file)
confirmed PII-safe.

Error-response audit: both catches now return generic 'Server
error' body; no error.message / error.detail / error.stack in any
response path. The four non-200 paths return generic envelopes only
('Forbidden', 'Invalid user id', 'Not found', 'Server error', and
the pre-existing 'User not found' on the trailing 404). Pre-fix
body-leak ({ error: error.message }) PII surface — which would
have echoed row values from pg error text on UNIQUE/FK violations —
is fully closed.

Cross-tenant risk: closed per the §5 check.

pass per docs/ai-context/4B_GREP_PATTERNS.md ran against added-
lines-only (^+ excluding ^+++). Patterns 1 (emails) and 2
(school-type tokens) clean against added-lines-only on this entry's
diff. Pattern 3 (tenant slugs) remains gap-acknowledged per S90
precedent — .4b-tenant-slugs.local not provisioned this session.

diffs pre-commit (per feedback_4b_per_entry_standard.md per-entry
standard); narrow pass clean across all three commits AND against
the aggregate git diff main...HEAD diff. Reviewer-trio's
privacy-reviewer independently confirmed the per-commit posture
(INFO findings I-1 through I-3).

Per the S89 recursion-trap lesson, this entry does not enumerate
which categories the grep checked for; it cites only acts and
results.

WARN-1 disposition: tenant-isolation-auditor surfaced one WARN on
R3 LEFT JOIN against parent_student_links. The JOIN aggregates a
foreign-key column from a table without a tenant-scoping column,
scoped only on the parent's tenant. If a row exists where the
parent's tenant ≠ the linked student's tenant, R3 returns the
foreign foreign-key in the aggregate. Pre-existing latent behavior,
unchanged from main (verified via git show main:routes/users.js).
PR A *narrows* the surface from unauthenticated to authenticated
INTERVENTION_MANAGER_ROLES callers within the requested tenant —
strictly better than baseline. Tracked under Followup #122
(cross-school parent-student link product decision); must resolve
before multi-school user_school_access onboards.

SESSION 95 — 2026-05-27
========================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Frontend-only chore PR (#152) eliminating the documented login-
time 403 noise floor that PR #150's role-gated /users/parents
endpoint introduced for the family-facing user role. Two
unconditional fetchParentsList call sites in App.jsx (the Google
login and password login flows) now skip the call when the just-
authenticated user belongs to the family-facing role. Net diff:
+6/−2, single file (frontend/src/App.jsx).

Session opened against a freshly-pulled main that already carried
both S94 artifacts: PR #150 (sec/users-tenant-reads-require-auth,
merged 6a0c23f) and PR #151 (chore/activities-log-session-94,
merged 5dead2b). No outstanding S94 carryovers; this session is
the immediate sibling FE cleanup queued by S94's close note.

Two design decisions documented in PR #152 body:

  (1) Guard PLACEMENT: call-site, not function-entry. A function-
      entry guard would read `user` from closure (potentially
      stale relative to the just-executed setUser(data.user)),
      while call-site guards read the role attribute directly off
      the auth-response object. Function contract preserved — any
      future staff-facing surface that calls fetchParentsList
      still gets the unguarded fetch.

  (2) Guard FORM: negative-list against the family-facing role,
      not positive-list against the staff allowlist. If a future
      BE-allowed role is added to INTERVENTION_MANAGER_ROLES
      (e.g., a new specialist role), positive-list would silently
      fail to fetch; negative-list lets new staff roles fetch
      automatically and only surfaces a benign console error if a
      newly-excluded role is added. Aligns with the dominant
      App.jsx pattern (10+ pre-existing negative-and-positive
      checks against the family-facing role at module scope; zero
      positive-list role allowlists at module scope).

AppContext.jsx:280 — the duplicate fetchParentsList that S93
banking flagged as "likely dead" — left untouched per scope
discipline. Documented in PR #152 body as out-of-scope.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FILES TOUCHED
------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Lines 1126 (handleGoogleSignIn) and 1263 (handleLogin): wrap
  fetchParentsList(data.user.tenant_id) in a single-condition
  guard that skips the call when the auth-response role attribute
  matches the family-facing role enum value. Indentation matches
  each enclosing block's pre-existing style (2-space for the
  Google flow, 4-space for the password flow).
